    10	
    11	import type { Placement } from '@floating-ui/vue'
    12	import type { ComputedRef, Ref } from 'vue'
    13	import {
    14	  flip as flipMiddleware,
    15	  offset as offsetMiddleware,
    16	  shift as shiftMiddleware,
    17	  useFloating as useFloatingUI,
    18	} from '@floating-ui/vue'
    19	import { computed, ref } from 'vue'
    20	
    21	/** Placement options for the floating element */
    22	export type FloatingPlacement
    23	  = | 'top'
    24	    | 'bottom'
    25	    | 'left'
    26	    | 'right'
    27	    | 'top-start'
    28	    | 'top-end'
    29	    | 'bottom-start'
    30	    | 'bottom-end'
    31	    | 'left-start'
    32	    | 'left-end'
    33	    | 'right-start'
    34	    | 'right-end'
    35	
    36	/** Options for the useFloating composable */
    37	export interface UseFloatingOptions {
    38	  /** Where to place the floating element relative to the reference */
    39	  placement?: FloatingPlacement
    40	  /** Offset distance in pixels from the reference element */
    41	  offset?: number
    42	  /** Whether to flip placement when insufficient space */
    43	  flip?: boolean
    44	  /** Whether to shift along the axis to stay in view */
    45	  shift?: boolean
    46	}
    47	
    48	/** Return value of the useFloating composable */
    49	export interface UseFloatingReturn {
    50	  /** Ref to bind to the reference (trigger) element */
    51	  referenceRef: Ref<HTMLElement | null>
    52	  /** Ref to bind to the floating element */
    53	  floatingRef: Ref<HTMLElement | null>
    54	  /** Computed CSS styles to apply to the floating element */
    55	  floatingStyles: ComputedRef<Record<string, string>>
    56	  /** The resolved placement after middleware adjustments */
    57	  placement: Ref<string>
    58	  /** Manually trigger a position recalculation */
    59	  update: () => void
    60	}
    61	
    62	/**
    63	 * Wraps `@floating-ui/vue` with sensible defaults for dzup-ui overlay components.
    64	 *
    65	 * @param options - Positioning configuration
    66	 * @returns Refs, styles, and controls for the floating element
    67	 */
    68	export function useFloating(options?: UseFloatingOptions): UseFloatingReturn {
    69	  const {
    70	    placement: initialPlacement = 'bottom',
    71	    offset: offsetValue = 8,
    72	    flip: enableFlip = true,
    73	    shift: enableShift = true,
    74	  } = options ?? {}
    75	
    76	  const referenceRef = ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)
    77	  const floatingRef = ref<HTMLElement | null>(null)
    78	
    79	  // Build middleware array based on options
    80	  const middleware = [
    81	    offsetMiddleware(offsetValue),
    82	    ...(enableFlip ? [flipMiddleware()] : []),
    83	    ...(enableShift ? [shiftMiddleware()] : []),
    84	  ]
    85	
    86	  const { floatingStyles: rawStyles, placement: resolvedPlacement, update } = useFloatingUI(
    87	    referenceRef,
    88	    floatingRef,
    89	    {
    90	      placement: initialPlacement as Placement,
    91	      middleware,
    92	    },
    93	  )
    94	
    95	  // Convert CSSProperties to Record<string, string> for consistent typing
    96	  const floatingStyles = computed<Record<string, string>>(() => {
    97	    const styles = rawStyles.value
    98	    const result: Record<string, string> = {}
    99	    if (typeof styles === 'object' && styles !== null) {
   100	      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(styles)) {
   101	        if (typeof value === 'string') {
   102	          result[key] = value
   103	        }
   104	        else if (typeof value === 'number') {
   105	          result[key] = `${value}px`
   106	        }
   107	      }
   108	    }
   109	    return result
   110	  })
   111	
   112	  // Expose placement as a string ref
   113	  const placementRef = computed(() => resolvedPlacement.value as string)
   114	
   115	  return {
   116	    referenceRef,
   117	    floatingRef,
   118	    floatingStyles,
   119	    placement: placementRef as unknown as Ref<string>,
   120	    update,
   121	  }
   122	}

     1	<script setup lang="ts">
     2	import type { DzDialogProps, DzDialogSlots } from './DzDialog.types.ts'

     4	/**
     5	 * DzDialog -- Root compound component wrapping Reka UI DialogRoot (ADR-07).
     6	 *
     7	 * Manages open state via defineModel<boolean>('open') (ADR-16).
     8	 * Children (DzDialogTrigger, DzDialogContent, etc.) are rendered via default slot.
     9	 *
    10	 * @example
    11	 * ``\`vue
    12	 * <DzDialog v-model:open="isOpen">
    13	 *   <DzDialogTrigger as-child>
    14	 *     <DzButton>Open</DzButton>
    15	 *   </DzDialogTrigger>
    16	 *   <DzDialogContent>
    17	 *     <DzDialogTitle>Title</DzDialogTitle>
    18	 *     <DzDialogDescription>Description</DzDialogDescription>
    19	 *     <DzDialogClose />
    20	 *   </DzDialogContent>
    21	 * </DzDialog>
    22	 * ``\`
    23	 */
    24	import { provide, toRef } from 'vue'
    25	import { DZ_DIALOG_KEY } from './DzDialog.types.ts'
    26	
    27	const open = defineModel<boolean>('open', { default: false })
    28	
    29	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzDialogProps>(), {
    30	  modal: true,
    31	  animated: true,
    32	  overlayTransition: 'dz-dialog-overlay',
    33	  contentTransition: 'dz-dialog-content',
    34	})
    35	
    36	defineSlots<DzDialogSlots>()
    37	
    38	provide(DZ_DIALOG_KEY, {
    39	  size: toRef(() => 'md' as const),
    40	  animated: toRef(() => props.animated),
    41	  overlayTransition: toRef(() => props.animated ? props.overlayTransition : ''),
    42	  contentTransition: toRef(() => props.animated ? props.contentTransition : ''),
    43	})
    44	</script>
    45	
    46	<script lang="ts">
    47	export default {
    48	  inheritAttrs: false,
    49	}
    50	</script>
    51	
    52	<template>
    53	  <DialogRoot v-model:open="open" :modal="modal">
    54	    <slot />
    55	  </DialogRoot>
    56	</template>

    10	} from './DzDataGrid.types.ts'
    11	/**
    12	 * DzDataGrid — Compound data grid root component.
    13	 *
    14	 * The most complex core component. Phase 1: sorting, pagination, row selection.
    15	 * Uses useDataGrid composable for state management.
    16	 * Composes DzDataGridHeader, DzDataGridBody, DzDataGridPagination sub-parts.

    20	 * @example

    68	    emit('sort', model)

    70	  onFilterChange: (filterState: DzDataGridFilter[]) => {

    72	    emit('filter', filterState)
    73	  },

    85	const context: DzDataGridContext = {

   191	    </DzDataGridPagination>

   167	] as const
   168	
   169	/**
   170	 * Component names exported from @dzup-ui/pro.
   171	 */
   172	const PRO_COMPONENTS = [
   173	  // builders
   174	  'DzFormBuilder',
   175	  'DzFormBuilderField',

   192	  // planning

   194	  'DzCalendarDayView',
   195	  'DzCalendarWeekView',
   196	  'DzCalendarMonthView',
   197	  'DzScheduler',
   198	  'DzKanban',
   199	  'DzKanbanColumn',
   200	  'DzKanbanCard',
   201	  'DzGantt',
   202	  'DzGanttRow',
   203	  'DzGanttBar',
   204	  // workflow
   205	  'DzWorkflowDesigner',
   206	  'DzWorkflowNode',
   207	  'DzWorkflowEdge',
   208	  // communication
   209	  'DzChat',
   210	  'DzChatMessage',
   211	  'DzComment',
   212	  'DzCommentItem',
   213	  'DzReactionPicker',
   214	  // business
   215	  'DzAppShell',
   216	  'DzNotificationCenter',
   217	  'DzAuditLog',
   218	  // visualization
   219	  'DzChart',
   220	  'DzHeatMap',
   221	  'DzTreeMap',
   222	  'DzDiagramEditor',
   223	] as const
   224	
   225	export default defineNuxtModule<DzupUiModuleOptions>({
   226	  meta: {
   227	    name: '@dzup-ui/nuxt',
   228	    configKey: 'dzupUi',
   229	    compatibility: {
   230	      nuxt: '>=3.0.0',
   231	    },
   232	  },
   233	  defaults: {
   234	    includePro: false,
   235	    prefix: '',
   236	  },
   237	  setup(options, nuxt) {
   238	    const _resolver = createResolver(import.meta.url)
   239	
   240	    // Add @dzup-ui/tokens CSS
   241	    nuxt.options.css.push('@dzup-ui/tokens/dist/tokens.css')
   242	
   243	    // Add @dzup-ui/core styles
   244	    nuxt.options.css.push('@dzup-ui/core/styles')
   245	
   246	    // Transpile packages
   247	    nuxt.options.build.transpile.push('@dzup-ui/core', '@dzup-ui/tokens')
   248	    if (options.includePro) {
   249	      nuxt.options.build.transpile.push('@dzup-ui/pro')
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    // Register core components
   253	    for (const name of CORE_COMPONENTS) {
   254	      addComponent({
   255	        name: options.prefix ? `${options.prefix}${name.slice(2)}` : name,
   256	        export: name,
   257	        filePath: '@dzup-ui/core',
   258	      })
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    // Register pro components if enabled
   262	    if (options.includePro) {
   263	      for (const name of PRO_COMPONENTS) {
   264	        addComponent({
   265	          name: options.prefix ? `${options.prefix}${name.slice(2)}` : name,
   266	          export: name,
   267	          filePath: '@dzup-ui/pro',
   268	        })
   269	      }
   270	    }
   271	
   272	    // Add theme script to head for FOUC prevention (ADR-15)
   273	    nuxt.options.app.head.script = nuxt.options.app.head.script || []
   274	    nuxt.options.app.head.script.push({
   275	      innerHTML: `(function(){try{var t=localStorage.getItem('dz-theme');if(t)document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme',t);else if(window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').matches)document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme','dark')}catch(e){}})()`,
   276	      type: 'text/javascript',
   277	    })
   278	  },
   279	})

for(const fam of Object.values(man.exports.components)) manifest.push(...fam.exports);
for(const comp of Object.values(man.exports.composables||{})) manifest.push(...comp.exports);
for(const p of Object.values(man.exports.providers||{})) manifest.push(...p.exports);
const mset=new Set(manifest);
const nset=new Set(nuxt);
const missingInNuxt=[...mset].filter(x=>"'!nset.has(x)).sort();
const extraInNuxt=[...nset].filter(x=>!mset.has(x)).sort();

console.log('manifest count',manifest.length);
console.log('missingInNuxt',missingInNuxt.length,missingInNuxt.slice(0,40));
console.log('extraInNuxt',extraInNuxt.length,extraInNuxt.slice(0,80));

const fs=require('fs');
const txt=fs.readFileSync('/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts','utf8');
const coreBlock=txt.split('const CORE_COMPONENTS = [')[1].split('] as const')[0];

const componentNames=[];
for(const fam of Object.values(man.exports.components)) componentNames.push(...fam.exports.filter(n=>/"'^Dz[A-Z]/.test(n)));
for(const p of Object.values(man.exports.providers||{})) componentNames.push(...p.exports.filter(n=>/''^Dz[A-Z]/.test(n)));
const mset=new Set(componentNames);
const nset=new Set(nuxt);
const missing=[...mset].filter(x=>!nset.has(x)).sort();
const extra=[...nset].filter(x=>!mset.has(x)).sort();
console.log('"'manifest components',componentNames.length);
console.log('nuxt components',nuxt.length);
console.log('missing',missing.length,missing);
console.log('extra',extra.length,extra);

     1	/**
     2	 * @dzup-ui/compat
     3	 * Migration adapters from old dzup-ui API to vNext.
     4	 *
     5	 * IMPORTANT: Stable core/pro must NOT import from compat (ADR-06).
     6	 * This package provides backward-compatible wrappers for migration.
     7	 *
     8	 * @example
     9	 * ``\`ts
    10	 * // Before (old dzup-ui)
    11	 * import { DzButton } from 'dzup-ui'
    12	 * // <DzButton type="primary" size="small">Click</DzButton>
    13	 *
    14	 * // Migration step (use compat adapters)
    15	 * import { DzButtonCompat as DzButton } from '@dzup-ui/compat'
    16	 * // <DzButton type="primary" size="small">Click</DzButton>  -- still works
    17	 *
    18	 * // Final (migrate to vNext API)
    19	 * import { DzButton } from '@dzup-ui/core'
    20	 * // <DzButton variant="solid" tone="primary" size="sm">Click</DzButton>
    21	 * ``\`
    22	 */
    23	
    24	// ── Compat Adapters ──
    25	export { default as DzAccordionCompat } from './adapters/DzAccordionCompat.vue'
    26	export { default as DzAlertCompat } from './adapters/DzAlertCompat.vue'
    27	export { default as DzButtonCompat } from './adapters/DzButtonCompat.vue'

    36	
    37	// ── Utilities ──
    38	export { resetDeprecationWarnings, warnDeprecated } from './utils/deprecation.ts'

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Shared deprecation warning utility for compat adapters.
     3	 *
     4	 * Warnings are only emitted in dev mode (`import.meta.env?.DEV`)
     5	 * and each component name is warned about at most once per session.
     6	 *
     7	 * @module @dzup-ui/compat/utils/deprecation
     8	 */
     9	
    10	/** Set tracking which component names have already warned */
    11	const warned = new Set<string>()
    12	
    13	/**
    14	 * Emit a one-time deprecation warning for a compat adapter.
    15	 *
    16	 * @param oldName - The deprecated component name (e.g. `DzButtonCompat`)
    17	 * @param newName - The replacement component name (e.g. `DzButton`)
    18	 * @param packageName - The package where the replacement lives (default: `@dzup-ui/core`)
    19	 */
    20	export function warnDeprecated(
    21	  oldName: string,
    22	  newName: string,
    23	  packageName: string = '@dzup-ui/core',
    24	): void {
    25	  if (import.meta.env?.DEV && !warned.has(oldName)) {
    26	    warned.add(oldName)
    27	    console.warn(
    28	      `[dzup-ui/compat] ${oldName} is deprecated. Use ${newName} from ${packageName} instead.`,
    29	    )
    30	  }
    31	}
    32	
    33	/**
    34	 * Reset the warning set. Useful for testing.
    35	 */
    36	export function resetDeprecationWarnings(): void {
    37	  warned.clear()
    38	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	<script setup lang="ts">
     2	import type { ButtonVariant, CanonicalSize, CanonicalTone } from '@dzup-ui/contracts'
     3	import type { DzButtonCompatProps, OldButtonType, OldSize } from '../adapter-types.ts'
     4	import { DzButton } from '@dzup-ui/core'
     5	/**
     6	 * DzButtonCompat — backward-compatible wrapper for DzButton.
     7	 *
     8	 * Maps old dzup-ui button API to the new vNext API:
     9	 * - `type` prop (old: "primary" | "success" | "warning" | "danger" | "info" | "default")
    10	 *   maps to `tone` + `variant="solid"` in the new API.
    11	 * - `size` values: "small" -> "sm", "medium" -> "md", "large" -> "lg"
    12	 *
    13	 * @deprecated Use DzButton from @dzup-ui/core instead.
    14	 */
    15	import { computed, onMounted, useAttrs } from 'vue'
    16	import { warnDeprecated } from '../utils/deprecation.ts'
    17	
    18	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzButtonCompatProps>(), {
    19	  type: 'default',
    20	  size: 'medium',
    21	  disabled: false,
    22	  loading: false,
    23	})
    24	
    25	const emit = defineEmits<{
    26	  click: [event: MouseEvent]
    27	  focus: [event: FocusEvent]
    28	  blur: [event: FocusEvent]
    29	}>()
    30	
    31	const attrs = useAttrs()
    32	
    33	onMounted(() => {
    34	  warnDeprecated('DzButtonCompat', 'DzButton')
    35	})
    36	
    37	/** Map old type to new tone */
    38	const mappedTone = computed<CanonicalTone>(() => {
    39	  const typeToTone: Record<OldButtonType, CanonicalTone> = {
    40	    primary: 'primary',
    41	    success: 'success',
    42	    warning: 'warning',
    43	    danger: 'danger',
    44	    info: 'info',
    45	    default: 'neutral',
    46	    text: 'neutral',
    47	    link: 'neutral',
    48	  }
    49	  return typeToTone[props.type ?? 'default']
    50	})
    51	
    52	/** Map old type to new variant */
    53	const mappedVariant = computed<ButtonVariant>(() => {
    54	  if (props.type === 'text')
    55	    return 'text'
    56	  if (props.type === 'link')
    57	    return 'link'
    58	  return 'solid'
    59	})
    60	
    61	/** Map old size values to canonical sizes */
    62	const mappedSize = computed<CanonicalSize>(() => {
    63	  const sizeMap: Record<OldSize, CanonicalSize> = {
    64	    small: 'sm',
    65	    medium: 'md',
    66	    large: 'lg',
    67	    xs: 'xs',
    68	    sm: 'sm',
    69	    md: 'md',
    70	    lg: 'lg',
    71	    xl: 'xl',
    72	  }
    73	  return sizeMap[props.size ?? 'medium']
    74	})
    75	</script>
    76	
    77	<script lang="ts">
    78	export default { inheritAttrs: false }
    79	</script>
    80	
    81	<template>
    82	  <DzButton
    83	    :variant="mappedVariant"
    84	    :tone="mappedTone"
    85	    :size="mappedSize"
    86	    :disabled="disabled"
    87	    :loading="loading"
    88	    v-bind="attrs"
    89	    @click="emit('click', $event)"
    90	    @focus="emit('focus', $event)"
    91	    @blur="emit('blur', $event)"
    92	  >
    93	    <slot />
    94	    <template v-if="$slots.prefix" #prefix>
    95	      <slot name="prefix" />
    96	    </template>
    97	    <template v-if="$slots.suffix" #suffix>
    98	      <slot name="suffix" />
    99	    </template>
   100	  </DzButton>
   101	</template>

    10	import type { TransformStats } from './utils/logger.js'
    11	import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    12	import process from 'node:process'
    13	import { globSync } from 'glob'
    14	import { CodemodLogger } from './utils/logger.js'
    15	import { extractScriptFromVue, isVueFile, replaceScriptInVue } from './utils/vue-sfc.js'
    16	
    17	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18	// Types
    19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20	
    21	/** A jscodeshift-compatible transform function. */
    22	export type TransformFn = (
    23	  file: { path: string, source: string },
    24	  api: { jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI, stats: () => void },
    25	  options: Record<string, unknown>,
    26	) => string | null
    27	
    28	/**
    29	 * Minimal jscodeshift API surface used by the runner.
    30	 * We import jscodeshift lazily so the module can be loaded without it
    31	 * installed (e.g. for type-checking only).
    32	 */
    33	interface JscodeshiftAPI {
    34	  (source: string): unknown
    35	  withParser: (parser: string) => JscodeshiftAPI
    36	  [key: string]: unknown
    37	}
    38	
    39	/** Options accepted by {@link CodemodRunner}. */
    40	export interface RunnerOptions {
    41	  /** When true, do not write files — only report what would change. */
    42	  dryRun?: boolean
    43	  /** File extensions to process (without leading dot). */
    44	  extensions?: string[]
    45	  /** Glob patterns to ignore. */
    46	  ignore?: string[]
    47	  /** jscodeshift parser to use. */
    48	  parser?: 'babel' | 'ts' | 'tsx'
    49	  /** Print per-file status messages. */
    50	  verbose?: boolean
    51	}
    52	
    53	interface ResolvedOptions {
    54	  dryRun: boolean
    55	  extensions: string[]
    56	  ignore: string[]
    57	  parser: 'babel' | 'ts' | 'tsx'
    58	  verbose: boolean
    59	}
    60	
    61	/** Result returned by {@link CodemodRunner.run}. */
    62	export interface RunResult {
    63	  success: boolean
    64	  stats: TransformStats
    65	}
    66	
    67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68	// Runner
    69	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70	
    71	export class CodemodRunner {
    72	  private readonly logger = new CodemodLogger()
    73	  private readonly options: ResolvedOptions
    74	
    75	  constructor(options: RunnerOptions = {}) {
    76	    this.options = {
    77	      dryRun: options.dryRun ?? false,
    78	      extensions: options.extensions ?? ['js', 'jsx', 'ts', 'tsx', 'vue'],
    79	      ignore: options.ignore ?? ['**/node_modules/**', '**/dist/**', '**/.git/**'],
    80	      parser: options.parser ?? 'tsx',
    81	      verbose: options.verbose ?? false,
    82	    }
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  /**
    86	   * Run a transform against all matching files under `targetPath`.
    87	   *
    88	   * @param transform - The transform function (default export of a codemod file).
    89	   * @param targetPath - Directory to scan for files.
    90	   * @returns A {@link RunResult} with success status and statistics.
    91	   */
    92	  async run(transform: TransformFn, targetPath: string): Promise<RunResult> {
    93	    const files = this.findFiles(targetPath)
    94	
    95	    if (this.options.verbose) {
    96	      process.stdout.write(`Found ${String(files.length)} file(s) in ${targetPath}\n`)
    97	    }
    98	
    99	    if (files.length === 0) {
   100	      process.stdout.write('No files to transform.\n')
   101	      return { success: true, stats: this.logger.getStats() }
   102	    }
   103	
   104	    // Lazy-import jscodeshift so the module can load without it.
   105	    const jscodeshift = await this.loadJscodeshift()
   106	
   107	    for (const file of files) {
   108	      this.processFile(file, transform, jscodeshift)
   109	    }
   110	
   111	    this.logger.printSummary()
   112	
   113	    return {
   114	      success: this.logger.getStats().filesErrored === 0,
   115	      stats: this.logger.getStats(),
   116	    }
   117	  }
   118	
   119	  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   120	  // Private helpers
   121	  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   122	
   123	  private findFiles(targetPath: string): string[] {
   124	    const exts = this.options.extensions.join(',')
   125	    const pattern = `${targetPath}/**/*.{${exts}}`
   126	    return globSync(pattern, {
   127	      ignore: this.options.ignore,
   128	      absolute: true,
   129	    })
   130	  }
   131	
   132	  private processFile(
   133	    filePath: string,
   134	    transform: TransformFn,
   135	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   136	  ): void {
   137	    this.logger.incrementProcessed()
   138	
   139	    try {
   140	      const source = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   141	      let result: string | null = null
   142	
   143	      if (isVueFile(filePath)) {
   144	        result = this.transformVueFile(filePath, source, transform, jscodeshift)
   145	      }
   146	      else {
   147	        result = this.transformJsFile(filePath, source, transform, jscodeshift)
   148	      }
   149	
   150	      if (result !== null && result !== source) {
   151	        if (!this.options.dryRun) {
   152	          writeFileSync(filePath, result, 'utf-8')
   153	        }
   154	        this.logger.incrementModified()
   155	        if (this.options.verbose) {
   156	          process.stdout.write(`  modified: ${filePath}\n`)
   157	        }
   158	      }
   159	      else {
   160	        this.logger.incrementSkipped()
   161	        if (this.options.verbose) {
   162	          process.stdout.write(`  skipped:  ${filePath}\n`)
   163	        }
   164	      }
   165	    }
   166	    catch (error: unknown) {
   167	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
   168	      this.logger.logError(filePath, message)
   169	      if (this.options.verbose) {
   170	        process.stderr.write(`  error:    ${filePath}: ${message}\n`)
   171	      }
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  private transformJsFile(
   176	    filePath: string,
   177	    source: string,
   178	    transform: TransformFn,
   179	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   180	  ): string | null {
   181	    const fileInfo = { path: filePath, source }
   182	    const api = {
   183	      jscodeshift: jscodeshift.withParser(this.options.parser),
   184	      stats: () => {},
   185	    }
   186	    return transform(fileInfo, api, { dryRun: this.options.dryRun })
   187	  }
   188	
   189	  private transformVueFile(
   190	    filePath: string,
   191	    source: string,
   192	    transform: TransformFn,
   193	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   194	  ): string | null {
   195	    // Many transforms handle Vue files themselves (template + script).
   196	    // Try the transform on the whole file first — if the transform is
   197	    // Vue-aware it will handle extraction internally.
   198	    const wholeFileResult = transform(
   199	      { path: filePath, source },
   200	      { jscodeshift: jscodeshift.withParser(this.options.parser), stats: () => {} },
   201	      { dryRun: this.options.dryRun },
   202	    )
   203	
   204	    if (wholeFileResult !== null) {
   205	      return wholeFileResult
   206	    }
   207	
   208	    // Fallback: extract script and transform it in isolation.
   209	    const extraction = extractScriptFromVue(source)
   210	    if (!extraction)
   211	      return null
   212	
   213	    const scriptResult = this.transformJsFile(
   214	      filePath,
   215	      extraction.script,
   216	      transform,
   217	      jscodeshift,
   218	    )
   219	
   220	    if (scriptResult !== null && scriptResult !== extraction.script) {
   221	      return replaceScriptInVue(source, scriptResult, extraction)
   222	    }
   223	
   224	    return null
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  private async loadJscodeshift(): Promise<JscodeshiftAPI> {
   228	    try {
   229	      const mod = await import('jscodeshift')
   230	      return (mod.default ?? mod) as JscodeshiftAPI
   231	    }
   232	    catch {
   233	      throw new Error(
   234	        'jscodeshift is required but not installed. Run: yarn add jscodeshift',
   235	      )
   236	    }
   237	  }
   238	}

    15	test.describe('Storybook root', () => {
    16	  test('root page loads without JavaScript errors', async ({ page }) => {

   208	      )
   209	
   210	      expect(actionableErrors).toHaveLength(0)
   211	    })
   212	  }

     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * Keyboard navigation and accessibility smoke tests.
     5	 *
     6	 * These tests verify that core interactive components are reachable

    13	test.describe('DzButton — keyboard accessibility', () => {

    15	
    16	  test('button is focusable via Tab key from the page', async ({ page }) => {

    20	
    21	    // Click into the iframe body so Tab works within its document context

    23	    await page.keyboard.press('Tab')

    25	    const button = frame.getByRole('button', { name: 'Button' })

    88	
    89	test.describe('DzInput — keyboard accessibility', () => {
    90	  const STORY_ROOT = 'core-inputs-dzinput'

   105	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${STORY_ROOT}--interactive`)
   106	
   107	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   108	
   109	    const input = frame.getByRole('textbox')
   110	    await input.focus()
   111	    await expect(input).toBeFocused()
   112	
   113	    // Type character by character using keyboard events
   114	    await page.keyboard.type('keyboard test')
   115	
   116	    await expect(input).toHaveValue('keyboard test')
   117	  })
   118	
   119	  test('Tab moves focus out of the input to the next focusable element', async ({ page }) => {
   120	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${STORY_ROOT}--interactive`)
   121	
   122	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   123	
   124	    const input = frame.getByRole('textbox')
   125	    await input.focus()
   126	    await expect(input).toBeFocused()
   127	
   128	    await page.keyboard.press('Tab')
   129	
   130	    // After Tab, the input is no longer focused
   131	    await expect(input).not.toBeFocused()
   132	  })
   133	})
   134	
   135	test.describe('DzDialog — focus trap when open', () => {
   136	  const STORY_ROOT = 'core-overlays-dzdialog'
   137	
   138	  test('focus moves into the dialog when it opens', async ({ page }) => {
   139	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${STORY_ROOT}--default`)
   140	
   141	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   142	
   143	    const triggerButton = frame.getByRole('button', { name: /open dialog/i })
   144	    await triggerButton.click()
   145	
   146	    // Reka UI Dialog automatically moves focus to the first focusable element
   147	    // inside the dialog content — typically the close / cancel button
   148	    const dialogContent = frame.getByRole('dialog')
   149	    await expect(dialogContent).toBeVisible()
   150	
   151	    // Some element within the dialog must receive focus
   152	    const focusedEl = frame.locator(':focus')
   153	    await expect(focusedEl).toBeVisible()
   154	  })
   155	
   156	  test('Tab key cycles focus within the open dialog (focus trap)', async ({ page }) => {
   157	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${STORY_ROOT}--accessibility`)
   158	
   159	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   160	
   161	    await frame.getByRole('button', { name: /open accessible dialog/i }).click()
   162	
   163	    // Dialog is open
   164	    const dialog = frame.getByRole('dialog')
   165	    await expect(dialog).toBeVisible()
   166	
   167	    // Collect the focusable buttons inside the dialog (Cancel, OK)
   168	    const cancelButton = frame.getByRole('button', { name: /cancel/i })
   169	    const okButton = frame.getByRole('button', { name: /ok/i })
   170	
   171	    // Focus should be within the dialog — press Tab to move through its elements
   172	    await page.keyboard.press('Tab')
   173	
   174	    // After cycling through dialog buttons, focus stays inside (not on the trigger)
   175	    const triggerButton = frame.getByRole('button', { name: /open accessible dialog/i })
   176	    await expect(triggerButton).not.toBeFocused()
   177	
   178	    // The currently focused element must be one of the dialog's interactive elements
   179	    const focusedEl = frame.locator(':focus')
   180	    const focusedText = await focusedEl.textContent()
   181	
   182	    const dialogButtonTexts = ['Cancel', 'OK']
   183	    const isFocusedOnDialogButton = dialogButtonTexts.some(t => focusedText?.includes(t))
   184	    expect(isFocusedOnDialogButton).toBe(true)
   185	
   186	    // Verify cancel and ok buttons are accessible from within the dialog
   187	    await expect(cancelButton).toBeVisible()
   188	    await expect(okButton).toBeVisible()
   189	  })
   190	
   191	  test('Escape closes the dialog and returns focus to trigger', async ({ page }) => {
   192	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${STORY_ROOT}--default`)
   193	
   194	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   195	
   196	    const triggerButton = frame.getByRole('button', { name: /open dialog/i })
   197	    await triggerButton.click()
   198	
   199	    const dialog = frame.getByRole('dialog')
   200	    await expect(dialog).toBeVisible()
   201	
   202	    await page.keyboard.press('Escape')
   203	
   204	    await expect(dialog).not.toBeVisible()
   205	
   206	    // Reka UI returns focus to the trigger after close
   207	    await expect(triggerButton).toBeFocused()
   208	  })
   209	})

     9	  addons: [],
    10	  stories: [
    11	    // Standalone stories directories
    12	    '../../../packages/core/stories/**/*.stories.ts',

    21	    // Tailwind CSS 4 — required for utility classes in component variants
    22	    config.plugins = config.plugins || []

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/stories/Introduction.mdx | sed -n '1,260p'" in /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	{/* Introduction.mdx */}
     2	import { Meta } from '@storybook/blocks'
     3	
     4	<Meta title="Introduction" />
     5	
     6	# dzup-ui Component Library
     7	
     8	Welcome to the **dzup-ui** component library documentation — 147 foundational Vue 3 components built with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, and design tokens.
     9	
    10	> **Enterprise components** (Kanban, Gantt, FormBuilder, WorkflowDesigner, etc.) are available in [@dzup-ui/pro](https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui-pro) under a commercial license.
    11	
    12	## Architecture
    13	
    14	- **Vue 3.5+** with Composition API and `<script setup lang="ts">`
    15	- **TypeScript** with strict mode and full type coverage
    16	- **Tailwind CSS 4** with CSS-first design tokens
    17	- **tailwind-variants (tv)** for type-safe variant styling
    18	- **Reka UI** for headless interaction primitives
    19	- **OKLCH color system** for perceptually uniform colors
    20	
    21	## Component Families
    22	
    23	| Family | Components |
    24	|--------|-----------|
    25	| **Buttons** | DzButton, DzIconButton, DzButtonGroup |
    26	| **Cards** | DzCard, DzStatCard |
    27	| **Forms** | DzSelect, DzCheckbox, DzRadio, DzSwitch, DzFormField |
    28	| **Inputs** | DzInput, DzTextarea, DzNumberInput |
    29	| **Layout** | DzContainer, DzGrid, DzFlex, DzStack |
    30	| **Navigation** | DzTabs, DzBreadcrumb, DzPagination, DzMenu |
    31	| **Overlays** | DzDialog, DzSheet, DzTooltip, DzPopover |
    32	| **Feedback** | DzAlert, DzBadge, DzProgress, DzToast |
    33	| **Data** | DzDataGrid, DzTable, DzAccordion |
    34	| **Media** | DzAvatar, DzImage, DzIcon |
    35	| **Typography** | DzHeading, DzText |
    36	
    37	## Design Tokens
    38	
    39	All components use a three-tier token system:
    40	
    41	1. **Primitive** — raw palette and scale values (OKLCH colors)
    42	2. **Semantic** — intent-based mappings that adapt to light/dark themes
    43	3. **Component** — per-component overrides
    44	
    45	Use the **Theme** toolbar toggle above to switch between light and dark modes.
    46	
    47	## Contract Spec
    48	
    49	Every component follows the Contract Spec v1 with canonical prop groups:
    50	
    51	- **Appearance**: `size` (xs/sm/md/lg/xl), `variant`, `tone`
    52	- **Behavior**: `disabled`, `loading`, `readonly`
    53	- **State**: `invalid`, `error`, `required`
    54	- **Accessibility**: `id`, `ariaLabel`, `ariaLabelledby`

exec
/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md | sed -n '1,280p'" in /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# dzup-ui
     2	
     3	**Open-source Vue 3 component library — 147 foundational components.**
     4	
     5	![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@dzup-ui/core?label=%40dzup-ui%2Fcore)
     6	![build status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/datazup/dzup-ui/ci.yml?branch=main)
     7	![license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green)
     8	
     9	> Looking for enterprise components? See [@dzup-ui/pro](https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui-pro).
    10	
    11	## Features
    12	
    13	- 147 Vue 3 components across 11 families
    14	- TypeScript strict mode with zero `any` types and full type inference
    15	- Tailwind CSS 4 design token system with light/dark/system theming
    16	- Reka UI headless primitives for accessible interactive components
    17	- WCAG AA accessibility with keyboard navigation and ARIA support
    18	- SSR-safe, ESM-only distribution with tree-shaking support
    19	
    20	## Quick Start
    21	
    22	**1. Install packages**
    23	
    24	``\`bash
    25	yarn add @dzup-ui/core @dzup-ui/tokens vue@^3.5.0 reka-ui@^2.0.0
    26	``\`
    27	
    28	**2. Import styles**
    29	
    30	``\`css
    31	@import "@dzup-ui/tokens/css";
    32	@import "@dzup-ui/core/styles";
    33	``\`
    34	
    35	**3. Use components**
    36	
    37	``\`vue
    38	<script setup lang="ts">
    39	import { DzButton, DzInput, DzThemeProvider } from '@dzup-ui/core'
    40	import { ref } from 'vue'
    41	
    42	const name = ref('')
    43	</script>
    44	
    45	<template>
    46	  <DzThemeProvider default-theme="system">
    47	    <DzInput v-model="name" placeholder="Enter your name" />
    48	    <DzButton tone="primary" @click="submit">Submit</DzButton>
    49	  </DzThemeProvider>
    50	</template>
    51	``\`
    52	
    53	## Packages
    54	
    55	| Package | Version | Description |
    56	|---------|---------|-------------|
    57	| [`@dzup-ui/core`](./packages/core) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | 147 foundational components (buttons, inputs, forms, layout, navigation, overlays, feedback, data, media, typography) |
    58	| [`@dzup-ui/tokens`](./packages/tokens) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Design tokens — CSS custom properties, Tailwind theme, TypeScript definitions |
    59	| [`@dzup-ui/contracts`](./packages/contracts) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Canonical public API contract types (props, events, slots) |
    60	| [`@dzup-ui/compat`](./packages/compat) | 0.0.1 | Migration adapters from old dzup-ui to vNext |
    61	| [`@dzup-ui/codemods`](./packages/codemods) | 0.0.1 | Automated code transforms for migration (CLI: `dzup-codemod`) |
    62	| [`@dzup-ui/nuxt`](./packages/nuxt) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Nuxt 3 module for auto-importing Dz* components |
    63	
    64	## Component Families
    65	
    66	Buttons · Cards · Inputs · Forms · Layout · Navigation · Overlays · Feedback · Data · Media · Typography
    67	
    68	## Tech Stack
    69	
    70	| Category | Technology |
    71	|----------|------------|
    72	| Framework | Vue 3.5+ with Composition API and `<script setup>` |
    73	| Language | TypeScript 5.6+ (strict mode) |
    74	| Styling | Tailwind CSS 4 with `@theme` directive |
    75	| Variants | tailwind-variants (tv) for type-safe component styling |
    76	| Primitives | Reka UI 2.x for headless interactive components |
    77	| Icons | lucide-vue-next |
    78	| Dates | @internationalized/date |
    79	| Positioning | @floating-ui/vue |
    80	| Testing | Vitest + Playwright |
    81	| Bundler | Vite (library mode, ESM-only) |
    82	| Monorepo | Yarn 4 workspaces |
    83	
    84	## Development
    85	
    86	``\`bash
    87	# Install dependencies
    88	yarn install
    89	
    90	# Type check
    91	yarn typecheck
    92	
    93	# Lint
    94	yarn lint
    95	
    96	# Run tests
    97	yarn test
    98	
    99	# Build all packages
   100	yarn build
   101	
   102	# Start Storybook
   103	yarn storybook
   104	``\`
   105	
   106	## Enterprise Components
   107	
   108	Need Kanban, Gantt, FormBuilder, DashboardBuilder, WorkflowDesigner, and more?
   109	See **[@dzup-ui/pro](https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui-pro)** — 40 enterprise components available under a commercial license.
   110	
   111	## Contributing
   112	
   113	See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for development workflow and guidelines.
   114	
   115	## Migration
   116	
   117	Upgrading from old `dzup-ui`? See [`@dzup-ui/compat`](./packages/compat) and [`@dzup-ui/codemods`](./packages/codemods).
   118	
   119	## License
   120	
   121	MIT — see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details.

     2	  "name": "dzup-ui",

     5	  "packageManager": "yarn@4.6.0",

     8	  },
     9	  "workspaces": [
    10	    "packages/*",
    11	    "apps/*"
    12	  ],
    13	  "scripts": {
    14	    "typecheck": "vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/core/tsconfig.json",
    15	    "typecheck:all": "tsc --noEmit -p packages/tokens/tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p packages/contracts/tsconfig.json && vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/core/tsconfig.json && vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/compat/tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p packages/codemods/tsconfig.json",

     1	# Changelog
     2	
     3	All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
     4	
     5	The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/),
     6	and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
     7	
     8	## [0.1.0-alpha.0] - 2026-03-28
     9	
    10	Initial alpha release of dzup-ui vNext — a greenfield Vue 3 component library
    11	rebuilt from scratch with Contract Spec v1, typed injection keys, ESM-only
    12	distribution, and OKLCH design tokens.
    13	
    14	### Added
    15	
    16	#### Packages
    17	
    18	- **@dzup-ui/core** (v0.1.0-alpha.0) — 146 foundational components across 11 families
    19	- **@dzup-ui/pro** (v0.1.0-alpha.0) — 40 enterprise components across 8 families
    20	- **@dzup-ui/tokens** (v0.1.0-alpha.0) — OKLCH design token system with Tailwind CSS 4
    21	- **@dzup-ui/contracts** — Canonical public API type contracts
    22	- **@dzup-ui/tooling** — Import boundary validator, token compliance checker, manifest generator
    23	- **@dzup-ui/compat** — Migration adapters (old to new)
    24	- **@dzup-ui/codemods** — Code transforms for migration
    25	
    26	#### Core Components (146 across 11 families)
    27	
    28	| Family | Count | Key Components |
    29	|--------|-------|----------------|
    30	| buttons | 7 | DzButton, DzIconButton, DzButtonGroup, DzSplitButton, DzFAB, DzSpeedDial |
    31	| cards | 6 | DzCard, DzCardHeader, DzCardBody, DzCardFooter, DzStatCard, DzImageCard |
    32	| data | 21 | DzDataGrid, DzTable, DzList, DzAccordion, DzTimeline, DzTree |
    33	| feedback | 11 | DzAlert, DzBadge, DzProgress, DzSpinner, DzToast, DzNotification |
    34	| forms | 20 | DzSelect, DzCheckbox, DzRadio, DzSwitch, DzDatePicker, DzMultiSelect, DzFileUpload |
    35	| inputs | 7 | DzInput, DzTextarea, DzNumberField, DzOtpInput, DzSearchBox |
    36	| layout | 15 | DzContainer, DzGrid, DzFlex, DzDivider, DzResizable, DzSplitter |
    37	| media | 10 | DzAvatar, DzImage, DzCarousel, DzLightbox |
    38	| navigation | 14 | DzTabs, DzBreadcrumb, DzPagination, DzMenu, DzStepper, DzSidebar |
    39	| overlays | 30 | DzDialog, DzTooltip, DzPopover, DzDropdown, DzContextMenu, DzSheet |
    40	| typography | 5 | DzHeading, DzText, DzCode, DzBlockquote |
    41	
    42	#### Pro Components (40 across 8 families)
    43	
    44	| Family | Count | Key Components |
    45	|--------|-------|----------------|
    46	| builders | 5 | DzFormBuilder, DzDashboardBuilder |
    47	| business | 3 | DzAuditLog, DzComplianceTracker, DzInvoiceGenerator |
    48	| communication | 5 | DzChat, DzComments |
    49	| data-pro | 5 | DzPivotTable, DzFilterBuilder |
    50	| editors | 4 | DzCodeEditor, DzRichTextEditor |
    51	| planning | 10 | DzGanttChart, DzCalendar, DzScheduler, DzKanban |
    52	| visualization | 4 | Charts, diagrams |
    53	| workflow | 4 | DzWorkflowDesigner |
    54	
    55	#### Composables
    56	
    57	- **Core**: `useCollapse`, `useDataGrid`, `useDatePicker`, `useFormField`, `useToast`
    58	- **Pro**: `useCalendar`, `useDashboardBuilder`, `useDataGridPro`, `useFilterBuilder`, `useFormBuilder`, `useGantt`, `useKanban`, `useScheduler`, `useWorkflowDesigner`
    59	
    60	#### Design Tokens
    61	
    62	- OKLCH color space with semantic aliases (light/dark themes)
    63	- Tailwind CSS 4 integration via `@theme` directive
    64	- CSS custom properties: `--dz-{category}-{element}-{property}`
    65	- Token build artifacts committed (ADR-12)
    66	- Theme persistence with localStorage + FOUC-preventing head script (ADR-15)
    67	
    68	#### Architecture
    69	
    70	- **Contract Spec v1**: All 186 components satisfy canonical prop groups (behavior, appearance, state, accessibility), typed events, and typed slots
    71	- **Typed injection keys** (ADR-08): Compound component families use `InjectionKey<T>` with `DZ_{FAMILY}_KEY` naming
    72	- **ESM-only** (ADR-11): No CJS output, `"type": "module"` everywhere
    73	- **defineModel()** (ADR-16): All v-model components use `defineModel<T>()` — no manual prop+emit
    74	- **Reka UI** (ADR-07): Headless primitives for Dialog, Select, Tabs, Menu, Combobox, Popover, Tooltip, Accordion
    75	- **tailwind-variants (tv)**: Type-safe variant styling for all components
    76	- **cn() merging** (ADR-10): Consumer class override on all components
    77	- **Manifest-only exports** (ADR-01): Generated from `public-api.manifest.json`
    78	
    79	#### Quality Infrastructure
    80	
    81	- 227 test files, 2303 tests (all passing)
    82	- 110 contract conformance specs
    83	- 110 Storybook stories
    84	- Import boundary validator (0 violations)
    85	- Token compliance checker (0 raw color literals)
    86	- ESLint with Vue/TypeScript rules (0 errors)
    87	- vue-tsc typecheck (0 errors)
    88	- CI pipeline (GitHub Actions): typecheck, lint, test, validate, build, storybook build, coverage
    89	
    90	#### Tech Stack
    91	
    92	- Vue 3.5+ with `<script setup lang="ts">`
    93	- TypeScript 5.6+ (strict mode)
    94	- Tailwind CSS 4
    95	- Reka UI (headless primitives)
    96	- @internationalized/date (date manipulation)
    97	- @floating-ui/vue (positioning)
    98	- @vue-dnd-kit/core (drag and drop)
    99	- lucide-vue-next (icons)
   100	- Vite (library mode build)
   101	- Vitest (testing)
   102	- Storybook 8.5 (documentation)
   103	- Yarn 4 workspaces
   104	
   105	### Package Sizes (npm pack)
   106	
   107	| Package | Packed | Unpacked | Files |
   108	|---------|--------|----------|-------|
   109	| @dzup-ui/core | 200.6 kB | 1.3 MB | 803 |
   110	| @dzup-ui/pro | 104.8 kB | 597.9 kB | 263 |

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# credential-scan.sh — scan git history for accidentally committed secrets
     3	# Run this BEFORE executing git filter-repo (H3 history purge).
     4	# Usage: bash scripts/credential-scan.sh [--fix]
     5	#
     6	# Requires: git (already installed)
     7	# Optionally uses: gitleaks (https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks) if available
     8	# No network access required; pure git log scanning.
     9	
    10	set -euo pipefail
    11	
    12	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
    13	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    14	
    15	RED='\033[0;31m'
    16	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    17	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    18	NC='\033[0m'
    19	
    20	echo "=== Credential scan for: $(basename "$REPO_ROOT") ==="
    21	echo "Working dir: $REPO_ROOT"
    22	echo ""
    23	
    24	FOUND=0
    25	
    26	# ─── Pattern list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27	# Covers tokens, keys, and credentials commonly leaked in UI repos.
    28	declare -a PATTERNS=(
    29	  # Generic high-entropy tokens / API keys
    30	  'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'                         # AWS access key ID
    31	  '[Aa][Ww][Ss]_[Ss][Ee][Cc][Rr][Ee][Tt]'   # AWS secret reference
    32	  'ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}'                      # GitHub personal access token (classic)
    33	  'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}'             # GitHub fine-grained PAT
    34	  'npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}'                      # npm automation token
    35	  'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}'                       # OpenAI-style secret key
    36	  'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9\-]{10,48}'         # Slack token
    37	  # Generic patterns
    38	  '[Pp][Aa][Ss][Ss][Ww][Oo][Rr][Dd]\s*=\s*["\x27][^"]{6,}'
    39	  '[Ss][Ee][Cc][Rr][Ee][Tt]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][^"]{8,}'
    40	  '[Aa][Pp][Ii][_-][Kk][Ee][Yy]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][^"]{8,}'
    41	  '[Tt][Oo][Kk][Ee][Nn]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][A-Za-z0-9_\-\.]{16,}'
    42	  # Private keys
    43	  '-----BEGIN (RSA|EC|DSA|OPENSSH|PGP) PRIVATE KEY'
    44	  # Connection strings
    45	  'mongodb(\+srv)?://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    46	  'postgres(ql)?://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    47	  'mysql://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    48	  'redis://:?[^@]+@'
    49	  # .env-style
    50	  '^(export )?[A-Z_]+=.{8,}$'
    51	)
    52	
    53	# ─── 1. Scan current working tree ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    54	echo "--- [1/3] Scanning working tree ---"
    55	for pat in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    56	  matches=$(git grep -lE "$pat" -- ':!node_modules' ':!*.lock' ':!scripts/credential-scan.sh' 2>/dev/null || true)
    57	  if [[ -n "$matches" ]]; then
    58	    echo -e "${RED}PATTERN HIT (working tree):${NC} $pat"
    59	    echo "$matches" | sed 's/^/  /'
    60	    FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    61	  fi
    62	done
    63	
    64	# ─── 2. Scan git history (all refs) ──────────────────────────────────────────
    65	echo ""
    66	echo "--- [2/3] Scanning git history (all commits) ---"
    67	echo "    (This may take a minute on large repos)"
    68	
    69	for pat in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    70	  # git log -G searches diff content; -S counts occurrences
    71	  hits=$(git log --all --oneline -G "$pat" -- 2>/dev/null | head -20 || true)
    72	  if [[ -n "$hits" ]]; then
    73	    echo -e "${RED}PATTERN HIT (history):${NC} $pat"
    74	    echo "$hits" | sed 's/^/  commit: /'
    75	    FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    76	  fi
    77	done
    78	
    79	# ─── 3. Check for .env files ever tracked ────────────────────────────────────
    80	echo ""
    81	echo "--- [3/3] Checking for .env files ever tracked ---"
    82	env_history=$(git log --all --full-history --oneline -- '**/.env' '.env' '**/.env.*' '.env.*' 2>/dev/null || true)
    83	if [[ -n "$env_history" ]]; then
    84	  echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: .env files found in history:${NC}"
    85	  echo "$env_history" | sed 's/^/  /'
    86	  FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    87	else
    88	  echo -e "${GREEN}OK: No .env files found in history.${NC}"
    89	fi
    90	
    91	# ─── Optional: gitleaks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92	echo ""
    93	if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    94	  echo "--- [bonus] Running gitleaks detect ---"
    95	  gitleaks detect --source . --no-git 2>&1 || true
    96	else
    97	  echo -e "${YELLOW}TIP: Install gitleaks for a more thorough scan:${NC}"
    98	  echo "  https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks#installation"
    99	fi
   100	
   101	# ─── Summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   102	echo ""
   103	echo "========================================="
   104	if [[ $FOUND -gt 0 ]]; then
   105	  echo -e "${RED}SCAN COMPLETE — $FOUND pattern(s) matched.${NC}"
   106	  echo ""
   107	  echo "Next steps:"
   108	  echo "  1. Manually review each hit above to confirm it is a real secret."
   109	  echo "  2. Rotate any confirmed credentials immediately."
   110	  echo "  3. Then run the H3 filter-repo purge (see STATUS.md §H3)."
   111	  exit 1
   112	else
   113	  echo -e "${GREEN}SCAN COMPLETE — No obvious credentials found.${NC}"
   114	  echo "You may proceed with the H3 git filter-repo purge."
   115	fi

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# release-rehearsal.sh — full pre-publish smoke test for dzup-ui
     3	# Simulates what CI does on a clean checkout, but runs locally.
     4	#
     5	# Usage:
     6	#   bash scripts/release-rehearsal.sh
     7	#   bash scripts/release-rehearsal.sh --skip-install   # reuse existing node_modules
     8	#
     9	# Requirements: node >=20, yarn (corepack), git
    10	
    11	set -euo pipefail
    12	
    13	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
    14	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    15	
    16	RED='\033[0;31m'
    17	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    18	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    19	BOLD='\033[1m'
    20	NC='\033[0m'
    21	
    22	SKIP_INSTALL=false
    23	for arg in "$@"; do
    24	  [[ "$arg" == "--skip-install" ]] && SKIP_INSTALL=true
    25	done
    26	
    27	step() { echo -e "\n${BOLD}==> $*${NC}"; }
    28	ok()   { echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $*${NC}"; }
    29	fail() { echo -e "${RED}✗ FAILED: $*${NC}"; exit 1; }
    30	warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $*${NC}"; }
    31	
    32	echo ""
    33	echo -e "${BOLD}dzup-ui release rehearsal${NC}"
    34	echo "Repo: $REPO_ROOT"
    35	echo "Date: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
    36	echo "Node: $(node --version)"
    37	echo "Yarn: $(yarn --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')"
    38	echo ""
    39	
    40	# ─── 1. Clean install ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    41	if [[ "$SKIP_INSTALL" == "false" ]]; then
    42	  step "1. yarn install --immutable"
    43	  yarn install --immutable || fail "yarn install --immutable"
    44	  ok "Dependencies installed"
    45	else
    46	  warn "Skipping install (--skip-install)"
    47	fi
    48	
    49	# ─── 2. Typecheck ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50	step "2. yarn typecheck:all"
    51	yarn typecheck:all || fail "typecheck:all"
    52	ok "No type errors"
    53	
    54	# ─── 3. Lint ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55	step "3. yarn lint"
    56	yarn lint || fail "lint"
    57	ok "No lint errors"
    58	
    59	# ─── 4. Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60	step "4. yarn test (unit + contracts + a11y)"
    61	yarn test           || fail "yarn test"
    62	yarn test:contracts || fail "yarn test:contracts"
    63	yarn test:a11y      || fail "yarn test:a11y"
    64	ok "All tests pass"
    65	
    66	# ─── 5. Build ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67	step "5. yarn build"
    68	yarn build || fail "yarn build"
    69	ok "Build succeeded"
    70	
    71	# ─── 6. Verify dist artifacts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72	step "6. Verify dist artifacts"
    73	MISSING=()
    74	declare -a EXPECTED=(
    75	  "packages/contracts/dist/index.js"
    76	  "packages/contracts/dist/index.d.ts"
    77	  "packages/tokens/dist/index.js"
    78	  "packages/tokens/dist/index.d.ts"
    79	  "packages/core/dist/index.js"
    80	  "packages/core/dist/index.d.ts"
    81	  "packages/compat/dist/index.js"
    82	  "packages/codemods/dist/index.js"
    83	  "packages/nuxt/dist/module.js"
    84	)
    85	for f in "${EXPECTED[@]}"; do
    86	  [[ -f "$f" ]] || MISSING+=("$f")
    87	done
    88	if [[ ${#MISSING[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    89	  echo "Missing dist artifacts:"
    90	  printf '  %s\n' "${MISSING[@]}"
    91	  fail "dist artifacts incomplete"
    92	fi
    93	ok "All expected dist artifacts present"
    94	
    95	# ─── 7. ESM-only check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96	step "7. ESM-only policy check (no CJS output)"
    97	if find packages/core/dist packages/tokens/dist packages/contracts/dist \
    98	     -name "*.cjs" -o -name "*.cjs.js" 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
    99	  fail "CJS output detected — ESM-only policy violation"
   100	fi
   101	ok "ESM-only output verified"
   102	
   103	# ─── 8. Pack smoke tests for all publishable packages ─────────────────────────
   104	step "8. npm pack --dry-run smoke tests"
   105	declare -a PUBLISHABLE=(
   106	  "packages/contracts"
   107	  "packages/tokens"
   108	  "packages/core"
   109	  "packages/compat"
   110	  "packages/codemods"
   111	  "packages/nuxt"
   112	)
   113	for pkg in "${PUBLISHABLE[@]}"; do
   114	  if [[ ! -f "$pkg/package.json" ]]; then
   115	    warn "Skipping $pkg (no package.json)"
   116	    continue
   117	  fi
   118	  pack_out=$(cd "$pkg" && npm pack --dry-run 2>&1)
   119	  if echo "$pack_out" | grep -q "link:"; then
   120	    echo "$pack_out"
   121	    fail "$pkg: link: dependency in tarball"
   122	  fi
   123	  if echo "$pack_out" | grep -q "workspace:"; then
   124	    echo "$pack_out"
   125	    fail "$pkg: workspace: dependency in tarball (not resolved — use real semver)"
   126	  fi
   127	  ok "$pkg: pack clean"
   128	done
   129	
   130	# ─── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   131	echo ""
   132	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}=========================================${NC}"
   133	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}  Release rehearsal PASSED${NC}"
   134	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}=========================================${NC}"
   135	echo ""
   136	echo "All gates passed. The repo is ready to publish."
   137	echo "Next: coordinate H3 history purge, then run the actual changeset publish."

     1	import type { Component } from 'vue'
     2	/**
     3	 * SSR Smoke Tests — Core Components
     4	 *
     5	 * Verifies that all core component families can render to string
     6	 * via @vue/server-renderer without throwing. This catches SSR-unsafe
     7	 * patterns like direct DOM access outside onMounted(), missing
     8	 * browser API guards, etc.
     9	 *
    10	 * These are NOT behavioral tests — they only assert "renders without crash".
    11	 */
    12	import { renderToString } from '@vue/server-renderer'
    13	import { createSSRApp, h } from 'vue'
    14	
    15	// Reka UI primitives can be slow during SSR in jsdom — allow generous timeout
    16	vi.setConfig({ testTimeout: 15000 })
    17	
    18	/**
    19	 * Helper: render a component to HTML string via SSR.
    20	 * Returns the HTML or throws if SSR fails.
    21	 */
    22	async function ssrRender(
    23	  component: Component,
    24	  props: Record<string, unknown> = {},
    25	  children?: Record<string, () => ReturnType<typeof h>>,
    26	): Promise<string> {
    27	  const app = createSSRApp({
    28	    render() {
    29	      return h(component, props, children)
    30	    },
    31	  })
    32	  return renderToString(app)
    33	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Export Validation Script (ADR-01)
     3	 *
     4	 * For manifest-driven packages (core): reads public-api.manifest.json and validates:
     5	 *   1. Every referenced source file exists on disk
     6	 *   2. Every declared export symbol is actually exported from its source file
     7	 *   3. No orphaned manifest entries (file exists but exports nothing declared)
     8	 *
     9	 * For flat-export packages (compat, nuxt): validates that:
    10	 *   1. The package entry file exists
    11	 *   2. The file exports at least one symbol (non-empty barrel)
    12	 *   3. The package.json `exports` map points to resolvable paths
    13	 *
    14	 * Usage:
    15	 *   tsx packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts
    16	 *
    17	 * Exit code 1 if any errors found.
    18	 */
    19	
    20	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    21	import { dirname, relative, resolve } from 'node:path'
    22	import process from 'node:process'
    23	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    24	
    25	// --- Types matching manifest JSON shape (from manifest-generator.ts) ---
    26	
    27	interface ManifestEntry {
    28	  path: string
    29	  exports: string[]
    30	}
    31	
    32	interface ManifestExports {
    33	  components: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    34	  composables: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    35	  utilities: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    36	  injectionKeys?: string[]
    37	  variants?: string[]
    38	  runtimeExports?: string[]
    39	  types?: string[]
    40	}
    41	
    42	interface Manifest {
    43	  $schema?: string
    44	  package: string
    45	  version: string
    46	  description?: string
    47	  exports: ManifestExports
    48	}
    49	
    50	interface ValidationError {
    51	  package: string
    52	  category: string
    53	  entry: string
    54	  filePath: string
    55	  message: string
    56	}
    57	
    58	interface PackageJson {
    59	  exports?: Record<string, unknown>
    60	  main?: string
    61	  module?: string
    62	}
    63	
    64	// --- Constants ---
    65	
    66	const ROOT = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../')
    67	
    68	const MANIFEST_PATHS: Array<{ name: string, path: string, packageDir: string }> = [
    69	  {
    70	    name: '@dzup-ui/core',
    71	    path: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json'),
    72	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core'),
    73	  },
    74	]
    75	
    76	/**
    77	 * Flat-export packages: no manifest — validate the entry file and export map.
    78	 * entryFile is relative to packageDir (src/ path, the source of truth pre-build).
    79	 */
    80	const FLAT_EXPORT_PACKAGES: Array<{ name: string, packageDir: string, entryFile: string }> = [
    81	  {
    82	    name: '@dzup-ui/compat',
    83	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/compat'),
    84	    entryFile: 'src/index.ts',
    85	  },
    86	  {
    87	    name: '@dzup-ui/nuxt',
    88	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/nuxt'),
    89	    entryFile: 'src/module.ts',
    90	  },
    91	]
    92	
    93	// --- Helpers ---
    94	
    95	/**
    96	 * Resolves a manifest-relative path (e.g. "./src/components/buttons/index.ts")
    97	 * to an absolute path from the package directory.
    98	 */
    99	function resolveManifestPath(packageDir: string, manifestPath: string): string {
   100	  return resolve(packageDir, manifestPath)
   101	}
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Reads a file and returns the set of exported symbol names.
   105	 * For `export *` re-exports, returns a special marker.
   106	 */
   107	function getExportedSymbols(filePath: string): { symbols: Set<string>, hasStarExport: boolean } {
   108	  const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   109	  const symbols = new Set<string>()
   110	  let hasStarExport = false
   111	
   112	  // Check for `export *` re-exports
   113	  if (/export\s*\*\s*from/.test(content)) {
   114	    hasStarExport = true
   115	  }
   116	
   117	  // Named exports: export const/let/var/function/class/interface/type/enum
   118	  const namedRe = /export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:const|let|var|function|class|interface|type|enum|abstract\s+class)\s+(\w+)/g
   119	  let match: RegExpExecArray | null = namedRe.exec(content)
   120	  while (match !== null) {
   121	    if (match[1] !== undefined) {
   122	      symbols.add(match[1])
   123	    }
   124	    match = namedRe.exec(content)
   125	  }
   126	
   127	  // Destructured exports: export { Foo, Bar, Baz as Qux }
   128	  const bracketRe = /export\s*\{([^}]+)\}/g
   129	  match = bracketRe.exec(content)
   130	  while (match !== null) {
   131	    if (match[1] !== undefined) {
   132	      const names = match[1].split(',').map(s => s.trim())
   133	      for (const name of names) {
   134	        // Handle "Foo as Bar" — the exported name is "Bar"
   135	        const asMatch = /\w+\s+as\s+(\w+)/.exec(name)
   136	        if (asMatch !== null && asMatch[1] !== undefined) {
   137	          symbols.add(asMatch[1])
   138	        }
   139	        else {
   140	          // Simple export, handle "type Foo" prefix
   141	          const cleanName = name.replace(/^type\s+/, '').trim()
   142	          if (cleanName.length > 0) {
   143	            symbols.add(cleanName)
   144	          }
   145	        }
   146	      }
   147	    }
   148	    match = bracketRe.exec(content)
   149	  }
   150	
   151	  return { symbols, hasStarExport }
   152	}
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * Validates all entries in a single category (components, composables, utilities).
   156	 */
   157	function validateCategory(
   158	  packageName: string,
   159	  packageDir: string,
   160	  categoryName: string,
   161	  entries: Record<string, ManifestEntry>,
   162	): ValidationError[] {
   163	  const errors: ValidationError[] = []
   164	
   165	  for (const [entryName, entry] of Object.entries(entries)) {
   166	    const filePath = resolveManifestPath(packageDir, entry.path)
   167	    const relPath = relative(ROOT, filePath)
   168	
   169	    // Check 1: Source file exists
   170	    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
   171	      errors.push({
   172	        package: packageName,
   173	        category: categoryName,
   174	        entry: entryName,
   175	        filePath: relPath,
   176	        message: `Source file does not exist: ${relPath}`,
   177	      })
   178	      continue // Cannot check exports if file is missing
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    // Check 2: Declared exports exist in the source file
   182	    const { symbols, hasStarExport } = getExportedSymbols(filePath)
   183	
   184	    for (const exportName of entry.exports) {
   185	      if (!symbols.has(exportName) && !hasStarExport) {
   186	        errors.push({
   187	          package: packageName,
   188	          category: categoryName,
   189	          entry: entryName,
   190	          filePath: relPath,
   191	          message: `Export "${exportName}" declared in manifest but not found in ${relPath}`,
   192	        })
   193	      }
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    // Check 3: Orphan detection — file has no matching exports and no star re-export
   197	    if (!hasStarExport && entry.exports.length > 0) {
   198	      const foundCount = entry.exports.filter(e => symbols.has(e)).length
   199	      if (foundCount === 0 && symbols.size === 0) {
   200	        errors.push({
   201	          package: packageName,
   202	          category: categoryName,
   203	          entry: entryName,
   204	          filePath: relPath,
   205	          message: `File ${relPath} exports nothing — all ${entry.exports.length} manifest symbols are orphaned`,
   206	        })
   207	      }
   208	    }
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  return errors
   212	}
   213	
   214	// --- Flat-export validation (compat, nuxt) ---
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * Validates a flat-export package (no manifest):
   218	 *  1. Entry source file exists
   219	 *  2. Entry file exports at least one symbol
   220	 *  3. package.json `exports` map has a "." entry pointing to a path (dist existence
   221	 *     is skipped pre-build — we validate the source barrel only)
   222	 */
   223	function validateFlatExportPackage(
   224	  pkg: { name: string, packageDir: string, entryFile: string },
   225	): ValidationError[] {
   226	  const errors: ValidationError[] = []
   227	  const entryPath = resolve(pkg.packageDir, pkg.entryFile)
   228	  const relEntry = relative(ROOT, entryPath)
   229	
   230	  // Check 1: entry source file exists
   231	  if (!existsSync(entryPath)) {
   232	    errors.push({
   233	      package: pkg.name,
   234	      category: 'entry',
   235	      entry: pkg.entryFile,
   236	      filePath: relEntry,
   237	      message: `Entry file does not exist: ${relEntry}`,
   238	    })
   239	    return errors
   240	  }
   241	
   242	  // Check 2: entry file exports at least one symbol
   243	  const { symbols, hasStarExport } = getExportedSymbols(entryPath)
   244	  if (symbols.size === 0 && !hasStarExport) {
   245	    errors.push({
   246	      package: pkg.name,
   247	      category: 'entry',
   248	      entry: pkg.entryFile,
   249	      filePath: relEntry,
   250	      message: `Entry file exports nothing: ${relEntry}`,
   251	    })
   252	  }
   253	
   254	  // Check 3: package.json exports map has a "." entry
   255	  const pkgJsonPath = resolve(pkg.packageDir, 'package.json')
   256	  if (existsSync(pkgJsonPath)) {
   257	    const pkgJson = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pkgJsonPath, 'utf-8')) as PackageJson
   258	    if (!pkgJson.exports || !('.' in pkgJson.exports)) {
   259	      errors.push({
   260	        package: pkg.name,
   261	        category: 'package.json',
   262	        entry: '.',
   263	        filePath: relative(ROOT, pkgJsonPath),
   264	        message: `package.json "exports" map is missing a "." entry`,
   265	      })
   266	    }
   267	  }
   268	
   269	  return errors
   270	}
   271	
   272	// --- Main ---
   273	
   274	function main(): void {
   275	  const allErrors: ValidationError[] = []
   276	  let totalEntries = 0
   277	  let totalExports = 0
   278	
   279	  for (const manifestDef of MANIFEST_PATHS) {
   280	    if (!existsSync(manifestDef.path)) {
   281	      console.error(`  SKIP  Manifest not found: ${relative(ROOT, manifestDef.path)}`)
   282	      continue
   283	    }
   284	
   285	    const raw = readFileSync(manifestDef.path, 'utf-8')
   286	    const manifest: Manifest = JSON.parse(raw) as Manifest
   287	    const { exports: me } = manifest
   288	
   289	    console.warn(`\nValidating ${manifestDef.name} (${relative(ROOT, manifestDef.path)})`)
   290	
   291	    // Validate each category
   292	    const categories: Array<{ name: string, entries: Record<string, ManifestEntry> }> = [
   293	      { name: 'components', entries: me.components },
   294	      { name: 'composables', entries: me.composables },
   295	      { name: 'utilities', entries: me.utilities },
   296	    ]
   297	
   298	    for (const cat of categories) {
   299	      const entryCount = Object.keys(cat.entries).length
   300	      const exportCount = Object.values(cat.entries).reduce((sum, e) => sum + e.exports.length, 0)
   301	      totalEntries += entryCount
   302	      totalExports += exportCount
   303	
   304	      const errors = validateCategory(manifestDef.name, manifestDef.packageDir, cat.name, cat.entries)
   305	      allErrors.push(...errors)
   306	
   307	      const status = errors.length === 0 ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL'
   308	      console.warn(`  ${status}  ${cat.name}: ${entryCount} entries, ${exportCount} exports`)
   309	    }
   310	  }
   311	
   312	  // Validate flat-export packages (no manifest — check entry file + export map)
   313	  for (const pkg of FLAT_EXPORT_PACKAGES) {
   314	    console.warn(`\nValidating ${pkg.name} (flat-export: ${pkg.entryFile})`)
   315	    const errors = validateFlatExportPackage(pkg)
   316	    allErrors.push(...errors)
   317	
   318	    const { symbols, hasStarExport } = existsSync(resolve(pkg.packageDir, pkg.entryFile))
   319	      ? getExportedSymbols(resolve(pkg.packageDir, pkg.entryFile))
   320	      : { symbols: new Set<string>(), hasStarExport: false }
