     1	/**
     2	 * Declaration File (.d.ts) Validator
     3	 *
     4	 * For each publishable package validates that:
     5	 *   1. dist/ directories exist (skip with warning if not — pre-build state)
     6	 *   2. Every .js file in dist/ has a corresponding .d.ts file
     7	 *      (Vite SFC runtime chunks are excluded from this check for Vue packages)
     8	 *   3. The root declaration file (dist/index.d.ts or dist/module.d.ts for nuxt)
     9	 *      contains at least one export statement
    10	 *
    11	 * Covers all 6 publishable packages: contracts, tokens, core, compat, codemods, nuxt.
    12	 *
    13	 * Usage:
    14	 *   tsx packages/tooling/scripts/validate-dts.ts
    15	 *
    16	 * Exit code 1 if any errors found.
    17	 */
    18	
    19	import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
    20	import { dirname, relative, resolve } from 'node:path'
    21	import process from 'node:process'
    22	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    23	
    24	// --- Types ---
    25	
    26	interface ManifestEntry {
    27	  path: string
    28	  exports: string[]
    29	}
    30	
    31	interface ManifestExports {
    32	  components: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    33	  composables: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    34	  utilities: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    35	  injectionKeys?: string[]
    36	  variants?: string[]
    37	  runtimeExports?: string[]
    38	  types?: string[]
    39	}
    40	
    41	interface Manifest {
    42	  $schema?: string
    43	  package: string
    44	  version: string
    45	  description?: string
    46	  exports: ManifestExports
    47	}
    48	
    49	interface ValidationError {
    50	  package: string
    51	  file: string
    52	  message: string
    53	}
    54	
    55	// --- Constants ---
    56	
    57	const ROOT = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../')
    58	
    59	interface PackageDef {
    60	  name: string
    61	  distDir: string
    62	  /** Filename of the root declaration file inside distDir (default: "index.d.ts") */
    63	  rootDts?: string
    64	  /** Whether to apply Vite SFC chunk exclusion (Vue packages only) */
    65	  hasViteChunks?: boolean
    66	  /** Optional path to public-api.manifest.json — used only for index.d.ts export coverage */
    67	  manifestPath?: string
    68	}
    69	
    70	const PACKAGES: PackageDef[] = [
    71	  {
    72	    name: '@dzup-ui/contracts',
    73	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/contracts/dist'),
    74	  },
    75	  {
    76	    name: '@dzup-ui/tokens',
    77	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/tokens/dist'),
    78	  },
    79	  {
    80	    name: '@dzup-ui/core',
    81	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core/dist'),
    82	    hasViteChunks: true,
    83	    manifestPath: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json'),
    84	  },
    85	  {
    86	    name: '@dzup-ui/compat',
    87	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/compat/dist'),
    88	    hasViteChunks: true,
    89	  },
    90	  {
    91	    name: '@dzup-ui/codemods',
    92	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/codemods/dist'),
    93	  },
    94	  {
    95	    name: '@dzup-ui/nuxt',
    96	    distDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/nuxt/dist'),
    97	    rootDts: 'module.d.ts',
    98	  },
    99	]
   100	
   101	// --- Helpers ---
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Recursively collects all files under a directory.
   105	 */
   106	function collectFiles(dir: string): string[] {
   107	  const results: string[] = []
   108	  const entries = readdirSync(dir, { withFileTypes: true })
   109	
   110	  for (const entry of entries) {
   111	    const fullPath = resolve(dir, entry.name)
   112	    if (entry.isDirectory()) {
   113	      results.push(...collectFiles(fullPath))
   114	    }
   115	    else {
   116	      results.push(fullPath)
   117	    }
   118	  }
   119	
   120	  return results
   121	}
   122	
   123	/**
   124	 * Returns true if a .js file is a Vite-internal chunk that does not need
   125	 * its own .d.ts file.  Vite splits Vue SFCs into `.vue.js` / `.vue2.js`
   126	 * runtime chunks, and emits helpers in `_virtual/`.  The type information
   127	 * for these lives in the corresponding `Component.d.ts` produced by
   128	 * vite-plugin-dts, so a 1:1 `.js → .d.ts` check would be a false positive.
   129	 */
   130	function isViteChunk(filePath: string): boolean {
   131	  const rel = relative(ROOT, filePath)
   132	  // SFC render/setup chunks: DzButton.vue.js, DzButton.vue2.js
   133	  if (/\.vue\d*\.js$/.test(filePath)) {
   134	    return true
   135	  }
   136	  // Vite virtual helper modules (_virtual/_plugin-vue_export-helper.js)
   137	  if (rel.includes('_virtual/')) {
   138	    return true
   139	  }
   140	  return false
   141	}
   142	
   143	/**
   144	 * Checks that every non-chunk .js file in dist/ has a corresponding .d.ts file.
   145	 * Pass `hasViteChunks: true` for Vue (Vite-built) packages to skip SFC runtime chunks.
   146	 */
   147	function validateDtsParity(
   148	  packageName: string,
   149	  distDir: string,
   150	  hasViteChunks = false,
   151	): { errors: ValidationError[], jsCount: number, dtsCount: number, chunkCount: number } {
   152	  const errors: ValidationError[] = []
   153	  const allFiles = collectFiles(distDir)
   154	
   155	  const allJsFiles = allFiles.filter(f => f.endsWith('.js') || f.endsWith('.mjs'))
   156	  const jsFiles = hasViteChunks ? allJsFiles.filter(f => !isViteChunk(f)) : allJsFiles
   157	  const chunkCount = allJsFiles.length - jsFiles.length
   158	  const dtsFiles = new Set(allFiles.filter(f => f.endsWith('.d.ts') || f.endsWith('.d.mts')))
   159	
   160	  for (const jsFile of jsFiles) {
   161	    // Convert .js → .d.ts and .mjs → .d.mts
   162	    const dtsFile = jsFile.endsWith('.mjs')
   163	      ? jsFile.replace(/\.mjs$/, '.d.mts')
   164	      : jsFile.replace(/\.js$/, '.d.ts')
   165	
   166	    if (!dtsFiles.has(dtsFile)) {
   167	      errors.push({
   168	        package: packageName,
   169	        file: relative(ROOT, jsFile),
   170	        message: `Missing .d.ts for ${relative(ROOT, jsFile)} (expected ${relative(ROOT, dtsFile)})`,
   171	      })
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  return { errors, jsCount: jsFiles.length, dtsCount: dtsFiles.size, chunkCount }
   176	}
   177	
   178	/**
   179	 * Reads the root declaration file and checks that it contains export statements.
   180	 * For manifest-backed packages (core), also verifies category coverage.
   181	 * For manifest-less packages, only verifies that exports exist (non-empty barrel).
   182	 */
   183	function validateRootDtsExports(
   184	  packageName: string,
   185	  distDir: string,
   186	  rootDtsName: string,
   187	  manifest: Manifest | null,
   188	): ValidationError[] {
   189	  const errors: ValidationError[] = []
   190	  const rootDts = resolve(distDir, rootDtsName)
   191	
   192	  if (!existsSync(rootDts)) {
   193	    errors.push({
   194	      package: packageName,
   195	      file: relative(ROOT, rootDts),
   196	      message: `Root declaration file missing: ${relative(ROOT, rootDts)}`,
   197	    })
   198	    return errors
   199	  }
   200	
   201	  const content = readFileSync(rootDts, 'utf-8')
   202	
   203	  // Check that the root .d.ts contains at least one export statement
   204	  if (!/export[\s{*]/.test(content)) {
   205	    errors.push({
   206	      package: packageName,
   207	      file: relative(ROOT, rootDts),
   208	      message: `Root ${rootDtsName} contains no export statements`,
   209	    })
   210	    return errors
   211	  }
   212	
   213	  // For manifest-less packages, the above check is sufficient
   214	  if (manifest === null) {
   215	    return errors
   216	  }
   217	
   218	  // Collect all `export * from '...'` re-export paths (resolved to dist/)
   219	  const starReExportPaths = new Set<string>()
   220	  const starRe = /export\s*\*\s*from\s*['"]([^'"]+)['"]/g
   221	  let reMatch = starRe.exec(content)
   222	  while (reMatch !== null) {
   223	    if (reMatch[1] !== undefined) {
   224	      // Resolve relative to dist dir, normalizing .ts → .d.ts for matching
   225	      const resolved = resolve(distDir, reMatch[1])
   226	      starReExportPaths.add(resolved)
   227	    }
   228	    reMatch = starRe.exec(content)
   229	  }
   230	
   231	  // For each manifest category, verify that at least one symbol is reachable —
   232	  // either by direct name in the index.d.ts OR via a `export *` re-export whose
   233	  // path covers the manifest entry's directory.
   234	  const categories: Array<{ name: string, entries: Record<string, ManifestEntry> }> = [
   235	    { name: 'components', entries: manifest.exports.components },
   236	    { name: 'composables', entries: manifest.exports.composables },
   237	    { name: 'utilities', entries: manifest.exports.utilities },
   238	  ]
   239	
   240	  for (const cat of categories) {
   241	    const entryNames = Object.keys(cat.entries)
   242	    if (entryNames.length === 0) {
   243	      continue
   244	    }
   245	
   246	    // Collect all declared export symbols from this category
   247	    const allSymbols: string[] = []
   248	    for (const entry of Object.values(cat.entries)) {
   249	      allSymbols.push(...entry.exports)
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    if (allSymbols.length === 0) {
   253	      continue
   254	    }
   255	
   256	    // Check 1: Does any symbol name appear directly in the content?
   257	    const directHit = allSymbols.some(symbol => content.includes(symbol))
   258	    if (directHit) {
   259	      continue
   260	    }
   261	
   262	    // Check 2: Do any `export *` re-exports cover the manifest entry paths?
   263	    // Manifest paths are like "./src/components/buttons/index.ts" — in dist
   264	    // they become "./components/buttons/index.ts" (src/ stripped).
   265	    const coveredByReExport = Object.values(cat.entries).some((entry) => {
   266	      // Convert manifest source path to dist-relative path
   267	      const distRelative = entry.path.replace(/^\.\/src\//, './')
   268	      const resolved = resolve(distDir, distRelative)
   269	      return starReExportPaths.has(resolved)
   270	    })
   271	
   272	    if (!coveredByReExport) {
   273	      errors.push({
   274	        package: packageName,
   275	        file: relative(ROOT, rootDts),
   276	        message: `Category "${cat.name}" has ${allSymbols.length} declared exports but none found in ${rootDtsName} (no direct symbols or re-export paths match)`,
   277	      })
   278	    }
   279	  }
   280	
   281	  return errors
   282	}
   283	
   284	// --- Main ---
   285	
   286	function main(): void {
   287	  const allErrors: ValidationError[] = []
   288	  let totalJs = 0
   289	  let totalDts = 0
   290	  let skippedCount = 0
   291	
   292	  console.warn('Declaration file (.d.ts) validation\n')
   293	
   294	  for (const pkg of PACKAGES) {
   295	    // Gracefully handle pre-build state
   296	    if (!existsSync(pkg.distDir) || !statSync(pkg.distDir).isDirectory()) {
   297	      console.warn(`  SKIP  ${pkg.name} — dist/ not found (run build first)`)
   298	      skippedCount++
   299	      continue
   300	    }
   301	
   302	    // Load manifest if available (core only)
   303	    let manifest: Manifest | null = null
   304	    if (pkg.manifestPath !== undefined) {
   305	      if (!existsSync(pkg.manifestPath)) {
   306	        console.warn(`  SKIP  ${pkg.name} — manifest not found: ${relative(ROOT, pkg.manifestPath)}`)
   307	        skippedCount++
   308	        continue
   309	      }
   310	      const raw = readFileSync(pkg.manifestPath, 'utf-8')
   311	      manifest = JSON.parse(raw) as Manifest
   312	    }
   313	
   314	    const rootDtsName = pkg.rootDts ?? 'index.d.ts'
   315	    console.warn(`Validating ${pkg.name} (${relative(ROOT, pkg.distDir)})`)
   316	
   317	    // Check 1: .js → .d.ts parity
   318	    const { errors: parityErrors, jsCount, dtsCount, chunkCount } = validateDtsParity(
   319	      pkg.name,
   320	      pkg.distDir,

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Peer Dependency Validation Script
     3	 *
     4	 * Reads package.json from each workspace package and checks that every
     5	 * peerDependencies entry is satisfied by:
     6	 *   - The version installed in root node_modules/ (for external deps)
     7	 *   - The workspace package version (for @dzup-ui/* workspace deps)
     8	 *
     9	 * Usage:
    10	 *   tsx packages/tooling/scripts/validate-peers.ts
    11	 *
    12	 * Exit code 1 if any incompatible peer dependency found.
    13	 */
    14	
    15	import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    16	import { dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
    17	import process from 'node:process'
    18	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    19	
    20	// --- Types ---
    21	
    22	interface PackageJson {
    23	  name: string
    24	  version: string
    25	  peerDependencies?: Record<string, string>
    26	  peerDependenciesMeta?: Record<string, { optional?: boolean }>
    27	}
    28	
    29	type Status = 'PASS' | 'WARN' | 'FAIL'
    30	
    31	interface PeerCheckResult {
    32	  dep: string
    33	  range: string
    34	  resolved: string
    35	  status: Status
    36	  message: string
    37	}
    38	
    39	interface PackageResult {
    40	  name: string
    41	  checks: PeerCheckResult[]
    42	}
    43	
    44	// --- Constants ---
    45	
    46	const ROOT = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../')
    47	const PACKAGES_DIR = resolve(ROOT, 'packages')
    48	const NODE_MODULES = resolve(ROOT, 'node_modules')
    49	
    50	// --- Helpers ---
    51	
    52	/**
    53	 * Reads and parses a package.json file.
    54	 * Returns null if the file does not exist.
    55	 */
    56	function readPackageJson(dir: string): PackageJson | null {
    57	  const pkgPath = resolve(dir, 'package.json')
    58	  if (!existsSync(pkgPath)) {
    59	    return null
    60	  }
    61	  const raw = readFileSync(pkgPath, 'utf-8')
    62	  return JSON.parse(raw) as PackageJson
    63	}
    64	
    65	/**
    66	 * Builds a map of workspace package names to their versions.
    67	 */
    68	function buildWorkspaceVersionMap(): Map<string, string> {
    69	  const map = new Map<string, string>()
    70	  const packageDirs = readdirSync(PACKAGES_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
    71	    .filter(d => d.isDirectory())
    72	    .map(d => resolve(PACKAGES_DIR, d.name))
    73	
    74	  for (const dir of packageDirs) {
    75	    const pkg = readPackageJson(dir)
    76	    if (pkg !== null) {
    77	      map.set(pkg.name, pkg.version)
    78	    }
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  return map
    82	}
    83	
    84	/**
    85	 * Gets the installed version of a package from node_modules.
    86	 * Returns null if not found.
    87	 */
    88	function getInstalledVersion(depName: string): string | null {
    89	  // Handle scoped packages (@scope/name)
    90	  const depDir = resolve(NODE_MODULES, depName)
    91	  const pkg = readPackageJson(depDir)
    92	  return pkg !== null ? pkg.version : null
    93	}
    94	
    95	/**
    96	 * Checks if a concrete version satisfies a semver range.
    97	 * Implements basic semver range checking without external dependencies:
    98	 *   - ^x.y.z (caret ranges)
    99	 *   - ~x.y.z (tilde ranges)
   100	 *   - >=x.y.z
   101	 *   - x.y.z (exact)
   102	 *   - workspace:* (always satisfied for workspace deps)
   103	 */
   104	function satisfiesRange(version: string, range: string): boolean {
   105	  // workspace protocol — always satisfied within monorepo
   106	  if (range.startsWith('workspace:')) {
   107	    return true
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  const parseSemver = (v: string): [number, number, number, string] | null => {
   111	    // Strip leading = or v
   112	    const cleaned = v.replace(/^[=v]+/, '')
   113	    const match = /^(\d+)\.(\d+)\.(\d+)(?:-(.+))?$/.exec(cleaned)
   114	    if (match === null || match[1] === undefined || match[2] === undefined || match[3] === undefined) {
   115	      return null
   116	    }
   117	    return [
   118	      Number.parseInt(match[1], 10),
   119	      Number.parseInt(match[2], 10),
   120	      Number.parseInt(match[3], 10),
   121	      match[4] ?? '',
   122	    ]
   123	  }
   124	
   125	  const compareVersions = (a: [number, number, number, string], b: [number, number, number, string]): number => {
   126	    for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
   127	      if (a[i] !== b[i]) {
   128	        return (a[i] as number) - (b[i] as number)
   129	      }
   130	    }
   131	    // Pre-release versions sort before release
   132	    if (a[3] !== '' && b[3] === '')
   133	      return -1
   134	    if (a[3] === '' && b[3] !== '')
   135	      return 1
   136	    return a[3].localeCompare(b[3])
   137	  }
   138	
   139	  const ver = parseSemver(version)
   140	  if (ver === null)
   141	    return false
   142	
   143	  // Handle caret range: ^x.y.z
   144	  if (range.startsWith('^')) {
   145	    const rangeVer = parseSemver(range.slice(1))
   146	    if (rangeVer === null)
   147	      return false
   148	
   149	    // Version must be >= range version
   150	    if (compareVersions(ver, rangeVer) < 0)
   151	      return false
   152	
   153	    // Major must match (for major > 0)
   154	    if (rangeVer[0] > 0) {
   155	      return ver[0] === rangeVer[0]
   156	    }
   157	    // For ^0.y.z, minor must match
   158	    if (rangeVer[1] > 0) {
   159	      return ver[0] === 0 && ver[1] === rangeVer[1]
   160	    }
   161	    // For ^0.0.z, patch must match
   162	    return ver[0] === 0 && ver[1] === 0 && ver[2] === rangeVer[2]
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  // Handle tilde range: ~x.y.z
   166	  if (range.startsWith('~')) {
   167	    const rangeVer = parseSemver(range.slice(1))
   168	    if (rangeVer === null)
   169	      return false
   170	
   171	    if (compareVersions(ver, rangeVer) < 0)
   172	      return false
   173	
   174	    return ver[0] === rangeVer[0] && ver[1] === rangeVer[1]
   175	  }
   176	
   177	  // Handle >= range
   178	  if (range.startsWith('>=')) {
   179	    const rangeVer = parseSemver(range.slice(2).trim())
   180	    if (rangeVer === null)
   181	      return false
   182	    return compareVersions(ver, rangeVer) >= 0
   183	  }
   184	
   185	  // Exact version
   186	  const rangeVer = parseSemver(range)
   187	  if (rangeVer === null)
   188	    return false
   189	  return compareVersions(ver, rangeVer) === 0
   190	}
   191	
   192	/**
   193	 * Checks all peer dependencies for a single package.
   194	 */
   195	function checkPeerDeps(
   196	  pkg: PackageJson,
   197	  workspaceVersions: Map<string, string>,
   198	): PeerCheckResult[] {
   199	  const results: PeerCheckResult[] = []
   200	  const peers = pkg.peerDependencies ?? {}
   201	  const meta = pkg.peerDependenciesMeta ?? {}
   202	
   203	  for (const [dep, range] of Object.entries(peers)) {
   204	    const isOptional = meta[dep]?.optional === true
   205	
   206	    // Check if it's a workspace package
   207	    const workspaceVersion = workspaceVersions.get(dep)
   208	    if (workspaceVersion !== undefined) {
   209	      const satisfied = satisfiesRange(workspaceVersion, range)
   210	      results.push({
   211	        dep,
   212	        range,
   213	        resolved: workspaceVersion,
   214	        status: satisfied ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL',
   215	        message: satisfied
   216	          ? `workspace ${workspaceVersion} satisfies ${range}`
   217	          : `workspace ${workspaceVersion} does NOT satisfy ${range}`,
   218	      })
   219	      continue
   220	    }
   221	
   222	    // Check installed version in node_modules
   223	    const installedVersion = getInstalledVersion(dep)
   224	
   225	    if (installedVersion === null) {
   226	      results.push({
   227	        dep,
   228	        range,
   229	        resolved: 'not installed',
   230	        status: isOptional ? 'WARN' : 'FAIL',
   231	        message: isOptional
   232	          ? `not installed (optional peer — acceptable)`
   233	          : `not installed but required`,
   234	      })
   235	      continue
   236	    }
   237	
   238	    const satisfied = satisfiesRange(installedVersion, range)
   239	    results.push({
   240	      dep,
   241	      range,
   242	      resolved: installedVersion,
   243	      status: satisfied ? 'PASS' : 'FAIL',
   244	      message: satisfied
   245	        ? `${installedVersion} satisfies ${range}`
   246	        : `${installedVersion} does NOT satisfy ${range}`,
   247	    })
   248	  }
   249	
   250	  return results
   251	}
   252	
   253	// --- Main ---
   254	
   255	function main(): void {
   256	  console.warn('Peer dependency validation\n')
   257	
   258	  const workspaceVersions = buildWorkspaceVersionMap()
   259	  const packageResults: PackageResult[] = []
   260	
   261	  const packageDirs = readdirSync(PACKAGES_DIR, { withFileTypes: true })
   262	    .filter(d => d.isDirectory())
   263	    .map(d => resolve(PACKAGES_DIR, d.name))
   264	
   265	  for (const dir of packageDirs) {
   266	    const pkg = readPackageJson(dir)
   267	    if (pkg === null)
   268	      continue
   269	
   270	    const peers = pkg.peerDependencies ?? {}
   271	    if (Object.keys(peers).length === 0)
   272	      continue
   273	
   274	    const checks = checkPeerDeps(pkg, workspaceVersions)
   275	    packageResults.push({ name: pkg.name, checks })
   276	  }
   277	
   278	  if (packageResults.length === 0) {
   279	    console.warn('  No packages with peer dependencies found.')
   280	    console.warn('\nPeer dependency validation passed.')

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/* eslint-disable no-console */
     2	/**
     3	 * Dependency License Audit
     4	 *
     5	 * Scans all production dependencies of core (MIT) and pro (commercial) packages
     6	 * for incompatible licenses. Flags GPL, AGPL, and other copyleft licenses that
     7	 * could create legal issues.
     8	 *
     9	 * Usage:
    10	 *   npx tsx packages/tooling/src/license-audit.ts
    11	 *   npx tsx packages/tooling/src/license-audit.ts --ci
    12	 *
    13	 * @module @dzup-ui/tooling/license-audit
    14	 */
    15	
    16	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    17	import { join, resolve } from 'node:path'
    18	import process from 'node:process'
    19	
    20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21	// Types
    22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23	
    24	export interface LicenseEntry {
    25	  package: string
    26	  version: string
    27	  license: string
    28	  allowed: boolean
    29	  reason?: string
    30	}
    31	
    32	export interface LicenseAuditResult {
    33	  entries: LicenseEntry[]
    34	  passed: number
    35	  flagged: number
    36	  unknown: number
    37	  allPassed: boolean
    38	}
    39	
    40	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41	// License Classification
    42	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    43	
    44	/**
    45	 * Licenses that are compatible with both MIT and commercial distribution.
    46	 */
    47	const ALLOWED_LICENSES = new Set([
    48	  'MIT',
    49	  'ISC',
    50	  'BSD-2-Clause',
    51	  'BSD-3-Clause',
    52	  'Apache-2.0',
    53	  '0BSD',
    54	  'CC0-1.0',
    55	  'CC-BY-4.0',
    56	  'CC-BY-3.0',
    57	  'Unlicense',
    58	  'BlueOak-1.0.0',
    59	  'Python-2.0',
    60	  'Zlib',
    61	  'Artistic-2.0',
    62	  'W3C',
    63	])
    64	
    65	/**
    66	 * Licenses that are definitely incompatible with commercial distribution.
    67	 */
    68	const BLOCKED_LICENSES = new Set([
    69	  'GPL-2.0',
    70	  'GPL-2.0-only',
    71	  'GPL-2.0-or-later',
    72	  'GPL-3.0',
    73	  'GPL-3.0-only',
    74	  'GPL-3.0-or-later',
    75	  'AGPL-3.0',
    76	  'AGPL-3.0-only',
    77	  'AGPL-3.0-or-later',
    78	  'LGPL-2.0',
    79	  'LGPL-2.1',
    80	  'LGPL-3.0',
    81	  'SSPL-1.0',
    82	  'EUPL-1.1',
    83	  'EUPL-1.2',
    84	  'OSL-3.0',
    85	  'CPAL-1.0',
    86	  'CPOL-1.02',
    87	])
    88	
    89	function classifyLicense(license: string): { allowed: boolean, reason?: string } {
    90	  const normalized = license.trim()
    91	
    92	  if (ALLOWED_LICENSES.has(normalized)) {
    93	    return { allowed: true }
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  if (BLOCKED_LICENSES.has(normalized)) {
    97	    return { allowed: false, reason: `Copyleft license: ${normalized}` }
    98	  }
    99	
   100	  // Handle SPDX expressions like "(MIT OR Apache-2.0)"
   101	  if (normalized.startsWith('(') && normalized.endsWith(')')) {
   102	    const inner = normalized.slice(1, -1)
   103	    const parts = inner.split(/\s+OR\s+/i)
   104	    const anyAllowed = parts.some(p => ALLOWED_LICENSES.has(p.trim()))
   105	    if (anyAllowed) {
   106	      return { allowed: true }
   107	    }
   108	  }
   109	
   110	  return { allowed: false, reason: `Unknown license: ${normalized}` }
   111	}
   112	
   113	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   114	// Dependency Discovery
   115	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116	
   117	function getProductionDeps(packageJsonPath: string): Map<string, string> {
   118	  if (!existsSync(packageJsonPath))
   119	    return new Map()
   120	
   121	  const raw = JSON.parse(readFileSync(packageJsonPath, 'utf-8'))
   122	  const deps = new Map<string, string>()
   123	
   124	  for (const [name, version] of Object.entries(raw.dependencies ?? {})) {
   125	    deps.set(name, version as string)
   126	  }
   127	  for (const [name, version] of Object.entries(raw.peerDependencies ?? {})) {
   128	    deps.set(name, version as string)
   129	  }
   130	
   131	  return deps
   132	}
   133	
   134	function getPackageLicense(rootDir: string, pkgName: string): { version: string, license: string } {
   135	  const pkgJsonPath = join(rootDir, 'node_modules', pkgName, 'package.json')
   136	
   137	  if (!existsSync(pkgJsonPath)) {
   138	    return { version: 'unknown', license: 'UNKNOWN' }
   139	  }
   140	
   141	  const raw = JSON.parse(readFileSync(pkgJsonPath, 'utf-8'))
   142	  return {
   143	    version: raw.version ?? 'unknown',
   144	    license: raw.license ?? 'UNKNOWN',
   145	  }
   146	}
   147	
   148	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   149	// Audit
   150	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   151	
   152	export function runLicenseAudit(rootDir: string): LicenseAuditResult {
   153	  const packages = ['packages/core', 'packages/tokens']
   154	  const allDeps = new Map<string, string>()
   155	
   156	  for (const pkg of packages) {
   157	    const deps = getProductionDeps(join(rootDir, pkg, 'package.json'))
   158	    for (const [name, version] of deps) {
   159	      // Skip workspace and internal dependencies
   160	      if (version === 'workspace:*' || name.startsWith('@dzup-ui/'))
   161	        continue
   162	      allDeps.set(name, version)
   163	    }
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  const entries: LicenseEntry[] = []
   167	
   168	  for (const [name] of allDeps) {
   169	    const { version, license } = getPackageLicense(rootDir, name)
   170	    const { allowed, reason } = classifyLicense(license)
   171	
   172	    entries.push({
   173	      package: name,
   174	      version,
   175	      license,
   176	      allowed,
   177	      reason,
   178	    })
   179	  }
   180	
   181	  entries.sort((a, b) => {
   182	    if (a.allowed !== b.allowed)
   183	      return a.allowed ? 1 : -1
   184	    return a.package.localeCompare(b.package)
   185	  })
   186	
   187	  const passed = entries.filter(e => e.allowed).length
   188	  const flagged = entries.filter(e => !e.allowed && e.license !== 'UNKNOWN').length
   189	  const unknown = entries.filter(e => e.license === 'UNKNOWN').length
   190	
   191	  return {
   192	    entries,
   193	    passed,
   194	    flagged,
   195	    unknown,
   196	    allPassed: flagged === 0,
   197	  }
   198	}
   199	
   200	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   201	// Output
   202	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   203	
   204	function printHumanReport(result: LicenseAuditResult): void {
   205	  console.log('\n=== Dependency License Audit ===\n')
   206	
   207	  const maxNameLen = Math.max(...result.entries.map(e => e.package.length), 20)
   208	
   209	  for (const entry of result.entries) {
   210	    const status = entry.allowed ? 'OK  ' : entry.license === 'UNKNOWN' ? 'WARN' : 'FAIL'
   211	    const name = entry.package.padEnd(maxNameLen + 2)
   212	    const reason = entry.reason ? ` — ${entry.reason}` : ''
   213	    console.log(`  ${status}  ${name}${entry.license.padEnd(15)} v${entry.version}${reason}`)
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  console.log(`\nResults: ${result.passed} allowed, ${result.flagged} blocked, ${result.unknown} unknown`)
   217	
   218	  if (result.flagged > 0) {
   219	    console.error(`\nLicense audit FAILED: ${result.flagged} dependencies have incompatible licenses`)
   220	  }
   221	  else if (result.unknown > 0) {
   222	    console.log(`\nWarning: ${result.unknown} dependencies have unknown licenses — review manually`)
   223	  }
   224	  else {
   225	    console.log('\nAll dependency licenses are compatible.')
   226	  }
   227	}
   228	
   229	function printCiReport(result: LicenseAuditResult): void {
   230	  console.log(JSON.stringify({
   231	    status: result.allPassed ? 'pass' : 'fail',
   232	    summary: { passed: result.passed, flagged: result.flagged, unknown: result.unknown },
   233	    entries: result.entries,
   234	  }, null, 2))
   235	}
   236	
   237	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   238	// CLI
   239	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   240	
   241	function main(): void {
   242	  const args = process.argv.slice(2)
   243	  const ciMode = args.includes('--ci')
   244	  const rootDir = resolve(import.meta.dirname, '..', '..', '..')
   245	
   246	  const result = runLicenseAudit(rootDir)
   247	
   248	  if (ciMode) {
   249	    printCiReport(result)
   250	  }
   251	  else {
   252	    printHumanReport(result)
   253	  }
   254	
   255	  process.exit(result.flagged > 0 ? 1 : 0)
   256	}
   257	
   258	const isDirectRun = process.argv[1]?.includes('license-audit')
   259	if (isDirectRun) {
   260	  main()
   261	}

     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * dzup-codemod CLI entry point.
     5	 *
     6	 * Usage:
     7	 *   dzup-codemod <transform> <target-directory> [options]
     8	 *
     9	 * Transforms:
    10	 *   rename-imports   Rewrite old import paths to @dzup-ui/core and @dzup-ui/pro
    11	 *   rename-props     Rename deprecated props to vNext equivalents
    12	 *   rename-events    Rename deprecated events to vNext equivalents
    13	 *   all              Run all transforms in sequence
    14	 *
    15	 * Options:
    16	 *   --dry-run        Preview changes without writing files
    17	 *   --verbose        Print per-file status
    18	 *   --extensions     Comma-separated file extensions (default: js,jsx,ts,tsx,vue)
    19	 *   --parser         jscodeshift parser: babel | ts | tsx (default: tsx)
    20	 *   --help           Show this help message
    21	 *
    22	 * Examples:
    23	 *   dzup-codemod rename-imports ./src
    24	 *   dzup-codemod all ./src --dry-run --verbose
    25	 */
    26	
    27	import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    28	import { resolve } from 'node:path'
    29	import process from 'node:process'
    30	
    31	const TRANSFORMS = ['rename-imports', 'rename-props', 'rename-events', 'all']
    32	
    33	function printHelp() {
    34	  process.stdout.write(`
    35	dzup-codemod - Migrate from old dzup-ui to vNext
    36	
    37	Usage:
    38	  dzup-codemod <transform> <target-directory> [options]
    39	
    40	Transforms:
    41	  rename-imports   Rewrite old import paths to @dzup-ui/core and @dzup-ui/pro
    42	  rename-props     Rename deprecated props to vNext equivalents
    43	  rename-events    Rename deprecated events to vNext equivalents
    44	  all              Run all transforms in sequence
    45	
    46	Options:
    47	  --dry-run        Preview changes without writing files
    48	  --verbose        Print per-file status
    49	  --extensions     Comma-separated file extensions (default: js,jsx,ts,tsx,vue)
    50	  --parser         jscodeshift parser: babel | ts | tsx (default: tsx)
    51	  --help           Show this help message
    52	
    53	Examples:
    54	  dzup-codemod rename-imports ./src
    55	  dzup-codemod all ./src --dry-run --verbose
    56	`)

    58	
    59	function parseArgs(argv) {

    71	  const positional = []

    74	    const arg = args[i]
    75	
    76	    if (arg === '--help' || arg === '-h') {
    77	      opts.help = true
    78	    }
    79	    else if (arg === '--dry-run') {
    80	      opts.dryRun = true
    81	    }
    82	    else if (arg === '--verbose' || arg === '-v') {
    83	      opts.verbose = true
    84	    }
    85	    else if (arg === '--extensions' && i + 1 < args.length) {
    86	      opts.extensions = args[++i].split(',')
    87	    }
    88	    else if (arg === '--parser' && i + 1 < args.length) {
    89	      opts.parser = args[++i]
    90	    }
    91	    else if (!arg.startsWith('--')) {
    92	      positional.push(arg)
    93	    }
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  opts.transform = positional[0]
    97	  opts.target = positional[1]
    98	
    99	  return opts
   100	}
   101	
   102	async function loadTransform(name) {
   103	  const modulePath = new URL(`../dist/transforms/${name}.js`, import.meta.url)
   104	  const mod = await import(modulePath.href)
   105	  return mod.default ?? mod.transformer
   106	}
   107	
   108	async function main() {
   109	  const opts = parseArgs(process.argv)
   110	
   111	  if (opts.help || !opts.transform || !opts.target) {
   112	    printHelp()
   113	    process.exit(opts.help ? 0 : 1)
   114	  }
   115	
   116	  if (!TRANSFORMS.includes(opts.transform)) {
   117	    process.stderr.write(
   118	      `Unknown transform: ${opts.transform}\nAvailable: ${TRANSFORMS.join(', ')}\n`,
   119	    )
   120	    process.exit(1)
   121	  }
   122	
   123	  const targetPath = resolve(process.cwd(), opts.target)
   124	  if (!existsSync(targetPath)) {
   125	    process.stderr.write(`Target directory does not exist: ${targetPath}\n`)
   126	    process.exit(1)
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  // Dynamically import the runner
   130	  const { CodemodRunner } = await import('../dist/runner.js')
   131	
   132	  const runnerOpts = {
   133	    dryRun: opts.dryRun,
   134	    verbose: opts.verbose,
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  if (opts.extensions)
   138	    runnerOpts.extensions = opts.extensions
   139	  if (opts.parser)
   140	    runnerOpts.parser = opts.parser
   141	
   142	  const runner = new CodemodRunner(runnerOpts)
   143	
   144	  const transformNames
   145	    = opts.transform === 'all'
   146	      ? ['rename-imports', 'rename-props', 'rename-events']
   147	      : [opts.transform]
   148	
   149	  let allSuccess = true
   150	
   151	  for (const name of transformNames) {
   152	    process.stdout.write(`\nRunning transform: ${name}\n`)
   153	    process.stdout.write(`${'─'.repeat(40)}\n`)
   154	
   155	    const transform = await loadTransform(name)
   156	    const result = await runner.run(transform, targetPath)
   157	
   158	    if (!result.success) {
   159	      allSuccess = false
   160	    }
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  process.exit(allSuccess ? 0 : 1)
   164	}
   165	
   166	main().catch((err) => {
   167	  process.stderr.write(`Fatal error: ${err.message}\n`)
   168	  process.exit(1)
   169	})

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Auto-import resolver for unplugin-vue-components.
     3	 *
     4	 * Enables consumers to use `<DzButton>`, `<DzInput>`, etc. in templates
     5	 * without manual import statements.
     6	 *
     7	 * @example
     8	 * ``\`ts
     9	 * // vite.config.ts
    10	 * import Components from 'unplugin-vue-components/vite'
    11	 * import { DzResolver } from '@dzup-ui/core/resolver'
    12	 *
    13	 * export default defineConfig({
    14	 *   plugins: [
    15	 *     Components({
    16	 *       resolvers: [DzResolver()],
    17	 *     }),
    18	 *   ],
    19	 * })
    20	 * ``\`
    21	 */
    22	
    23	export interface DzResolverOptions {
    24	  /**
    25	   * When true, also resolves pro components from `@dzup-ui/pro`.
    26	   * When false (default), only resolves core components.
    27	   */
    28	  includePro?: boolean
    29	}
    30	
    31	/**
    32	 * Component prefixes that belong to `@dzup-ui/pro`.
    33	 *
    34	 * This list is derived from the actual pro package component inventory.
    35	 * Components not matching any of these prefixes resolve to `@dzup-ui/core`.
    36	 */
    37	const PRO_COMPONENT_PREFIXES = [
    38	  // builders family
    39	  'DzFormBuilder',
    40	  'DzDashboardBuilder',
    41	  'DzDashboardWidget',
    42	  // data-pro family
    43	  'DzDataGridPro',
    44	  'DzFilterBuilder',
    45	  'DzPivotTable',
    46	  'DzQuickFilter',
    47	  'DzVirtualTable',
    48	  // editors family
    49	  'DzCodeEditor',
    50	  'DzJsonEditor',
    51	  'DzMarkdownEditor',
    52	  'DzRichTextEditor',
    53	  // planning family
    54	  'DzCalendar',
    55	  'DzScheduler',
    56	  'DzKanban',
    57	  'DzGantt',
    58	  // workflow family
    59	  'DzWorkflow',
    60	  // communication family
    61	  'DzChat',
    62	  'DzComment',
    63	  'DzReactionPicker',
    64	  // business family
    65	  'DzAppShell',
    66	  'DzNotificationCenter',
    67	  'DzAuditLog',
    68	  // visualization family
    69	  'DzChart',
    70	  'DzHeatMap',
    71	  'DzTreeMap',
    72	  'DzDiagramEditor',
    73	] as const
    74	
    75	/**
    76	 * Resolver for unplugin-vue-components that auto-imports Dz* components.
    77	 *
    78	 * All components prefixed with `Dz` are resolved. By default only core
    79	 * components are resolved; set `includePro: true` to also resolve pro
    80	 * components from `@dzup-ui/pro`.
    81	 *
    82	 * @param options - Resolver configuration
    83	 * @returns A component resolver compatible with unplugin-vue-components
    84	 */
    85	export function DzResolver(options: DzResolverOptions = {}) {
    86	  const { includePro = false } = options
    87	
    88	  return {
    89	    type: 'component' as const,
    90	    resolve: (name: string) => {
    91	      if (!name.startsWith('Dz'))
    92	        return
    93	
    94	      const isPro = PRO_COMPONENT_PREFIXES.some(prefix =>
    95	        name.startsWith(prefix),
    96	      )
    97	
    98	      if (isPro && !includePro)
    99	        return
   100	
   101	      const packageName = isPro ? '@dzup-ui/pro' : '@dzup-ui/core'
   102	
   103	      return {
   104	        name,
   105	        from: packageName,
   106	      }
   107	    },
   108	  }
   109	}

     1	/**
     2	 * Theme initialization script for FOUC prevention (ADR-15).
     3	 *
     4	 * This string contains inline JavaScript meant to be injected into the
     5	 * `<head>` of the HTML document BEFORE any rendering occurs. It reads the
     6	 * persisted theme from `localStorage` and sets the `data-theme` attribute
     7	 * on `<html>` so that the correct CSS variables are active on first paint.
     8	 *
     9	 * Usage in an HTML template or SSR framework:
    10	 *
    11	 * ``\`html
    12	 * <head>
    13	 *   <script>{{ themeScript }}</script>
    14	 * </head>
    15	 * ``\`
    16	 *
    17	 * Or with a bundler / SSR helper:
    18	 *
    19	 * ``\`ts
    20	 * import { getThemeScript } from '@dzup-ui/core/providers'
    21	 *
    22	 * // Custom storage key and attribute
    23	 * const script = getThemeScript({ storageKey: 'my-theme', attribute: 'data-mode' })
    24	 * ``\`
    25	 *
    26	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/providers/theme-script
    27	 */
    28	
    29	/** Options for generating the theme initialization script */
    30	export interface ThemeScriptOptions {
    31	  /** localStorage key (default: 'dz-theme') */
    32	  storageKey?: string
    33	  /** HTML attribute name (default: 'data-theme') */
    34	  attribute?: string
    35	  /** Default theme when nothing is persisted (default: 'system') */
    36	  defaultTheme?: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'
    37	}
    38	
    39	/**
    40	 * Generate a theme initialization script string with custom options.
    41	 *
    42	 * @param options - Script generation options
    43	 * @returns Inline JavaScript string for `<head>` injection
    44	 */
    45	export function getThemeScript(options: ThemeScriptOptions = {}): string {
    46	  const {
    47	    storageKey = 'dz-theme',
    48	    attribute = 'data-theme',
    49	    defaultTheme = 'system',
    50	  } = options
    51	
    52	  return `(function(){try{var s=localStorage.getItem(${JSON.stringify(storageKey)});var t=s==='light'||s==='dark'?s:s==='system'||!s?null:null;if(!t){var d=${JSON.stringify(defaultTheme)};t=d==='system'?window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').matches?'dark':'light':d}document.documentElement.setAttribute(${JSON.stringify(attribute)},t)}catch(e){}})()`
    53	}
    54	
    55	/**
    56	 * Pre-built inline script using default options (ADR-15).
    57	 *
    58	 * Reads `localStorage` key `dz-theme` and sets `data-theme` on `<html>`.
    59	 * Falls back to system preference detection via `matchMedia`.
    60	 *
    61	 * Inject in `<head>` before any stylesheets or body content:
    62	 *
    63	 * ``\`html
    64	 * <script>{{ themeScript }}</script>
    65	 * ``\`
    66	 */
    67	export const themeScript: string = getThemeScript()

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * useTheme — Consumer composable for DzThemeProvider context.
     3	 *
     4	 * Injects the theme context from the nearest DzThemeProvider ancestor.
     5	 * Issues a dev-mode warning if used outside a DzThemeProvider.
     6	 *
     7	 * Returns the ADR-09 minimal API: theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme.
     8	 *
     9	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/providers/useTheme
    10	 */
    11	
    12	import type { DzThemeContext } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    13	import { inject } from 'vue'
    14	import { DZ_THEME_KEY } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    15	
    16	/**
    17	 * Inject theme context from the nearest DzThemeProvider ancestor.
    18	 *
    19	 * @throws Dev-mode warning if no provider is found.
    20	 * @returns The DzThemeContext with theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme.
    21	 *
    22	 * @example
    23	 * ``\`vue
    24	 * <script setup lang="ts">
    25	 * import { useTheme } from '@dzup-ui/core/providers'
    26	 *
    27	 * const { theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme } = useTheme()
    28	 * </script>
    29	 *
    30	 * <template>
    31	 *   <button @click="toggleTheme">
    32	 *     Current: {{ resolvedTheme }}
    33	 *   </button>
    34	 * </template>
    35	 * ``\`
    36	 */
    37	export function useTheme(): DzThemeContext {
    38	  const context = inject(DZ_THEME_KEY)
    39	
    40	  if (!context) {
    41	    if (import.meta.env?.DEV) {
    42	      console.warn(
    43	        '[DzThemeProvider] useTheme() was called outside of a <DzThemeProvider>. '
    44	        + 'Wrap your app in <DzThemeProvider> to use theme context.',
    45	      )
    46	    }
    47	    throw new Error(
    48	      'useTheme() requires a <DzThemeProvider> ancestor. '
    49	      + 'Wrap your component tree in <DzThemeProvider>.',
    50	    )
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  return context
    54	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Contributing to dzup-ui
     2	
     3	Thank you for your interest in contributing to dzup-ui! This document outlines the process for contributing to this MIT-licensed Vue 3 component library.
     4	
     5	## Code of Conduct
     6	
     7	Please read and follow our [Code of Conduct](CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) before contributing.
     8	
     9	## Getting Started
    10	
    11	### Prerequisites
    12	
    13	- Node.js >= 20.0.0
    14	- Yarn 4.x (`corepack enable && corepack prepare yarn@4.6.0 --activate`)
    15	
    16	### Setup
    17	
    18	``\`bash
    19	git clone https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui.git
    20	cd dzup-ui
    21	yarn install
    22	``\`
    23	
    24	### Verify your setup
    25	
    26	``\`bash
    27	yarn typecheck
    28	yarn test
    29	yarn build
    30	``\`
    31	
    32	## Development Workflow
    33	
    34	### Branches
    35	
    36	- `main` — stable, release-ready code
    37	- `next` — upcoming major changes
    38	- `feat/<name>` — new features
    39	- `fix/<name>` — bug fixes
    40	
    41	### Making Changes
    42	
    43	1. Fork the repository and create a branch from `main`.
    44	2. Make your changes, adding tests where appropriate.
    45	3. Run the full validation suite before submitting:
    46	
    47	``\`bash
    48	yarn typecheck
    49	yarn lint
    50	yarn test
    51	yarn test:contracts
    52	yarn validate:all
    53	``\`
    54	
    55	4. Ensure all tests pass and coverage does not drop below 80%.
    56	
    57	### Adding a Component
    58	
    59	- Place new components under `packages/core/src/components/<family>/`.
    60	- Add a contract spec (`*.contract.spec.ts`) alongside the component.
    61	- Add an a11y spec (`*.a11y.spec.ts`) for interactive components.
    62	- Export the component from `packages/core/src/index.ts`.
    63	- Add a Storybook story under `apps/storybook/src/stories/`.
    64	
    65	### Commit Messages
    66	
    67	Follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/):
    68	
    69	``\`
    70	feat(buttons): add DzSplitButton loading state
    71	fix(inputs): correct focus ring in dark mode
    72	docs(contributing): add component checklist
    73	``\`
    74	
    75	## Pull Requests
    76	
    77	- Keep PRs focused — one feature or fix per PR.
    78	- Fill in the PR template completely.
    79	- Link any related issues with `Closes #<issue>`.
    80	- All CI checks must pass before merge.
    81	- A maintainer review is required before merging.
    82	
    83	## Changesets
    84	
    85	This project uses [Changesets](https://github.com/changesets/changesets) for versioning.
    86	
    87	Add a changeset for any user-facing change:
    88	
    89	``\`bash
    90	yarn changeset
    91	``\`
    92	
    93	Follow the prompts to select the packages changed and the bump type (patch / minor / major).
    94	
    95	## Reporting Issues
    96	
    97	- Search existing issues before opening a new one.
    98	- Use the provided issue templates.
    99	- For security vulnerabilities, see [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md) — do **not** open a public issue.
   100	
   101	## License
   102	
   103	By contributing, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the [MIT License](LICENSE).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Security Policy
     2	
     3	## Supported Versions
     4	
     5	| Version | Supported |
     6	|---------|-----------|
     7	| 0.1.x (alpha) | Yes |
     8	
     9	## Reporting a Vulnerability
    10	
    11	**Do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.**
    12	
    13	Please report security issues by emailing the maintainers directly (see the contact in the GitHub repository's security advisories tab), or by using [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability).
    14	
    15	Include in your report:
    16	- A description of the vulnerability
    17	- Steps to reproduce
    18	- Potential impact
    19	- Any suggested fix (optional)
    20	
    21	You will receive a response within 5 business days. We aim to release a fix within 30 days of confirmation.
    22	
    23	## Scope
    24	
    25	This policy applies to the `@dzup-ui/core`, `@dzup-ui/tokens`, `@dzup-ui/contracts`, and `@dzup-ui/nuxt` packages published under the `dzup-ui` repository.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# Code of Conduct
     2	
     3	## Our Standards
     4	
     5	We are committed to a welcoming, inclusive, and professional environment for everyone contributing to dzup-ui.
     6	
     7	**Expected behavior:**
     8	- Be respectful and constructive in all interactions
     9	- Give and receive feedback graciously
    10	- Focus on what is best for the project and community
    11	- Assume good faith from other contributors
    12	
    13	**Unacceptable behavior:**
    14	- Personal attacks, insults, or inflammatory language
    15	- Harassment of any kind, public or private
    16	- Publishing others' private information without consent
    17	- Any conduct that would be considered unprofessional
    18	
    19	## Enforcement
    20	
    21	Project maintainers are responsible for enforcing this code of conduct. Violations may be reported to the maintainers via the contact listed in [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md). All reports will be reviewed confidentially.
    22	
    23	Maintainers reserve the right to remove, edit, or reject contributions that violate these standards, and to temporarily or permanently ban contributors for repeated or serious violations.
    24	
    25	## Attribution
    26	
    27	This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1.

     9	- Test/e2e areas sampled: `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/tests/ssr/ssr-smoke.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/tests/a11y/buttons.a11y.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e/components/buttons.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e/a11y/keyboard-nav.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:1`.
    10	- Sandbox/storybook app surface: `ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/router.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:1`.
    11	- Local `docs/` presence: only `ui/dzup-ui/docs/analyze-full_2026_04_21/codex_out_log.md:1`.
    12	- Secondary `out/` artifacts used for drift/context: `out/workspace-repo-docs-static-portable-markdown/DZUP_UI.md:1`, `out/workspace-repo-docs-static-portable-markdown/SUMMARY.md:16`, `out/code-features-mvp-test-3/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:1`, `out/code-features-live-sample/summaries/workspace-summary.md:101`.
    13	
    14	## Repository Identity
    15	- `dzup-ui` is a Yarn 4 monorepo UI platform for Vue 3, with `apps/*` and `packages/*` workspaces (`ui/dzup-ui/package.json:5`, `ui/dzup-ui/package.json:9`).
    16	- Its stated role is an open-source component library (`@dzup-ui/core`, tokens/contracts/compat/codemods/nuxt) for downstream apps and integrations (`ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3`, `ui/dzup-ui/README.md:53`).
    17	- Primary audience is frontend teams needing reusable design-system components with typed contracts and theming; migration paths are explicitly supported via `@dzup-ui/compat` and `@dzup-ui/codemods` (`ui/dzup-ui/README.md:117`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/index.ts:2`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/src/index.ts:2`).
    18	- Core stack is Vue 3 + TypeScript strict mode + Vite + Tailwind tokens, with unit/a11y/E2E testing and Nuxt integration (`ui/dzup-ui/README.md:72`, `ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:7`, `ui/dzup-ui/package.json:18`, `ui/dzup-ui/package.json:43`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:1`).
    19	- This repo has direct coupling to broader Datazup runtime types via `@datazup/dzupagent-orchestration-kit` for some feedback components (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/package.json:43`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/DzRunStatusBadge.types.ts:9`).
    20	
    21	## Implementation Surface
    22	- Monorepo shape includes 7 package directories plus 2 internal apps (`sandbox`, `storybook`) and E2E suites (`ui/dzup-ui/packages`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e`).
    23	- `@dzup-ui/core` is the dominant surface: 11 component families exported through a generated barrel and manifest-driven API (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/index.ts:7`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:7`).
    24	- Major domains are explicit in code layout and router/story structure: buttons, cards, inputs, forms, layout, navigation, overlays, feedback, data, media, typography (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/index.ts:9`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/router.ts:12`).
    25	- Supporting domains:
    26	- `@dzup-ui/tokens` generates CSS vars/types/tailwind theme artifacts (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/tokens/src/generate.ts:4`).
    27	- `@dzup-ui/contracts` publishes canonical prop/event/slot types (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/contracts/package.json:5`).
    28	- `@dzup-ui/compat` and `@dzup-ui/codemods` provide migration adapters/transforms (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/package.json:5`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/package.json:5`).
    29	- `@dzup-ui/nuxt` auto-registers component imports and injects theme bootstrapping (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:22`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:272`).
    30	- Operational complexity is high for a UI repo:
    31	- Core package has hundreds of files (also reflected in generated inventory: `666 source files` in `out/workspace-repo-docs-static-portable-markdown/DZUP_UI.md:80`).
    32	- Large/high-complexity modules exist in data-grid/select/dialog/compliance tooling tests and validators (for example `useDataGrid.spec.ts`, `useSelect.spec.ts`, `bundle-budget-check.ts` from local scan).
    33	
    34	## Maturity Signals
    35	- Production-grade signals:
    36	- Strong CI gate graph: typecheck, lint, unit, contract, a11y, SSR smoke, build, e2e, coverage, bundle budgets, pack smoke checks (`ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:18`, `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:64`, `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:96`, `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:185`, `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:293`).
    37	- Release pipelines include Changesets and npm provenance paths (`ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:51`, `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:57`).
    38	- Boundary and policy automation exists, not just docs: import boundaries, color-literal lint, export validation, peer validation, bundle validation (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/validators/import-boundary.ts:4`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/token-checks/color-lint.ts:4`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:4`).
    39	- Accessibility and SSR are first-class in test setup (`ui/dzup-ui/vitest.setup.a11y.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/tests/ssr/ssr-smoke.spec.ts:3`).
    40	- Dist artifacts are committed and rehearsed by policy (`ui/dzup-ui/scripts/release-rehearsal.sh:72`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/dist`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tokens/dist`).
    41	- Transitional signals:
    42	- Nuxt module tests are shallow smoke checks and do not validate registration correctness (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:11`).
    43	- Some components are explicit stubs/TODOs and not fully contractized yet (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/GovernanceBadge.vue:2`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/TeamMemberBadge.vue:2`).
    44	- Repo-local docs under `docs/` are minimal for maintainers (only an analysis run log present in this folder).
    45	
    46	## Current-State Caveats
    47	- Documentation drift is material in several places:
    48	- README claims 147 foundational components and old compat/codemods versions (`ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3`, `ui/dzup-ui/README.md:60`), while package manifests currently show `0.1.0-alpha.0` for compat/codemods (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/package.json:4`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/package.json:4`).
    49	- Root changelog references `@dzup-ui/pro` as if in-repo and contains legacy component naming/counts (`ui/dzup-ui/CHANGELOG.md:19`, `ui/dzup-ui/CHANGELOG.md:30`, `ui/dzup-ui/CHANGELOG.md:35`), while README now points pro users to a separate repo (`ui/dzup-ui/README.md:9`).
    50	- Contributing guide points new stories to `apps/storybook/src/stories` (`ui/dzup-ui/CONTRIBUTING.md:63`) but actual Storybook sourcing is `packages/core/stories/**/*.stories.ts` (`ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:12`).
    51	- `@dzup-ui/nuxt` core component registration list appears out of sync with current `@dzup-ui/core` manifest exports:
    52	- It registers names such as `DzFAB`, `DzDrawer`, `DzTabsList`, `DzSearchBox` (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:31`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:152`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:141`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:90`) that are not declared in current core manifest exports.
    53	- Export validation currently treats Nuxt as a flat-export package and does not verify per-component registration parity (`ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:80`), so this drift can pass automation.
    54	- Secondary `out/` artifacts are useful for orientation but are static and can lag live code:
    55	- Example: generated health summary says “no package-level docs detected” and reports static source/test counts (`out/workspace-repo-docs-static-portable-markdown/DZUP_UI.md:41`), which may not reflect current in-repo evolution since generation timestamp (`out/workspace-repo-docs-static-portable-markdown/DZUP_UI.md:3`).
    56	
    57	## Overall Assessment
    58	`dzup-ui` is a mature, production-leaning UI platform monorepo with strong engineering controls (strict TS, extensive CI gates, boundary/policy validators, release rehearsal, and broad test coverage patterns). Its highest current risk is not missing infrastructure, but consistency drift between live implementation and documentation/integration metadata, especially around component inventory/versioning and Nuxt auto-registration parity. The codebase itself is robustly structured; the biggest caveat is that consumers may be misled by stale docs or silently hit integration mismatches unless those contract/docs/registration surfaces are tightened.

   232	  },
   233	  defaults: {
   234	    includePro: false,
   235	    prefix: '',
   236	  },
   237	  setup(options, nuxt) {
   238	    const _resolver = createResolver(import.meta.url)
   239	
   240	    // Add @dzup-ui/tokens CSS
   241	    nuxt.options.css.push('@dzup-ui/tokens/dist/tokens.css')
   242	
   243	    // Add @dzup-ui/core styles
   244	    nuxt.options.css.push('@dzup-ui/core/styles')
   245	
   246	    // Transpile packages
   247	    nuxt.options.build.transpile.push('@dzup-ui/core', '@dzup-ui/tokens')
   248	    if (options.includePro) {
   249	      nuxt.options.build.transpile.push('@dzup-ui/pro')
   250	    }
   251	
   252	    // Register core components
   253	    for (const name of CORE_COMPONENTS) {
   254	      addComponent({
   255	        name: options.prefix ? `${options.prefix}${name.slice(2)}` : name,
   256	        export: name,
   257	        filePath: '@dzup-ui/core',
   258	      })
   259	    }
   260	
   261	    // Register pro components if enabled
   262	    if (options.includePro) {
   263	      for (const name of PRO_COMPONENTS) {
   264	        addComponent({
   265	          name: options.prefix ? `${options.prefix}${name.slice(2)}` : name,
   266	          export: name,
   267	          filePath: '@dzup-ui/pro',
   268	        })
   269	      }
   270	    }
   271	
   272	    // Add theme script to head for FOUC prevention (ADR-15)
   273	    nuxt.options.app.head.script = nuxt.options.app.head.script || []
   274	    nuxt.options.app.head.script.push({
   275	      innerHTML: `(function(){try{var t=localStorage.getItem('dz-theme');if(t)document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme',t);else if(window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').matches)document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme','dark')}catch(e){}})()`,
   276	      type: 'text/javascript',
   277	    })
   278	  },
   279	})

# 01 Current State Inventory

## Scope Reviewed
- Repository identity and public docs: `ui/dzup-ui/README.md:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/CONTRIBUTING.md:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/SECURITY.md:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/CHANGELOG.md:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/CLAUDE.md:1`.
- Workspace/package metadata and scripts: `ui/dzup-ui/package.json:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/.yarnrc.yml:1`, `.changeset/config.json:1`.
- Core build/lint/test config: `ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/eslint.config.js:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/vitest.config.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/bundlesize.config.json:1`.
- CI/release automation: `ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:1`, `.github/workflows/release.yml:1`, `.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/scripts/release-rehearsal.sh:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:1`.
- Implementation entrypoints and manifests: `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/index.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/resolver.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tokens/src/generate.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/contracts/src/index.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/index.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/src/runner.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:1`.
- Quality tooling/validators: `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/validators/import-boundary.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-dts.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-peers.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/license-audit.ts:1`.
- Runtime surfaces sampled: `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useFloating/useFloating.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.vue:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzDialog.vue:1`.
- Test surfaces sampled: `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/tests/ssr/ssr-smoke.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/tests/a11y/buttons.a11y.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/e2e/a11y/keyboard-nav.spec.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:1`.
- Internal app surfaces sampled: `ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/router.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/App.vue:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/preview.ts:1`, `ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/stories/Introduction.mdx:1`.
- Secondary `out/` artifacts reviewed for drift/context: `out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:1`, `out/code-features-current/scored/ui-dzup-ui.md:1`, `out/code-features-live-sample-3/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:1`.

## Repository Overview
- `ui/dzup-ui` is a multi-package UI platform monorepo centered on `@dzup-ui/core`, with support packages for tokens, contracts, migration compatibility, codemods, and Nuxt integration (`package.json:9`, `README.md:53`).
- The repo is tool-heavy and quality-gated: strict TypeScript, linting, unit/contract/a11y/SSR tests, E2E Storybook checks, boundary validation, export validation, peer/license audits, bundle checks, and release rehearsals (`package.json:13`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml:18`, `.github/workflows/ci.yml:96`, `scripts/release-rehearsal.sh:40`).
- Two internal apps (`apps/sandbox`, `apps/storybook`) serve as development and documentation/visual validation environments rather than end-user production apps (`apps/sandbox/package.json:2`, `apps/storybook/package.json:2`).

## Repository Identity
- Product role: open-source Vue 3 component library ecosystem with migration path from legacy dzup-ui APIs (`README.md:3`, `README.md:117`, `packages/compat/src/index.ts:2`, `packages/codemods/package.json:5`).
- Primary users: frontend application teams needing reusable components, design tokens, contract types, and optional Nuxt auto-registration (`README.md:53`, `packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:225`).
- Languages/frameworks: TypeScript + Vue SFCs (`package.json:4`, `README.md:72`), Vite library builds (`packages/core/vite.config.ts:23`), Vitest + Playwright testing (`package.json:18`, `package.json:43`).
- Package manager/runtime: Yarn 4 with Node >=20 (`package.json:5`, `package.json:7`), node-modules linker (`.yarnrc.yml:1`).
- Repository shape: workspaces at `packages/*` and `apps/*` (`package.json:9`), with publishable packages and internal tooling packages (`packages/*/package.json`).

## Implementation Surface
- Core API surface is manifest-driven and generated: `packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json` feeds generated barrel exports in `packages/core/src/index.ts` (`manifest-generator.ts:4`, `manifest-generator.ts:90`, `core/src/index.ts:1`).
- Major component domains are explicit and broad: 11 families (buttons, cards, inputs, forms, layout, media, data, feedback, navigation, overlays, typography) (`core/src/index.ts:9`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:7`).
- Supporting libraries/services:
- `@dzup-ui/tokens` generates CSS variables, TS token types, and Tailwind theme artifacts (`tokens/src/generate.ts:4`, `tokens/src/generate.ts:421`).
- `@dzup-ui/contracts` publishes canonical prop/event/slot taxonomies (`contracts/src/canonical.types.ts:12`, `contracts/src/index.ts:14`).
- `@dzup-ui/compat` wraps legacy props/events and emits deprecation warnings (`compat/src/index.ts:24`, `compat/src/utils/deprecation.ts:20`).
- `@dzup-ui/codemods` provides automated transform runner/CLI (`codemods/bin/dzup-codemod.js:4`, `codemods/src/runner.ts:71`).
- `@dzup-ui/nuxt` injects CSS, transpile config, component auto-imports, and a head theme script (`nuxt/src/module.ts:240`, `nuxt/src/module.ts:252`, `nuxt/src/module.ts:272`).
- Runtime integration complexity is non-trivial: broad `reka-ui` usage, floating positioning, date handling, icons, and theme SSR/FOUC handling (`core/src/components/overlays/DzDialog.vue:3`, `core/src/composables/useFloating/useFloating.ts:11`, `core/src/components/forms/DzDatePicker.vue:24`, `core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:1`).
- Scale signals from local tree scan: `packages/core/src/components` currently contains 160 `.vue` files, overall repo has roughly ~2k files under `packages/`, and test files are dense (229 `*.spec/test*` files across `packages` + `e2e`).
- Large-module signals: highly complex files around data grid and advanced composables/tests, e.g. `useDataGrid.spec.ts` (627 LOC), `useSelect.spec.ts` (513 LOC), `DzDataGrid.vue` (multi-state compound component, context + pagination + selection) (`DzDataGrid.vue:12`).

## Maturity Signals
- Strong production-grade CI gates: typed checks, lint, unit tests across Node 20/22, contract/a11y/SSR test lanes, boundary/token/export/changelog/peer/license checks, build + declaration validation + bundle budgets + pack smoke tests (`ci.yml:18`, `ci.yml:64`, `ci.yml:89`, `ci.yml:91`, `ci.yml:94`, `ci.yml:116`, `ci.yml:177`, `ci.yml:185`).
- Release engineering is structured: Changesets-driven PR/version/publish flow with npm provenance and prerelease workflow (`release.yml:51`, `release.yml:53`, `publish-prerelease.yml:57`).
- Tooling hygiene is advanced for a UI repo: import-boundary validator, declaration parity checks, peer semver checks, and license audit scripts are all custom and integrated (`import-boundary.ts:4`, `validate-dts.ts:4`, `validate-peers.ts:4`, `license-audit.ts:4`).
- Testing breadth is substantial: contract specs, a11y specs, SSR smoke tests, and Playwright Storybook e2e checks exist; Storybook infra tests explicitly check console errors and story rendering health (`package.json:21`, `package.json:22`, `package.json:23`, `e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts:15`, `e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts:168`).
- Transitional/less-mature indicators:
- Nuxt module tests are mostly smoke-level and do not assert component parity correctness (`packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:11`).
- Some governance docs are stale relative to implementation (details below).
- No backend-style migrations/jobs/workers exist (expected for UI library), so operational maturity is concentrated in frontend package quality gates rather than runtime ops infrastructure.

## Current-State Caveats
- Component-count/documentation drift is material:
- README and Storybook intro still claim 147 foundational components (`README.md:3`, `README.md:13`, `apps/storybook/stories/Introduction.mdx:8`).
- Changelog still references a different historical count and in-repo `@dzup-ui/pro` framing (`CHANGELOG.md:18`, `CHANGELOG.md:19`, `CHANGELOG.md:26`), while README now points pro users to a separate repo (`README.md:9`).
- Live manifest exports indicate a larger and changed component set (e.g. explicit entries for newer names like `DzSearchInput`, `DzTabList`, `DzTransfer`, `DzToastViewport`) (`core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:38`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:93`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:66`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:155`).
- Architecture-doc drift inside repo:
- `CLAUDE.md` states “no `<style scoped>`” and “155 `.vue` files” (`CLAUDE.md:48`, `CLAUDE.md:72`), but current core has many scoped-style components (for example `DzDataGrid.vue:195`, `DzButton.vue:187`, `DzDialogContent.vue:106`) and current file count is higher by local scan.
- Nuxt integration drift risk:
- Nuxt core list still includes names like `DzSearchBox`, `DzTabsList`, `DzTabsTrigger`, `DzFAB`, `DzDrawer` (`nuxt/src/module.ts:31`, `nuxt/src/module.ts:90`, `nuxt/src/module.ts:141`, `nuxt/src/module.ts:152`).
- Core manifest exports now use different/current names (e.g. `DzSearchInput`, `DzTabList`, `DzTabTrigger`) (`core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:38`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:93`, `core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:94`).
- Export validation currently checks Nuxt as “flat export exists” and does not validate that its component registry matches manifest/core exports (`packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:9`, `packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:80`).
- Contributing docs mismatch on story location:
- Contribution guide says add stories under `apps/storybook/src/stories/` (`CONTRIBUTING.md:63`).
- Storybook config actually sources component stories from `packages/core/stories/**/*.stories.ts` (`apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:12`).
- Secondary `out/` artifacts are stale versus live tree:
- `out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md` reports static fallback counts (e.g., “40 components”, “80 test files”) (`out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9`, `out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:17`, `out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:75`), while current source has materially higher component/test volume.

## Overall Assessment
`dzup-ui` is currently a production-leaning, high-discipline component-platform monorepo with strong CI/release guardrails, substantial test coverage modes, and serious package-boundary/tooling rigor. The highest risk is not implementation immaturity; it is contract/documentation drift across README/changelog/architecture notes/Nuxt registry and static analysis artifacts. In practical terms, maintainers appear to be shipping real code faster than they are keeping all descriptive and integration metadata synchronized.
