name: CI

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  # G0: PR Gates (blocking)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  typecheck:
    name: Typecheck
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Typecheck (all packages)
        run: yarn typecheck:all

  lint:
    name: Lint
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: ESLint
        run: yarn lint

  test:
    name: Unit Tests (Node ${{ matrix.node-version }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [20, 22]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Run tests
        run: yarn test
      - name: Run contract tests
        run: yarn test:contracts
      - name: Run a11y tests
        run: yarn test:a11y
      - name: Run SSR smoke tests
        run: yarn test:ssr

  validate:
    name: Validate (boundaries + tokens)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Import boundary validation
        run: yarn validate:boundaries
      - name: Token compliance check
        run: yarn validate:tokens
      - name: Export map validation
        run: yarn validate:exports
      - name: Token build artifacts unchanged
        run: |
          yarn tokens:generate
          git diff --exit-code packages/tokens/dist/
      - name: Changelog validation
        run: yarn validate:changelog
      - name: Peer dependency validation
        run: yarn validate:peers
      - name: License audit
        run: yarn validate:licenses

  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
  # G1: Merge Gates (main branch)
  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────

  build:
    name: Build (library mode)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [typecheck, lint, test, validate]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Build all packages
        run: yarn build
      - name: Verify ESM-only output (no CJS)
        run: |
          # ADR-11: ESM-only distribution
          if find packages/core/dist -name "*.cjs" -o -name "*.cjs.js" | grep -q .; then
            echo "ERROR: CJS output detected — ADR-11 violation"
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "ESM-only output verified"
      - name: Verify dist artifacts exist
        run: |
          test -f packages/contracts/dist/index.js || (echo "contracts/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
          test -f packages/tokens/dist/index.js || (echo "tokens/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
          test -f packages/core/dist/index.js || (echo "core/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
          test -f packages/compat/dist/index.js || (echo "compat/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
          test -f packages/codemods/dist/index.js || (echo "codemods/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
          test -f packages/nuxt/dist/module.js || (echo "nuxt/dist/module.js missing" && exit 1)
          echo "Build artifacts verified"
      - name: Validate type declarations
        run: yarn validate:dts
      - name: Bundle budget check
        run: yarn validate:bundle
      - name: Bundle budget check (CI report)
        run: npx tsx packages/tooling/src/bundle-budget-check.ts --ci
      - name: Per-component size report
        run: npx tsx packages/tooling/src/component-size-report.ts --top=30
      - name: Pack smoke test (all publishable packages)
        run: |
          for pkg in contracts tokens core compat codemods nuxt; do
            echo "--- packing packages/$pkg ---"
            (cd packages/$pkg && npm pack --dry-run 2>&1) | tee /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt
            if grep -q "link:" /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt; then
              echo "ERROR: link: dependency in packages/$pkg tarball"
              exit 1
            fi
            if grep -q "workspace:" /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt; then
              echo "ERROR: workspace: dependency in packages/$pkg tarball"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
          echo "Pack smoke test passed for all packages"
      - name: Upload build artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: dist
          path: |
            packages/core/dist/
          retention-days: 7

  storybook:
    name: Storybook Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [typecheck]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Build Storybook
        run: yarn storybook:build
      - name: Upload Storybook
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: storybook
          path: apps/storybook/storybook-static/
          retention-days: 7

  e2e:
    name: E2E Tests (Playwright)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [typecheck]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Install Playwright browsers
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Run E2E tests (Chromium)
        run: yarn test:e2e --project=chromium
      - name: Upload Playwright report
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 7

  coverage:
    name: Test Coverage
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test]
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20

import { resolve } from 'node:path'
import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import { defineConfig } from 'vite'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [tailwindcss(), vue()],
  resolve: {
    alias: [
      // Token sub-path exports must come before the base alias
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/css', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/dist/tokens.css') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/tailwind', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/dist/tailwind-theme.js') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/utils', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/src/utils/index.ts') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/src') },
      // Other workspace packages
      { find: '@dzup-ui/core/styles', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/core/src/styles/base.css') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/contracts', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/contracts/src/index.ts') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/core', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/core/src') },
    ],
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000,
    open: true,
  },
})

import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'
import { resolve } from 'node:path'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue(), tailwindcss()],
  resolve: {

      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/css', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/dist/tokens.css') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/tailwind', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/dist/tailwind-theme.js') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens/utils', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/src/utils/index.ts') },
      { find: '@dzup-ui/tokens', replacement: resolve(__dirname, '../../packages/tokens/src') },
      // Other workspace packages

import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import { resolve } from 'node:path'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [vue()],
  resolve: {
    alias: {
      '@dzup-ui/tokens': resolve(__dirname, 'packages/tokens/src'),
      '@dzup-ui/contracts': resolve(__dirname, 'packages/contracts/src'),
      '@dzup-ui/core': resolve(__dirname, 'packages/core/src'),
      '@dzup-ui/compat': resolve(__dirname, 'packages/compat/src'),
      '@dzup-ui/tooling': resolve(__dirname, 'packages/tooling/src'),
    },
  },
  test: {
    globals: true,
    environment: 'jsdom',
    setupFiles: ['./vitest.setup.ts', './vitest.setup.a11y.ts'],
    include: [
      'packages/*/src/**/*.spec.ts',
      'packages/*/tests/**/*.spec.ts',
    ],
    exclude: [
      '**/node_modules/**',
      '**/dist/**',
    ],
    coverage: {
      provider: 'v8',
      reporter: ['text', 'json', 'html', 'lcov'],
      include: ['packages/*/src/**/*.{ts,vue}'],
      exclude: [
        '**/*.spec.ts',
        '**/*.contract.spec.ts',
        '**/*.a11y.spec.ts',
        '**/*.stories.ts',
        '**/*.types.ts',
        '**/*.tokens.ts',
        '**/index.ts',
        '**/dist/**',
        '**/env.d.ts',
        '**/generate.ts',
      ],
      thresholds: {
        branches: 80,
        functions: 80,
        lines: 80,
        statements: 80,
      },
    },
  },
})

exec
/bin/bash -lc "node - <<'NODE'
const fs=require('fs');
const path=require('path');
const root='/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui';
const moduleSrc=fs.readFileSync(path.join(root,'packages/nuxt/src/module.ts'),'utf8');
function parseArray(src,name){
  const re=new RegExp("'`const'"\\\\s+"'${name}'"\\\\s*=\\\\s*\\\\[(.*?)\\\\]\\\\s+as const"'`,'"'s');
  const m=src.match(re); if("'!m) return [];
  return [...m[1].matchAll(/'"'(["'^'"']+)'/g)].map(x=>x[1]);
}
const nuxtCore=parseArray(moduleSrc,'CORE_COMPONENTS');
const nuxtPro=parseArray(moduleSrc,'PRO_COMPONENTS');
const manifest=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path.join(root,'packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json'),'utf8'));
const coreComps=[];
for(const fam of Object.values(manifest.exports.components||{})){
  for(const e of fam.exports||[]){if(/"'^Dz[A-Z]/.test(e)) coreComps.push(e);}
}
const coreSet=new Set(coreComps);
const nuxtSet=new Set(nuxtCore);
const missingInCore=[...nuxtSet].filter(x=>!coreSet.has(x)).sort();
const notInNuxt=[...coreSet].filter(x=>!nuxtSet.has(x)).sort();
console.log('"'nuxt core count',nuxtCore.length);
console.log('manifest Dz* count',coreComps.length);
console.log('Nuxt names missing in manifest/core:',missingInCore.length);
console.log(missingInCore.join('\\n'));
console.log('Manifest/core names missing in Nuxt:',notInNuxt.length);
console.log(notInNuxt.slice(0,80).join('\\n'));
console.log('--- PRO count in nuxt',nuxtPro.length);

/**
 * Theme Initialization Script (FOUC Prevention)
 *
 * This script must be placed in the `<head>` of the HTML document
 * BEFORE any content renders. It reads the user's theme preference
 * from localStorage and sets the `data-theme` attribute on `<html>`
 * immediately, preventing a flash of unstyled/wrong-theme content.
 *
 * ADR-15: Theme persistence and FOUC prevention.
 *
 * Usage:
 *   import { themeScript } from '@dzup-ui/tokens/utils'
 *
 *   // In your HTML template or SSR head:
 *   `<script>${themeScript}</script>`
 */

/**
 * Inline script string for `<head>`.
 * Reads `localStorage('dz-theme')`, resolves 'system' to actual preference,
 * and sets `data-theme` on `document.documentElement`.
 */
export const themeScript = `(function(){try{var t=localStorage.getItem('dz-theme');if(t==='light'||t==='dark'){document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme',t)}else{var d=window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches?'dark':'light';document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme',d)}}catch(e){}})();`

/**
 * The localStorage key used for theme persistence.
 * Must match the key used in DzThemeProvider.
 */
export const THEME_STORAGE_KEY = 'dz-theme'
/**
 * Token Utilities
 */

export { THEME_STORAGE_KEY, themeScript } from './theme-script.js'
/**
 * Providers — Public exports.
 *
 * @module @dzup-ui/core/providers
 */

export { DZ_THEME_KEY } from './DzThemeProvider.types.ts'
export type {
  DzThemeContext,
  DzThemeProviderProps,
  ResolvedTheme,
  ThemePreference,
} from './DzThemeProvider.types.ts'
// DzThemeProvider
export { default as DzThemeProvider } from './DzThemeProvider.vue'
export { getThemeScript, themeScript } from './theme-script.ts'
export type { ThemeScriptOptions } from './theme-script.ts'
export { useTheme } from './useTheme.ts'

# 03 Architecture Review

## Repository Overview
`dzup-ui` is a Yarn 4 workspace monorepo with 7 packages and 2 internal apps, organized as a UI platform rather than a single application runtime. The architecture centers on a layered package model:

- `@dzup-ui/core`: runtime component/composable/provider layer (largest surface, 160 Vue components, 17 composable groups).
- `@dzup-ui/tokens`: design-token source plus generation pipeline for CSS and TypeScript artifacts.
- `@dzup-ui/contracts`: canonical type contracts for props/events/slots and shared utility types.
- `@dzup-ui/compat`: migration adapters and deprecation utilities.
- `@dzup-ui/codemods`: source-level migration runner and transform registry.
- `@dzup-ui/nuxt`: framework integration package for registration and bootstrap.
- `apps/sandbox` and `apps/storybook`: local UI harnesses for manual validation and docs.

This is architecturally a package ecosystem with adapters and tooling, not a business app with server-side domains.

## Architectural Overview
The high-level design is a clean “platform + adapters + tooling” split:

- Platform core:
  UI primitives and compound components live in `core`, with composables orchestrating state transitions and providers handling cross-tree concerns (theme/context).
- Styling system:
  `tokens` generates CSS variable layers and Tailwind-compatible theme outputs; `core` consumes these through CSS imports and variant systems.
- Type system:
  `contracts` provides shared API vocabulary to keep component signatures consistent.
- Migration system:
  `compat` handles runtime backward compatibility while `codemods` handles source migration.
- Integration system:
  `nuxt` and resolver APIs provide framework-level auto-import and bootstrap extension points.
- Runtime harnesses:
  `sandbox` route wiring and Storybook story wiring form the primary request/UI flow for local interaction and visual verification.

Main UI/event flow boundaries are:

- Consumer flow:
  app entry -> component import/auto-resolve -> component local/composable state -> token-driven CSS resolution.
- Compound component flow:
  root component -> `provide` context -> child part `inject` -> coordinated behavior and slot rendering.
- Theme flow:
  head script bootstrap -> provider state resolution -> DOM theme attribute -> token layer activation.
- Dev/demo flow:
  route family pages / Storybook stories -> same package runtime surfaces as external consumers.

## Strong Architectural Areas
- Package segmentation is intentional and mostly coherent:
  foundational runtime (`core`), design system (`tokens`), types (`contracts`), migration (`compat` + `codemods`), and framework integration (`nuxt`) are distinct units.
- Manifest-driven export management in `core` is a strong architecture choice:
  it reduces manual barrel drift and keeps public API composition systematic.
- Compound component patterns are consistently implemented:
  typed context keys and `provide/inject` usage produce repeatable internal contracts across data/navigation/overlay families.
- State-management shape fits a component library:
  local refs/computed/watch and composables dominate; there is no unnecessary global store coupling.
- Theming architecture is well-structured:
  token tiers, provider APIs, and first-paint script support are clearly separated concerns.
- Validation architecture is mature:
  custom boundary, export, declaration, peer, and license checks create strong non-functional guardrails around the codebase.

## Architectural Tensions
- Domain leakage inside the foundational layer:
  orchestration-specific run-status/governance concerns and external orchestration typing are present in `core`, which weakens “framework-agnostic UI foundation” boundaries.
- Registry drift risk in integration:
  Nuxt uses a manually maintained component registry that is currently out of sync with core manifest reality (hard mismatch, not just naming style differences).
- Source-of-truth duplication for theme bootstrap:
  similar theme-init logic exists in multiple places (tokens utility, core provider utility, Nuxt inline script), increasing drift probability.
- Layer inversion between composables and component-local types:
  several composables import types from component folders, creating reverse dependency pressure and tighter coupling than necessary.
- Hotspot concentration:
  a small set of very large modules/composables now carries disproportionate complexity, especially advanced data and overlay behaviors.
- Configuration duplication:
  workspace alias mapping is repeated across sandbox, Storybook, and test config, creating maintenance overhead and possible environment skew.
- Tooling policy gaps:
  boundary validation policy coverage does not fully include all integration surfaces, leaving enforcement asymmetry at package edges.
- Observability drift in secondary artifacts:
  `out/` summaries undercount current component/test scale, reducing trust in generated architecture telemetry.

## Domain Boundary Review
Responsibility separation is good at package level, but several boundaries should be tightened.

- Apps vs packages:
  `sandbox` and Storybook are correctly local harnesses; routing, demo navigation, and showcase-only metadata should remain app-local.
- Core vs shared packages:
  `core` should remain domain-neutral component runtime; `tokens` and `contracts` are correctly positioned as shared foundational packages.
- Integration boundary:
  `nuxt` should consume canonical component metadata, not maintain an independent inventory list.
- Migration boundary:
  `compat` and `codemods` are correctly isolated as transitional layers and should not become long-term homes for new feature development.
- State boundary:
  current architecture favors component/composable-local state with scoped context sharing; this is the right shape for a reusable UI library.
- Shared logic candidates:
  component registry metadata and theme bootstrap script generation should be centralized and consumed by all adapters.
- Extraction candidates:
  orchestration/agent-specific UI primitives should move to an optional extension package to preserve clean domain boundaries in `core`.

## Operational Architecture Review
- Queues/workers:
  no runtime queue/worker subsystem exists, which is appropriate for this repository type.
- Feature flags and toggles:
  extension toggles are lightweight and package-scoped (`includePro`, naming prefix), not a global flag framework.
- Persistence boundaries:
  runtime persistence is intentionally minimal and client-only (theme/sidebar preferences via browser storage).
- Observability hooks:
  mostly local console warnings and test assertions; there is no cross-package runtime telemetry abstraction.
- Error handling:
  browser-API interactions are generally wrapped defensively with graceful fallback behavior.
- Failure isolation:
  strongest isolation is at build/CI stage boundaries, not runtime process isolation.
- Integration weak point:
  Nuxt registration and theme bootstrap wiring are the most fragile operational boundary due to manual lists and duplicated bootstrap logic.

## Structural Recommendations
| Priority | Recommendation | Leverage | Implementation Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Replace manual integration registries with one generated component-inventory artifact consumed by `core`, `nuxt`, and docs/harnesses. | Very high | Medium |
| 2 | Extract orchestration-specific badges/types from `core` into an optional extension package (keep core domain-neutral). | High | Medium |
| 3 | Consolidate theme bootstrap to a single generator API, and make Nuxt/tokens wrappers consume that source. | High | Low |
| 4 | Introduce an internal shared type layer for composables so composables no longer depend on component-local type modules. | High | Medium |
| 5 | Expand architecture policy gates to full package surface parity (including all integration adapters) and close validator coverage gaps. | High | Low |
| 6 | Split large hotspot modules into state, adapter, and render-focused slices with explicit internal contracts. | Medium | Medium |
| 7 | Centralize alias/config composition used by sandbox, Storybook, and tests to reduce config drift. | Medium | Low |
| 8 | Add lightweight architecture drift checks against generated metadata (component counts/inventories) to keep docs and secondary artifacts current. | Medium | Low |

## Overall Assessment
The architecture is fundamentally strong and maintainable: package-level layering is clear, composition patterns are consistent, and quality guardrails are above typical UI-library baselines. The main risks are boundary drift and source-of-truth duplication, not missing architectural foundations. With registry unification, domain extraction from `core`, and tighter adapter boundary enforcement, the codebase is architecturally ready for continued growth with manageable refactor cost.

### Final Output
```md
# 04 Security Review

Scope: static security review of `ui/dzup-ui` with emphasis on authn/authz, tenant isolation, secret handling, unsafe input paths, file/path access, SSRF-like fetch paths, webhook exposure, queue/job trust boundaries, config defaults, and sensitive-data exposure.

Assessment summary: no high/critical vulnerability was confirmed in this repository’s own runtime scope. One medium-confidence client-side issue and two low-severity hardening gaps were identified.

## Repository Overview

`dzup-ui` is an open-source Vue 3 component-library monorepo (`core`, `tokens`, `contracts`, `compat`, `codemods`, `nuxt`) rather than an application backend or API service ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3), [README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:53)).  
The repository summary in `out/` also classifies it as a UI repo/package set, with Storybook and sandbox demo surfaces ([ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:1)).  
This matters for security interpretation: most real authn/authz and tenant isolation controls are expected in consuming apps/services, not in this library itself.

## Trust Boundaries

- Users: end users interact with downstream apps that render these components; this repo does not run the app auth boundary directly ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3)).
- Tenants: no tenant model or tenant-scoped data model exists in this codebase.
- Admins: no admin policy engine or privileged route guard subsystem exists in this repo.
- External providers: npm publish and GitHub release automation rely on GitHub Actions + npm registry credentials (`NPM_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`) ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:51), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:57)).
- Webhooks: no inbound webhook handlers were found in runtime packages.
- Background jobs/queues: no queue worker/job processor surface was found in runtime packages.
- Storage: browser `localStorage` is used for UI preference persistence (theme/sidebar), not session credentials ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:65)).
- Internal services: no service-to-service calls or backend client layer is present in runtime component code.

## Security Strengths

- Theme persistence is allowlisted to expected values (`light|dark|system`) before use, reducing storage-tampering impact ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:45)).
- Theme bootstrap string generation uses `JSON.stringify` for user-configurable fields, which reduces script-construction injection risk in that helper ([theme-script.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:52)).
- CI uses immutable install mode (`yarn install --immutable`), reducing lockfile drift and accidental dependency mutation during pipeline runs ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:37)).
- Secret-hygiene workflow exists (`scan:credentials`) and includes working-tree + git-history scanning patterns ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:50), [credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:53)).
- Private vulnerability disclosure policy is documented ([SECURITY.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/SECURITY.md:11)).

## Findings

### SR-01 (Medium): Unvalidated navigable URL props are forwarded to anchor navigation sinks

- Impact: if consuming applications bind attacker-controlled URLs to `href`/string `to`, this can enable client-side XSS via `javascript:` URIs or malicious redirect/navigation.
- Evidence: direct sink forwarding appears in [DzButton.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/buttons/DzButton.vue:126), [DzMenuItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenuItem.vue:49), [DzBreadcrumbItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzBreadcrumbItem.vue:49), and [DzSidebarItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebarItem.vue:83). Related prop types are unconstrained `string` ([DzButton.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/buttons/DzButton.types.ts:47), [DzMenu.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenu.types.ts:56), [DzBreadcrumb.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzBreadcrumb.types.ts:50), [DzSidebar.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebar.types.ts:80)).
- Attack path: attacker controls URL-like content (CMS/profile/link field) -> downstream app maps it into these props -> user clicks rendered element -> browser navigates/executes payload.
- Remediation: introduce a shared URL sanitizer/validator for all navigable props with explicit allowlist (`http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, safe relative paths), reject active/mixed schemes (`javascript:`, `data:`), and add contract tests with obfuscated payload variants.

### SR-02 (Low): Nuxt integration injects inline script with `innerHTML` by default, pressuring CSP relaxation

- Impact: consumers with strict CSP may weaken policy to keep theme pre-init behavior, increasing exploitability of unrelated DOM injection bugs in downstream apps.
- Evidence: Nuxt module pushes inline head script via `innerHTML` with no security toggle/nonce integration in options ([module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:3), [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:273), [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:275)).
- Attack path: downstream app allows `'unsafe-inline'` to accommodate module behavior -> unrelated script injection elsewhere becomes materially easier to weaponize.
- Remediation: add module options to disable inline injection and/or support nonce/hash-based CSP-safe mode; provide external-script alternative and explicit CSP guidance.

### SR-03 (Low): CI/release workflows use third-party GitHub Actions by mutable major tags instead of immutable SHAs

- Impact: compromised upstream action release or tag hijack increases supply-chain risk in CI/release pipeline.
- Evidence: workflows use `@v4`/`@v1` tag pins for actions (for example `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `changesets/action@v1`) ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:22), [release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:20), [release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:53)).
- Attack path: malicious or compromised upstream action update under same major tag -> pipeline executes unreviewed code with repo and publish credentials.
- Remediation: pin critical actions to full commit SHAs and add periodic update workflow for controlled upgrades.

## Sensitive Data And Secrets Review

- No hard-coded high-confidence credentials were identified in repository source by targeted static pattern review (excluding dependencies/build artifacts).
- Publish credentials are referenced through GitHub Secrets and environment injection (`NPM_TOKEN`, `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`), not plaintext commits ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:60), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:61)).
- Runtime preference persistence uses `localStorage` for theme/sidebar state; no session token persistence path was found in core runtime code ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:69)).
- No `document.cookie` handling, auth header construction, or session/csrf token management was found in runtime packages.
- Secret scanning exists as a script but is not currently visible as a mandatory CI gate in the reviewed workflows ([credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:53), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:13)).

## Authorization And Isolation Review

- No route guards, login/session middleware, RBAC/ABAC framework, or server-side authorization enforcement is implemented in this repo’s runtime surface.
- No tenant/team isolation logic exists in package runtime code; multi-tenant separation is delegated to consuming applications/services.
- Component states such as `disabled`, `active`, or role labels are presentation semantics, not authorization controls.
- No public sharing endpoints, webhook ingest routes, or privileged admin API surfaces were found in the repository runtime code.
- Conclusion: authn/authz and tenant isolation are out-of-scope by architecture for this repo; security contract should explicitly state these responsibilities for integrators.

## Residual Risks

- Primary residual risk is downstream misuse: consumers may bind untrusted URL/content into component props without validation, creating XSS/open-redirect conditions in host apps.
- CSP and security-header posture are mostly downstream app/server concerns; this repo cannot guarantee safe defaults at deployment perimeter.
- Release pipeline supply-chain exposure remains non-zero while actions are major-tag pinned and credential scanning is optional rather than enforced.
- Demo/storybook surfaces include external image URLs; while not a direct vulnerability here, they create outbound request behavior in preview environments.

## Recommended Remediation Plan

1. Immediate: implement `sanitizeNavigableUrl` in `@dzup-ui/core` and apply it consistently to all `href`/string `to` sinks before DOM binding.
2. Immediate: add security-focused tests for malicious URI schemes and obfuscation variants across `DzButton`, `DzMenuItem`, `DzBreadcrumbItem`, and `DzSidebarItem`.
3. Immediate: document a clear consumer security contract in repo docs: authn/authz/tenant enforcement is app-owned, and navigable props must be treated as untrusted input.
4. Short-term: extend `@dzup-ui/nuxt` options for CSP-safe theme bootstrap (disable inline mode, nonce/hash support, external script strategy).
5. Short-term: make secret scanning and dependency/audit checks first-class CI gates, not just local scripts.
6. Short-term: pin critical GitHub Actions to immutable SHAs and enable controlled update cadence.
7. Structural: add a lightweight security checklist to PR/release workflow covering URL sanitization, CSP compatibility, and supply-chain hardening controls.
```

{
  "name": "dzup-ui",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "packageManager": "yarn@4.6.0",
  "engines": {
    "node": ">=20.0.0"
  },
  "workspaces": [
    "packages/*",
    "apps/*"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "typecheck": "vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/core/tsconfig.json",
    "typecheck:all": "tsc --noEmit -p packages/tokens/tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p packages/contracts/tsconfig.json && vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/core/tsconfig.json && vue-tsc --noEmit -p packages/compat/tsconfig.json && tsc --noEmit -p packages/codemods/tsconfig.json",
    "lint": "eslint packages/",
    "lint:fix": "eslint packages/ --fix",
    "test": "vitest run",
    "test:watch": "vitest",
    "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
    "test:a11y": "vitest run --reporter=verbose packages/**/a11y/**/*.a11y.spec.ts",
    "test:contracts": "vitest run --reporter=verbose packages/**/*.contract.spec.ts",
    "test:ssr": "vitest run --reporter=verbose packages/**/ssr/**/*.spec.ts",
    "build": "yarn workspace @dzup-ui/contracts build && yarn workspace @dzup-ui/tokens build && yarn workspace @dzup-ui/core build && yarn workspace @dzup-ui/compat build && yarn workspace @dzup-ui/codemods build && yarn workspace @dzup-ui/nuxt build",
    "build:all": "yarn workspaces foreach -Apt run build",
    "tokens:generate": "yarn workspace @dzup-ui/tokens generate",
    "generate:exports": "tsx packages/tooling/src/manifest-generator.ts",

# 04 Security Review

Scope: static review of `ui/dzup-ui` (component-library monorepo) with emphasis on authn/authz, isolation, secret handling, unsafe input paths, SSRF-like fetch paths, webhook/job boundaries, config defaults, and sensitive-data exposure.

No critical or backend-compromise-class vulnerability was identified in this repository. The codebase is primarily a frontend UI library, with no built-in server API/webhook/queue plane in this repo context.

## Trust Boundaries

- Users: end users interact with consuming applications that render these components; this repo itself is a reusable UI library, not an app auth boundary ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3), [README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:57)).
- Tenants: no tenant model or tenant-scoped data enforcement exists in this repo; tenancy is delegated to consuming apps/services.
- Admins: no admin-role enforcement layer exists in this repo; “disabled/active” UI state is presentation only.
- External providers: package publishing and CI rely on GitHub Actions and npm credentials (`NPM_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`) ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:60), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:61)).
- Webhooks: none found in runtime code paths.
- Background jobs/queues: none found; this repo has no queue worker subsystem in source.
- Storage: browser local storage is used for UI preferences (theme/sidebar), not sessions (`dz-theme` and optional sidebar key) ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:69)).
- Internal services: no internal service-to-service calls in this repo’s runtime packages; repo classification artifacts also mark it as a UI package set ([ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:1)).

## Security Strengths

- Theme persistence handling validates allowed values (`light|dark|system`) before applying persisted state, reducing storage-tampering impact ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:45)).
- Theme bootstrap generator uses `JSON.stringify` for injected values, preventing direct script-string injection when custom options are supplied ([theme-script.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:52)).
- Shared disabled-state CSS hardening includes `pointer-events: none` for disabled controls/buttons, reducing accidental interaction bypasses in many components ([base.css](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/styles/base.css:122), [base.css](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/styles/base.css:128)).
- Secret hygiene process exists (`scan:credentials`) with history + working-tree pattern scanning and optional gitleaks integration ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:50), [credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:28)).
- Security disclosure process is documented with private reporting guidance ([SECURITY.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/SECURITY.md:11)).
- CI uses immutable dependency installation (`yarn install --immutable`) across gates, reducing lockfile drift/supply surprises during verification ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:37)).

## Findings

### SR-01 (Medium): Link-like components forward unvalidated URL schemes (`href`/string `to`) to DOM navigation sinks

- Impact: if a consuming app passes attacker-influenced URLs into these props, `javascript:` payloads can execute on click in the app origin (client-side XSS), or users can be redirected to unsafe destinations.
- Evidence: direct pass-through to anchor `href` appears in [DzButton.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/buttons/DzButton.vue:126), [DzMenuItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenuItem.vue:49), [DzBreadcrumbItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzBreadcrumbItem.vue:49), and [DzSidebarItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebarItem.vue:83). The related props are typed as unconstrained `string` ([DzMenu.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenu.types.ts:56), [DzSidebar.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebar.types.ts:80)).
- Attack path: attacker stores a crafted URL in CMS/profile/user-generated content; app binds it into a `Dz*` link prop; user clicks; browser executes/opens attacker-controlled destination.
- Remediation: introduce a shared URL sanitizer/validator in `core` for all navigable props, allowlisting `http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, and safe relative URLs; reject `javascript:`/`data:`/other active schemes; add contract tests specifically for malicious scheme inputs.

### SR-02 (Low): Nuxt module unconditionally injects inline script via `innerHTML`, creating CSP hardening pressure

- Impact: teams with strict CSP may be pushed toward weaker policies to keep theme-bootstrapping functional; this increases blast radius if any DOM XSS appears elsewhere in the consumer app.
- Evidence: module options do not expose a CSP-safe toggle for this behavior ([module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:3)); setup always appends inline script via `innerHTML` ([module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:273), [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:275)).
- Attack path: downstream app relaxes CSP (for inline execution compatibility), then unrelated client-side injection in that app becomes easier to exploit.
- Remediation: add module option to disable inline script injection, provide nonce/hash-compatible integration guidance, and/or expose a prebuilt external script asset path for CSP-strict deployments.

## Sensitive Data And Secrets Review

- No hard-coded high-confidence credentials were found by targeted pattern scan in source (excluding build artifacts and dependencies).
- Release credentials are sourced from GitHub Secrets (`NPM_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`) rather than committed plaintext ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:60)).
- Runtime persistence in this repo is preference-only local storage (`dz-theme`, optional sidebar collapse key), not auth/session token storage ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:69)).
- No cookie/session handling (`document.cookie`, CSRF token handling, `Authorization` header flows) was found in runtime package source.
- Secret-scanning automation exists and should remain a pre-release gate ([credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:54)).

## Authorization And Isolation Review

- No route guard/auth middleware exists in this repo because it is a component platform, not an application backend/auth service ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3)).
- No tenant/team isolation logic is present; tenant boundaries must be implemented by consuming services/apps.
- “Disabled”, “active”, and similar visual states in components are UX affordances, not authorization controls.
- No public sharing endpoint/webhook API surface exists in this repository.
- Storybook/sandbox are demo surfaces and should not be treated as privileged admin boundaries in downstream deployments.

## Residual Risks

- Consumer misuse risk remains significant: because several components accept raw navigable URL strings, downstream apps can accidentally create XSS/open-redirect conditions if they bind untrusted content directly.
- CSP and browser security header posture cannot be fully verified from this repo alone; those controls usually live at app/server/CDN layer.
- Supply-chain risk remains partly external: CI uses third-party actions by major-version tag rather than immutable commit SHA pinning.
- No dedicated dependency-vulnerability job is visible in CI (`npm audit`/OSV/Dependabot policy check lane), so package-level CVE detection relies on external processes.

## Recommended Remediation Plan

1. Immediate: implement a central `sanitizeNavigableUrl` helper in `@dzup-ui/core` and apply it to all `href`/string `to` sinks before DOM binding.
2. Immediate: add unit/contract tests covering malicious schemes (`javascript:`, `data:`, mixed-case/whitespace-obfuscated variants) for `DzButton`, `DzMenuItem`, `DzBreadcrumbItem`, and `DzSidebarItem`.
3. Short-term: add Nuxt module options for theme script injection control (`injectThemeScript`, optional nonce/hash metadata), and document CSP-safe integration patterns.
4. Short-term: add explicit CI security lanes for dependency vulnerability review and action hardening checks.
5. Structural: publish a “Security Contract for Consumers” doc that states authn/authz/tenant enforcement is app-owned and that URL-like props must be treated as untrusted input by default.
6. Structural: integrate credential scan + security checks into a single release gate so security hygiene is enforced consistently before publish.

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Theme initialization script for FOUC prevention (ADR-15).
     3	 *
     4	 * This string contains inline JavaScript meant to be injected into the
     5	 * `<head>` of the HTML document BEFORE any rendering occurs. It reads the
     6	 * persisted theme from `localStorage` and sets the `data-theme` attribute
     7	 * on `<html>` so that the correct CSS variables are active on first paint.
     8	 *
     9	 * Usage in an HTML template or SSR framework:
    10	 *
    11	 * ``\`html
    12	 * <head>
    13	 *   <script>{{ themeScript }}</script>
    14	 * </head>
    15	 * ``\`
    16	 *
    17	 * Or with a bundler / SSR helper:
    18	 *
    19	 * ``\`ts
    20	 * import { getThemeScript } from '@dzup-ui/core/providers'
    21	 *
    22	 * // Custom storage key and attribute
    23	 * const script = getThemeScript({ storageKey: 'my-theme', attribute: 'data-mode' })
    24	 * ``\`
    25	 *
    26	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/providers/theme-script
    27	 */
    28	
    29	/** Options for generating the theme initialization script */
    30	export interface ThemeScriptOptions {
    31	  /** localStorage key (default: 'dz-theme') */
    32	  storageKey?: string
    33	  /** HTML attribute name (default: 'data-theme') */
    34	  attribute?: string
    35	  /** Default theme when nothing is persisted (default: 'system') */
    36	  defaultTheme?: 'light' | 'dark' | 'system'
    37	}
    38	
    39	/**
    40	 * Generate a theme initialization script string with custom options.
    41	 *
    42	 * @param options - Script generation options
    43	 * @returns Inline JavaScript string for `<head>` injection
    44	 */
    45	export function getThemeScript(options: ThemeScriptOptions = {}): string {
    46	  const {
    47	    storageKey = 'dz-theme',
    48	    attribute = 'data-theme',
    49	    defaultTheme = 'system',
    50	  } = options
    51	
    52	  return `(function(){try{var s=localStorage.getItem(${JSON.stringify(storageKey)});var t=s==='light'||s==='dark'?s:s==='system'||!s?null:null;if(!t){var d=${JSON.stringify(defaultTheme)};t=d==='system'?window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme:dark)').matches?'dark':'light':d}document.documentElement.setAttribute(${JSON.stringify(attribute)},t)}catch(e){}})()`
    53	}
    54	
    55	/**
    56	 * Pre-built inline script using default options (ADR-15).
    57	 *
    58	 * Reads `localStorage` key `dz-theme` and sets `data-theme` on `<html>`.
    59	 * Falls back to system preference detection via `matchMedia`.
    60	 *
    61	 * Inject in `<head>` before any stylesheets or body content:
    62	 *
    63	 * ``\`html
    64	 * <script>{{ themeScript }}</script>
    65	 * ``\`
    66	 */
    67	export const themeScript: string = getThemeScript()

     1	<script setup lang="ts">
     2	import type { DzButtonEmits, DzButtonProps, DzButtonSlots } from './DzButton.types.ts'
     3	/**
     4	 * DzButton — Primary button component.
     5	 *
     6	 * Supports five variants (solid, outline, ghost, text, link),
     7	 * six semantic tones, five sizes, loading/disabled states,
     8	 * and prefix/suffix icon slots.
     9	 *
    10	 * @example
    11	 * ``\`vue
    12	 * <DzButton tone="primary" @click="handleClick">Save</DzButton>
    13	 * <DzButton variant="outline" size="sm" loading>Processing</DzButton>
    14	 * ``\`
    15	 */
    16	import { computed, getCurrentInstance, inject, isProxy, markRaw, toRaw, useAttrs } from 'vue'
    17	import { cn } from '../../utilities/cn.ts'
    18	import { buttonVariants } from './DzButton.variants.ts'
    19	import { DZ_BUTTON_GROUP_KEY } from './DzButtonGroup.types.ts'
    20	
    21	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzButtonProps>(), {
    22	  variant: undefined,
    23	  size: undefined,
    24	  tone: undefined,
    25	  disabled: false,
    26	  loading: false,
    27	  type: 'button',
    28	  asChild: false,
    29	  as: undefined,
    30	  href: undefined,
    31	  to: undefined,
    32	})
    33	
    34	const emit = defineEmits<DzButtonEmits>()
    35	defineSlots<DzButtonSlots>()
    36	
    37	const attrs = useAttrs()
    38	const groupContext = inject(DZ_BUTTON_GROUP_KEY, null)
    39	const instance = getCurrentInstance()
    40	
    41	/** Resolved size: prop wins, then group context, then default */
    42	const resolvedSize = computed(() => props.size ?? groupContext?.size.value ?? 'md')
    43	
    44	/** Resolved variant: prop wins, then group context, then default */
    45	const resolvedVariant = computed(() => props.variant ?? groupContext?.variant.value ?? 'solid')
    46	
    47	/** Resolved tone: prop wins, then group context, then default */
    48	const resolvedTone = computed(() => props.tone ?? groupContext?.tone.value ?? 'primary')
    49	
    50	/** Resolved disabled: own prop OR group-level disabled */
    51	const resolvedDisabled = computed(() => props.disabled || (groupContext?.disabled.value ?? false))
    52	
    53	/** Whether interaction is blocked */
    54	const isInert = computed(() => resolvedDisabled.value || props.loading)
    55	
    56	/**
    57	 * Resolved root element/component for polymorphic rendering.
    58	 * Priority: explicit `as` > `href` (renders <a>) > `to` (renders router-link) > 'button'
    59	 */
    60	const computedTag = computed(() => {
    61	  if (props.as) {
    62	    if (typeof props.as === 'object' && props.as !== null) {
    63	      const component = isProxy(props.as) ? toRaw(props.as) : props.as
    64	      return markRaw(component)
    65	    }
    66	    return props.as
    67	  }
    68	  if (props.href)
    69	    return 'a'
    70	  if (props.to) {
    71	    const routerLink = instance?.appContext.components.RouterLink
    72	    return routerLink ? markRaw(routerLink) : 'a'
    73	  }
    74	  return 'button'
    75	})
    76	
    77	/** Whether the resolved tag is a native anchor element */
    78	const isAnchor = computed(() => computedTag.value === 'a')
    79	
    80	/** Whether the resolved tag is a native button element */
    81	const isButton = computed(() => computedTag.value === 'button')
    82	
    83	/** Merged class string using cn() (ADR-10) */
    84	const classes = computed(() =>
    85	  cn(
    86	    buttonVariants({
    87	      variant: resolvedVariant.value,
    88	      size: resolvedSize.value,
    89	      tone: resolvedTone.value,
    90	    }),
    91	    attrs.class as string | undefined,
    92	  ),
    93	)
    94	
    95	function handleClick(event: MouseEvent): void {
    96	  if (isInert.value) {
    97	    event.preventDefault()
    98	    event.stopPropagation()
    99	    return
   100	  }
   101	  emit('click', event)
   102	}
   103	
   104	function handleFocus(event: FocusEvent): void {
   105	  emit('focus', event)
   106	}
   107	
   108	function handleBlur(event: FocusEvent): void {
   109	  emit('blur', event)
   110	}
   111	</script>
   112	
   113	<script lang="ts">
   114	export default {
   115	  inheritAttrs: false,
   116	}
   117	</script>
   118	
   119	<template>
   120	  <component
   121	    :is="computedTag"
   122	    :id="id"
   123	    :type="isButton ? type : undefined"
   124	    :class="classes"
   125	    :disabled="isButton ? (resolvedDisabled || undefined) : undefined"
   126	    :href="isAnchor && !resolvedDisabled ? (href ?? (to && typeof to === 'string' ? to : undefined)) : undefined"
   127	    :to="!isAnchor && !isButton && to ? to : undefined"
   128	    :role="!isButton ? 'button' : undefined"

   130	    :aria-disabled="isInert || undefined"

   133	    :aria-labelledby="ariaLabelledby"
   134	    :aria-describedby="ariaDescribedby"
   135	    :data-state="loading ? 'loading' : resolvedDisabled ? 'disabled' : 'idle'"
   136	    :data-tone="resolvedTone"
   137	    :data-loading="loading ? '' : undefined"
   138	    :data-disabled="resolvedDisabled ? '' : undefined"
   139	    style="contain: layout style"
   140	    v-bind="{ ...$attrs, class: undefined }"
   141	    @click="handleClick"
   142	    @focus="handleFocus"
   143	    @blur="handleBlur"
   144	  >
   145	    <!-- Loading spinner -->
   146	    <svg
   147	      v-if="loading"

   149	      :class="[
   150	        resolvedSize === 'xs' ? 'h-3 w-3' : '',
   151	        resolvedSize === 'sm' ? 'h-3.5 w-3.5' : '',

   165	        r="10"
   166	        stroke="currentColor"
   167	        stroke-width="4"

   172	        d="M4 12a8 8 0 018-8V0C5.373 0 0 5.373 0 12h4z"
   173	      />
   174	    </svg>
   175	
   176	    <!-- Prefix slot (hidden during loading to avoid double icon) -->
   177	    <slot v-if="!loading" name="prefix" />
   178	
   179	    <!-- Default content -->
   180	    <slot />
   181	
   182	    <!-- Suffix slot -->
   183	    <slot name="suffix" />
   184	  </component>
   185	</template>

     1	<script setup lang="ts">
     2	import type { DzBreadcrumbItemProps, DzBreadcrumbItemSlots } from './DzBreadcrumb.types.ts'
     3	/**
     4	 * DzBreadcrumbItem — Individual breadcrumb entry.
     5	 *
     6	 * Renders as an anchor link when `href` is provided, or a span otherwise.
     7	 * Marks current page with aria-current="page".
     8	 *
     9	 * @example
    10	 * ``\`vue
    11	 * <DzBreadcrumbItem href="/products">Products</DzBreadcrumbItem>
    12	 * <DzBreadcrumbItem current>Current Page</DzBreadcrumbItem>
    13	 * ``\`
    14	 */
    15	import { computed, useAttrs } from 'vue'
    16	import { cn } from '../../utilities/cn.ts'
    17	import { breadcrumbVariants } from './DzBreadcrumb.variants.ts'
    18	
    19	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzBreadcrumbItemProps>(), {
    20	  href: undefined,
    21	  current: false,
    22	  disabled: false,
    23	})
    24	
    25	defineSlots<DzBreadcrumbItemSlots>()
    26	
    27	const attrs = useAttrs()
    28	const styles = breadcrumbVariants()
    29	
    30	const linkClasses = computed(() => {
    31	  const base = props.current ? styles.currentPage() : styles.link()
    32	  const disabled = props.disabled ? styles.disabledLink() : ''
    33	  return cn(base, disabled, attrs.class as string | undefined)
    34	})
    35	
    36	const isLink = computed(() => !!props.href && !props.current && !props.disabled)
    37	</script>
    38	
    39	<script lang="ts">
    40	export default {
    41	  inheritAttrs: false,
    42	}
    43	</script>
    44	
    45	<template>
    46	  <li :class="styles.item()">
    47	    <a
    48	      v-if="isLink"
    49	      :href="href"
    50	      :class="linkClasses"
    51	      v-bind="{ ...$attrs, class: undefined }"
    52	    >
    53	      <slot />
    54	    </a>
    55	    <span
    56	      v-else
    57	      role="link"
    58	      :aria-current="current ? 'page' : undefined"
    59	      :aria-disabled="disabled || undefined"
    60	      :class="linkClasses"
    61	      :data-disabled="disabled ? '' : undefined"
    62	      v-bind="{ ...$attrs, class: undefined }"
    63	    >
    64	      <slot />
    65	    </span>
    66	  </li>
    67	</template>

     1	<script setup lang="ts">
     2	import type { DzSidebarItemEmits, DzSidebarItemProps, DzSidebarItemSlots } from './DzSidebar.types.ts'
     3	/**
     4	 * DzSidebarItem -- Individual navigation item within a sidebar.
     5	 *
     6	 * Supports polymorphic rendering: renders as `<a>` when `href` is provided,
     7	 * as `<RouterLink>` when `to` is provided, or as `<button>` by default.
     8	 * Inherits collapsed state from parent DzSidebar context (ADR-08).
     9	 *
    10	 * @example
    11	 * ``\`vue
    12	 * <DzSidebarItem active>
    13	 *   <template #icon><HomeIcon /></template>
    14	 *   Dashboard
    15	 *   <template #badge><span class="badge">3</span></template>
    16	 * </DzSidebarItem>
    17	 * ``\`
    18	 */
    19	import { computed, inject, resolveComponent, useAttrs } from 'vue'
    20	import { cn } from '../../utilities/cn.ts'
    21	import { DZ_SIDEBAR_KEY } from './DzSidebar.types.ts'
    22	import { sidebarVariants } from './DzSidebar.variants.ts'
    23	
    24	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzSidebarItemProps>(), {
    25	  active: false,
    26	  disabled: false,
    27	  as: undefined,
    28	  href: undefined,
    29	  to: undefined,
    30	  id: undefined,
    31	  ariaLabel: undefined,
    32	  ariaLabelledby: undefined,
    33	  ariaDescribedby: undefined,
    34	  ariaInvalid: undefined,
    35	})
    36	
    37	const emit = defineEmits<DzSidebarItemEmits>()
    38	defineSlots<DzSidebarItemSlots>()
    39	
    40	const attrs = useAttrs()
    41	const sidebarContext = inject(DZ_SIDEBAR_KEY, null)
    42	
    43	const isCollapsed = computed(() => sidebarContext?.collapsed.value ?? false)
    44	
    45	/** Determine which HTML element or component to render */
    46	const computedTag = computed(() => {
    47	  if (props.as)
    48	    return props.as
    49	  if (props.href)
    50	    return 'a'
    51	  if (props.to) {
    52	    try {
    53	      return resolveComponent('RouterLink')
    54	    }
    55	    catch {
    56	      return 'a'
    57	    }
    58	  }
    59	  return 'button'
    60	})
    61	
    62	const styles = computed(() =>
    63	  sidebarVariants({
    64	    collapsed: isCollapsed.value,
    65	  }),
    66	)
    67	
    68	const itemClasses = computed(() =>
    69	  cn(
    70	    styles.value.item(),
    71	    props.active && 'bg-[var(--dz-muted)] text-[var(--dz-foreground)] border-l-[3px] border-l-[var(--dz-primary)] !pl-[calc(var(--dz-spacing-3)-3px)]',
    72	    'dz-disabled-control',
    73	    attrs.class as string | undefined,
    74	  ),
    75	)
    76	
    77	const dataState = computed(() => (props.active ? 'active' : 'inactive'))
    78	
    79	/** Build link-specific attributes */
    80	const linkAttrs = computed(() => {
    81	  const result: Record<string, unknown> = {}
    82	  if (props.href)
    83	    result.href = props.href
    84	  if (props.to)
    85	    result.to = props.to
    86	  return result
    87	})
    88	
    89	function handleClick(event: MouseEvent): void {
    90	  if (props.disabled) {
    91	    event.preventDefault()
    92	    return
    93	  }
    94	  emit('click', event)
    95	}
    96	</script>
    97	
    98	<script lang="ts">
    99	export default {
   100	  inheritAttrs: false,
   101	}
   102	</script>
   103	
   104	<template>
   105	  <component
   106	    :is="computedTag"
   107	    :id="id"
   108	    :class="itemClasses"
   109	    :data-state="dataState"
   110	    :aria-current="active ? 'page' : undefined"
   111	    :aria-label="ariaLabel"
   112	    :aria-disabled="disabled || undefined"
   113	    :disabled="computedTag === 'button' ? disabled : undefined"
   114	    :tabindex="disabled ? -1 : 0"
   115	    v-bind="{ ...$attrs, class: undefined, ...linkAttrs }"
   116	    @click="handleClick"
   117	  >
   118	    <!-- Icon slot: always visible -->
   119	    <span v-if="$slots.icon" class="dz-sidebar-item-icon shrink-0">
   120	      <slot name="icon" />
   121	    </span>
   122	
   123	    <!-- Label: hidden when collapsed -->
   124	    <span
   125	      v-if="!isCollapsed"
   126	      class="dz-sidebar-item-label truncate"
   127	    >
   128	      <slot />
   129	    </span>
   130	
   131	    <!-- Badge: hidden when collapsed -->
   132	    <span
   133	      v-if="$slots.badge && !isCollapsed"
   134	      class="dz-sidebar-item-badge ml-auto shrink-0"
   135	    >
   136	      <slot name="badge" />
   137	    </span>
   138	  </component>

    59	/**
    60	 * Read a boolean value from localStorage.
    61	 * Returns `fallback` when storage is unavailable or key is absent.
    62	 *
    63	 * @internal
    64	 */
    65	function readStoredBoolean(key: string, fallback: boolean): boolean {
    66	  if (typeof window === 'undefined')
    67	    return fallback
    68	  try {
    69	    const raw = localStorage.getItem(key)
    70	    if (raw === null)
    71	      return fallback
    72	    return raw === 'true'
    73	  }
    74	  catch {
    75	    return fallback
    76	  }
    77	}
    78	
    79	/**
    80	 * Write a boolean value to localStorage.
    81	 * Silently no-ops when storage is unavailable.
    82	 *
    83	 * @internal
    84	 */
    85	function writeStoredBoolean(key: string, value: boolean): void {
    86	  if (typeof window === 'undefined')
    87	    return
    88	  try {
    89	    localStorage.setItem(key, String(value))
    90	  }
    91	  catch {
    92	    // Storage full or unavailable — silently ignore
    93	  }
    94	}
    95	
    96	/**
    97	 * Manages sidebar layout behavior including collapsed/expanded state,
    98	 * mobile overlay, responsive breakpoint detection via matchMedia,
    99	 * localStorage persistence, and Ctrl/Cmd+B keyboard shortcut.
   100	 *
   101	 * @param options - Configuration for the sidebar behavior
   102	 * @returns Refs, computed values, and methods to control the sidebar
   103	 *
   104	 * @example
   105	 * ``\`ts
   106	 * const {
   107	 *   collapsed,
   108	 *   mobileOpen,
   109	 *   isMobile,
   110	 *   sidebarWidth,
   111	 *   toggle,
   112	 *   toggleMobile,
   113	 * } = useSidebar({ storageKey: 'app-sidebar' })
   114	 * ``\`
   115	 */
   116	export function useSidebar(options: UseSidebarOptions = {}): UseSidebarReturn {
   117	  const {
   118	    defaultCollapsed = false,
   119	    defaultMobileOpen = false,
   120	    collapsedWidth = '4rem',
   121	    expandedWidth = '16rem',
   122	    mobileBreakpoint = 768,
   123	    storageKey,
   124	  } = options
   125	
   126	  // ---- State ----
   127	
   128	  const initialCollapsed = storageKey

   130	    : defaultCollapsed
   131	
   132	  const collapsed = ref(initialCollapsed)
   133	  const mobileOpen = ref(defaultMobileOpen)
   134	  const mobileMatch = ref(false)

   136	  // ---- Computed ----

   139	
   140	  const sidebarWidth: ComputedRef<string> = computed(() =>
   141	    collapsed.value ? collapsedWidth : expandedWidth,
   142	  )
   143	
   144	  // ---- Actions ----
   145	
   146	  /** Toggle collapsed state */
   147	  function toggle(): void {
   148	    collapsed.value = !collapsed.value
   149	  }
   150	
   151	  /** Expand the sidebar */
   152	  function expand(): void {
   153	    collapsed.value = false
   154	  }
   155	
   156	  /** Collapse the sidebar */
   157	  function collapse(): void {
   158	    collapsed.value = true
   159	  }
   160	
   161	  /** Toggle mobile overlay */
   162	  function toggleMobile(): void {
   163	    mobileOpen.value = !mobileOpen.value
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  /** Open mobile overlay */
   167	  function openMobile(): void {
   168	    mobileOpen.value = true
   169	  }
   170	
   171	  /** Close mobile overlay */
   172	  function closeMobile(): void {
   173	    mobileOpen.value = false
   174	  }
   175	
   176	  // ---- Persistence ----
   177	
   178	  if (storageKey) {
   179	    watch(collapsed, (value) => {
   180	      writeStoredBoolean(storageKey, value)
   181	    })
   182	  }
   183	
   184	  // ---- matchMedia for mobile detection ----
   185	
   186	  let mediaQuery: MediaQueryList | null = null
   187	
   188	  function handleMediaChange(event: MediaQueryListEvent | MediaQueryList): void {
   189	    mobileMatch.value = event.matches
   190	  }

     1	<!--
     2	  DzThemeProvider — Provides theme context to the component tree.
     3	
     4	  Uses provide() with DZ_THEME_KEY (ADR-08) to expose the minimal
     5	  useTheme API (ADR-09): theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme.
     6	
     7	  Persists to localStorage key (default 'dz-theme') and sets a data
     8	  attribute on document.documentElement (ADR-15).
     9	
    10	  SSR-safe: all window/document access is deferred to onMounted().

    15	<script setup lang="ts">
    16	import type { DzThemeProviderProps, ResolvedTheme, ThemePreference } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    17	import { computed, onMounted, onUnmounted, provide, ref, watch } from 'vue'
    18	import { DZ_THEME_KEY } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    19	
    20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21	// Props
    22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23	
    24	const props = withDefaults(defineProps<DzThemeProviderProps>(), {
    25	  defaultTheme: 'system',
    26	  storageKey: 'dz-theme',
    27	  attribute: 'data-theme',
    28	})
    29	
    30	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    31	// Helpers
    32	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    33	
    34	/** Check if code is running in a browser environment */
    35	function isBrowser(): boolean {
    36	  return typeof window !== 'undefined' && typeof document !== 'undefined'
    37	}
    38	
    39	/** Read persisted theme from localStorage (returns null if unavailable) */
    40	function readPersistedTheme(): ThemePreference | null {
    41	  if (!isBrowser())
    42	    return null
    43	  try {
    44	    const stored = localStorage.getItem(props.storageKey)
    45	    if (stored === 'light' || stored === 'dark' || stored === 'system') {
    46	      return stored
    47	    }
    48	  }
    49	  catch {
    50	    // localStorage may throw in restricted contexts (e.g. iframe sandboxes)
    51	  }
    52	  return null
    53	}
    54	
    55	/** Persist theme to localStorage */
    56	function persistTheme(value: ThemePreference): void {
    57	  if (!isBrowser())
    58	    return
    59	  try {
    60	    localStorage.setItem(props.storageKey, value)
    61	  }
    62	  catch {
    63	    // Silently ignore storage errors
    64	  }
    65	}
    66	
    67	/** Apply the theme attribute to document.documentElement */
    68	function applyAttribute(resolved: ResolvedTheme): void {
    69	  if (!isBrowser())
    70	    return
    71	  document.documentElement.setAttribute(props.attribute, resolved)
    72	}
    73	
    74	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75	// Reactive state
    76	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77	
    78	const theme = ref<ThemePreference>(readPersistedTheme() ?? props.defaultTheme)
    79	const systemPrefersDark = ref(false)
    80	
    81	/** Resolved theme: converts 'system' to actual light/dark */
    82	const resolvedTheme = computed<ResolvedTheme>(() => {
    83	  if (theme.value === 'system') {
    84	    return systemPrefersDark.value ? 'dark' : 'light'
    85	  }
    86	  return theme.value
    87	})
    88	
    89	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    90	// Media query listener
    91	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    92	
    93	let mediaQuery: MediaQueryList | null = null
    94	
    95	function handleMediaChange(event: MediaQueryListEvent): void {
    96	  systemPrefersDark.value = event.matches
    97	}
    98	
    99	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100	// Watchers
   101	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   102	
   103	// Sync resolved theme to DOM attribute
   104	watch(resolvedTheme, (resolved) => {
   105	  applyAttribute(resolved)
   106	})
   107	
   108	// Persist preference changes to localStorage
   109	watch(theme, (value) => {
   110	  persistTheme(value)
   111	})
   112	
   113	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   114	// SSR-safe lifecycle (ADR-15)
   115	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116	
   117	onMounted(() => {
   118	  if (!isBrowser())
   119	    return
   120	
   121	  mediaQuery = window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')
   122	  systemPrefersDark.value = mediaQuery.matches
   123	
   124	  mediaQuery.addEventListener('change', handleMediaChange)
   125	
   126	  // Apply initial theme attribute
   127	  applyAttribute(resolvedTheme.value)
   128	})
   129	
   130	onUnmounted(() => {
   131	  if (mediaQuery) {
   132	    mediaQuery.removeEventListener('change', handleMediaChange)
   133	    mediaQuery = null
   134	  }
   135	})
   136	
   137	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   138	// Public API (ADR-09 minimal)
   139	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   140	
   141	/** Set the theme preference */
   142	function setTheme(value: ThemePreference): void {
   143	  theme.value = value
   144	}
   145	
   146	/** Toggle between light and dark (if 'system', resolves to opposite of current) */
   147	function toggleTheme(): void {
   148	  theme.value = resolvedTheme.value === 'dark' ? 'light' : 'dark'
   149	}
   150	
   151	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   152	// Provide context (ADR-08)
   153	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154	
   155	provide(DZ_THEME_KEY, {
   156	  theme,
   157	  resolvedTheme,
   158	  setTheme,
   159	  toggleTheme,
   160	})
   161	</script>
   162	
   163	<template>
   164	  <slot />
   165	</template>

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# credential-scan.sh — scan git history for accidentally committed secrets
     3	# Run this BEFORE executing git filter-repo (H3 history purge).
     4	# Usage: bash scripts/credential-scan.sh [--fix]
     5	#
     6	# Requires: git (already installed)
     7	# Optionally uses: gitleaks (https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks) if available
     8	# No network access required; pure git log scanning.
     9	
    10	set -euo pipefail
    11	
    12	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
    13	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    14	
    15	RED='\033[0;31m'
    16	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    17	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    18	NC='\033[0m'
    19	
    20	echo "=== Credential scan for: $(basename "$REPO_ROOT") ==="
    21	echo "Working dir: $REPO_ROOT"
    22	echo ""
    23	
    24	FOUND=0
    25	
    26	# ─── Pattern list ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    27	# Covers tokens, keys, and credentials commonly leaked in UI repos.
    28	declare -a PATTERNS=(
    29	  # Generic high-entropy tokens / API keys
    30	  'AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}'                         # AWS access key ID
    31	  '[Aa][Ww][Ss]_[Ss][Ee][Cc][Rr][Ee][Tt]'   # AWS secret reference
    32	  'ghp_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}'                      # GitHub personal access token (classic)
    33	  'github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{82}'             # GitHub fine-grained PAT
    34	  'npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{36}'                      # npm automation token
    35	  'sk-[A-Za-z0-9]{48}'                       # OpenAI-style secret key
    36	  'xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9\-]{10,48}'         # Slack token
    37	  # Generic patterns
    38	  '[Pp][Aa][Ss][Ss][Ww][Oo][Rr][Dd]\s*=\s*["\x27][^"]{6,}'
    39	  '[Ss][Ee][Cc][Rr][Ee][Tt]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][^"]{8,}'
    40	  '[Aa][Pp][Ii][_-][Kk][Ee][Yy]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][^"]{8,}'
    41	  '[Tt][Oo][Kk][Ee][Nn]\s*[:=]\s*["\x27][A-Za-z0-9_\-\.]{16,}'
    42	  # Private keys
    43	  '-----BEGIN (RSA|EC|DSA|OPENSSH|PGP) PRIVATE KEY'
    44	  # Connection strings
    45	  'mongodb(\+srv)?://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    46	  'postgres(ql)?://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    47	  'mysql://[^:]+:[^@]+@'
    48	  'redis://:?[^@]+@'
    49	  # .env-style
    50	  '^(export )?[A-Z_]+=.{8,}$'
    51	)
    52	
    53	# ─── 1. Scan current working tree ─────────────────────────────────────────────
    54	echo "--- [1/3] Scanning working tree ---"
    55	for pat in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    56	  matches=$(git grep -lE "$pat" -- ':!node_modules' ':!*.lock' ':!scripts/credential-scan.sh' 2>/dev/null || true)
    57	  if [[ -n "$matches" ]]; then
    58	    echo -e "${RED}PATTERN HIT (working tree):${NC} $pat"
    59	    echo "$matches" | sed 's/^/  /'
    60	    FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    61	  fi
    62	done
    63	
    64	# ─── 2. Scan git history (all refs) ──────────────────────────────────────────
    65	echo ""
    66	echo "--- [2/3] Scanning git history (all commits) ---"
    67	echo "    (This may take a minute on large repos)"
    68	
    69	for pat in "${PATTERNS[@]}"; do
    70	  # git log -G searches diff content; -S counts occurrences
    71	  hits=$(git log --all --oneline -G "$pat" -- 2>/dev/null | head -20 || true)
    72	  if [[ -n "$hits" ]]; then
    73	    echo -e "${RED}PATTERN HIT (history):${NC} $pat"
    74	    echo "$hits" | sed 's/^/  commit: /'
    75	    FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    76	  fi
    77	done
    78	
    79	# ─── 3. Check for .env files ever tracked ────────────────────────────────────
    80	echo ""
    81	echo "--- [3/3] Checking for .env files ever tracked ---"
    82	env_history=$(git log --all --full-history --oneline -- '**/.env' '.env' '**/.env.*' '.env.*' 2>/dev/null || true)
    83	if [[ -n "$env_history" ]]; then
    84	  echo -e "${YELLOW}WARNING: .env files found in history:${NC}"
    85	  echo "$env_history" | sed 's/^/  /'
    86	  FOUND=$((FOUND + 1))
    87	else
    88	  echo -e "${GREEN}OK: No .env files found in history.${NC}"
    89	fi
    90	
    91	# ─── Optional: gitleaks ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    92	echo ""
    93	if command -v gitleaks &>/dev/null; then
    94	  echo "--- [bonus] Running gitleaks detect ---"
    95	  gitleaks detect --source . --no-git 2>&1 || true
    96	else
    97	  echo -e "${YELLOW}TIP: Install gitleaks for a more thorough scan:${NC}"
    98	  echo "  https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks#installation"
    99	fi
   100	
   101	# ─── Summary ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   102	echo ""
   103	echo "========================================="
   104	if [[ $FOUND -gt 0 ]]; then
   105	  echo -e "${RED}SCAN COMPLETE — $FOUND pattern(s) matched.${NC}"
   106	  echo ""
   107	  echo "Next steps:"
   108	  echo "  1. Manually review each hit above to confirm it is a real secret."
   109	  echo "  2. Rotate any confirmed credentials immediately."
   110	  echo "  3. Then run the H3 filter-repo purge (see STATUS.md §H3)."
   111	  exit 1
   112	else
   113	  echo -e "${GREEN}SCAN COMPLETE — No obvious credentials found.${NC}"
   114	  echo "You may proceed with the H3 git filter-repo purge."
   115	fi

   136	
   137	  build:

   191	              echo "ERROR: link: dependency in packages/$pkg tarball"

   229	      - name: Build Storybook

   237	
   238	  e2e:
   239	    name: E2E Tests (Playwright)

   242	    steps:
   243	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   244	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   245	        with:

   256	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
