```text
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	name: Release
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	
     7	concurrency:
     8	  group: release-${{ github.ref }}
     9	  cancel-in-progress: false # Don't cancel release in progress
    10	
    11	jobs:
    12	  release:
    13	    name: Release
    14	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15	    permissions:
    16	      contents: write # For creating tags and GitHub releases
    17	      pull-requests: write # For creating version PRs
    18	      id-token: write # For npm provenance
    19	    steps:
    20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    21	        with:
    22	          fetch-depth: 0 # Full history for changeset version
    23	
    24	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    25	        with:
    26	          node-version: 20
    27	          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
    28	
    29	      - name: Enable Corepack
    30	        run: corepack enable
    31	
    32	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    33	        id: yarn-cache-dir
    34	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    35	
    36	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    37	        with:
    38	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    39	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    40	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    41	
    42	      - name: Install dependencies
    43	        run: yarn install --immutable
    44	
    45	      - name: Build all packages
    46	        run: yarn build
    47	
    48	      - name: Run tests
    49	        run: yarn test
    50	
    51	      - name: Create Release Pull Request or Publish
    52	        id: changesets
    53	        uses: changesets/action@v1
    54	        with:
    55	          version: yarn version-packages
    56	          publish: yarn release
    57	          title: 'chore: version packages'
    58	          commit: 'chore: version packages'
    59	        env:
    60	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    61	          NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    62	          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	name: Publish Prerelease
     2	
     3	on:
     4	  workflow_dispatch:
     5	    inputs:
     6	      tag:
     7	        description: 'Prerelease tag (e.g., alpha, beta, rc)'
     8	        required: true
     9	        default: 'alpha'
    10	        type: choice
    11	        options:
    12	          - alpha
    13	          - beta
    14	          - rc
    15	
    16	concurrency:
    17	  group: publish-prerelease
    18	  cancel-in-progress: false # Don't cancel publish in progress
    19	
    20	jobs:
    21	  publish-prerelease:
    22	    name: Publish Prerelease (${{ inputs.tag }})
    23	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    24	    permissions:
    25	      contents: read
    26	      id-token: write # For npm provenance
    27	    steps:
    28	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    29	
    30	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    31	        with:
    32	          node-version: 20
    33	          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
    34	
    35	      - name: Enable Corepack
    36	        run: corepack enable
    37	
    38	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    39	        id: yarn-cache-dir
    40	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    41	
    42	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    43	        with:
    44	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    45	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    46	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    47	
    48	      - name: Install dependencies
    49	        run: yarn install --immutable
    50	
    51	      - name: Build all packages
    52	        run: yarn build
    53	
    54	      - name: Run tests
    55	        run: yarn test
    56	
    57	      - name: Publish prerelease
    58	        run: |
    59	          yarn changeset publish --tag ${{ inputs.tag }}
    60	        env:
    61	          NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    62	          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba README.md | sed -n '1,240p'" in /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	# dzup-ui
     2	
     3	**Open-source Vue 3 component library — 147 foundational components.**
     4	
     5	![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@dzup-ui/core?label=%40dzup-ui%2Fcore)
     6	![build status](https://img.shields.io/github/actions/workflow/status/datazup/dzup-ui/ci.yml?branch=main)
     7	![license](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-green)
     8	
     9	> Looking for enterprise components? See [@dzup-ui/pro](https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui-pro).
    10	
    11	## Features
    12	
    13	- 147 Vue 3 components across 11 families
    14	- TypeScript strict mode with zero `any` types and full type inference
    15	- Tailwind CSS 4 design token system with light/dark/system theming
    16	- Reka UI headless primitives for accessible interactive components
    17	- WCAG AA accessibility with keyboard navigation and ARIA support
    18	- SSR-safe, ESM-only distribution with tree-shaking support
    19	
    20	## Quick Start
    21	
    22	**1. Install packages**
    23	
    24	``\`bash
    25	yarn add @dzup-ui/core @dzup-ui/tokens vue@^3.5.0 reka-ui@^2.0.0
    26	``\`
    27	
    28	**2. Import styles**
    29	
    30	``\`css
    31	@import "@dzup-ui/tokens/css";
    32	@import "@dzup-ui/core/styles";
    33	``\`
    34	
    35	**3. Use components**
    36	
    37	``\`vue
    38	<script setup lang="ts">
    39	import { DzButton, DzInput, DzThemeProvider } from '@dzup-ui/core'
    40	import { ref } from 'vue'
    41	
    42	const name = ref('')
    43	</script>
    44	
    45	<template>
    46	  <DzThemeProvider default-theme="system">
    47	    <DzInput v-model="name" placeholder="Enter your name" />
    48	    <DzButton tone="primary" @click="submit">Submit</DzButton>
    49	  </DzThemeProvider>
    50	</template>
    51	``\`
    52	
    53	## Packages
    54	
    55	| Package | Version | Description |
    56	|---------|---------|-------------|
    57	| [`@dzup-ui/core`](./packages/core) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | 147 foundational components (buttons, inputs, forms, layout, navigation, overlays, feedback, data, media, typography) |
    58	| [`@dzup-ui/tokens`](./packages/tokens) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Design tokens — CSS custom properties, Tailwind theme, TypeScript definitions |
    59	| [`@dzup-ui/contracts`](./packages/contracts) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Canonical public API contract types (props, events, slots) |
    60	| [`@dzup-ui/compat`](./packages/compat) | 0.0.1 | Migration adapters from old dzup-ui to vNext |
    61	| [`@dzup-ui/codemods`](./packages/codemods) | 0.0.1 | Automated code transforms for migration (CLI: `dzup-codemod`) |
    62	| [`@dzup-ui/nuxt`](./packages/nuxt) | 0.1.0-alpha.0 | Nuxt 3 module for auto-importing Dz* components |
    63	
    64	## Component Families
    65	
    66	Buttons · Cards · Inputs · Forms · Layout · Navigation · Overlays · Feedback · Data · Media · Typography
    67	
    68	## Tech Stack
    69	
    70	| Category | Technology |
    71	|----------|------------|
    72	| Framework | Vue 3.5+ with Composition API and `<script setup>` |
    73	| Language | TypeScript 5.6+ (strict mode) |
    74	| Styling | Tailwind CSS 4 with `@theme` directive |
    75	| Variants | tailwind-variants (tv) for type-safe component styling |
    76	| Primitives | Reka UI 2.x for headless interactive components |
    77	| Icons | lucide-vue-next |
    78	| Dates | @internationalized/date |
    79	| Positioning | @floating-ui/vue |
    80	| Testing | Vitest + Playwright |
    81	| Bundler | Vite (library mode, ESM-only) |
    82	| Monorepo | Yarn 4 workspaces |
    83	
    84	## Development
    85	
    86	``\`bash
    87	# Install dependencies
    88	yarn install
    89	
    90	# Type check
    91	yarn typecheck
    92	
    93	# Lint
    94	yarn lint
    95	
    96	# Run tests
    97	yarn test
    98	
    99	# Build all packages
   100	yarn build
   101	
   102	# Start Storybook
   103	yarn storybook
   104	``\`
   105	
   106	## Enterprise Components
   107	
   108	Need Kanban, Gantt, FormBuilder, DashboardBuilder, WorkflowDesigner, and more?
   109	See **[@dzup-ui/pro](https://github.com/datazup/dzup-ui-pro)** — 40 enterprise components available under a commercial license.
   110	
   111	## Contributing
   112	
   113	See [CONTRIBUTING.md](./CONTRIBUTING.md) for development workflow and guidelines.
   114	
   115	## Migration
   116	
   117	Upgrading from old `dzup-ui`? See [`@dzup-ui/compat`](./packages/compat) and [`@dzup-ui/codemods`](./packages/codemods).
   118	
   119	## License
   120	
   121	MIT — see [LICENSE](./LICENSE) for details.

     1	# Security Policy
     2	
     3	## Supported Versions
     4	
     5	| Version | Supported |
     6	|---------|-----------|
     7	| 0.1.x (alpha) | Yes |
     8	
     9	## Reporting a Vulnerability
    10	
    11	**Do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.**
    12	
    13	Please report security issues by emailing the maintainers directly (see the contact in the GitHub repository's security advisories tab), or by using [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability).
    14	
    15	Include in your report:
    16	- A description of the vulnerability
    17	- Steps to reproduce
    18	- Potential impact
    19	- Any suggested fix (optional)
    20	
    21	You will receive a response within 5 business days. We aim to release a fix within 30 days of confirmation.
    22	
    23	## Scope
    24	
    25	This policy applies to the `@dzup-ui/core`, `@dzup-ui/tokens`, `@dzup-ui/contracts`, and `@dzup-ui/nuxt` packages published under the `dzup-ui` repository.

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba packages/core/src/providers/useTheme.ts | sed -n '1,200p'" in /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * useTheme — Consumer composable for DzThemeProvider context.
     3	 *
     4	 * Injects the theme context from the nearest DzThemeProvider ancestor.
     5	 * Issues a dev-mode warning if used outside a DzThemeProvider.
     6	 *
     7	 * Returns the ADR-09 minimal API: theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme.
     8	 *
     9	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/providers/useTheme
    10	 */
    11	
    12	import type { DzThemeContext } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    13	import { inject } from 'vue'
    14	import { DZ_THEME_KEY } from './DzThemeProvider.types'
    15	
    16	/**
    17	 * Inject theme context from the nearest DzThemeProvider ancestor.
    18	 *
    19	 * @throws Dev-mode warning if no provider is found.
    20	 * @returns The DzThemeContext with theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme.
    21	 *
    22	 * @example
    23	 * ``\`vue
    24	 * <script setup lang="ts">
    25	 * import { useTheme } from '@dzup-ui/core/providers'
    26	 *
    27	 * const { theme, resolvedTheme, setTheme, toggleTheme } = useTheme()
    28	 * </script>
    29	 *
    30	 * <template>
    31	 *   <button @click="toggleTheme">
    32	 *     Current: {{ resolvedTheme }}
    33	 *   </button>
    34	 * </template>
    35	 * ``\`
    36	 */
    37	export function useTheme(): DzThemeContext {
    38	  const context = inject(DZ_THEME_KEY)
    39	
    40	  if (!context) {
    41	    if (import.meta.env?.DEV) {
    42	      console.warn(
    43	        '[DzThemeProvider] useTheme() was called outside of a <DzThemeProvider>. '
    44	        + 'Wrap your app in <DzThemeProvider> to use theme context.',
    45	      )
    46	    }
    47	    throw new Error(
    48	      'useTheme() requires a <DzThemeProvider> ancestor. '
    49	      + 'Wrap your component tree in <DzThemeProvider>.',
    50	    )
    51	  }
    52	
    53	  return context
    54	}

# 04 Security Review

Scope: static security review of `ui/dzup-ui` with emphasis on authn/authz, tenant isolation, secret handling, unsafe input paths, file/path access, SSRF-like fetch paths, webhook exposure, queue/job trust boundaries, config defaults, and sensitive-data exposure.

Assessment summary: no high/critical vulnerability was confirmed in this repository’s own runtime scope. One medium-confidence client-side issue and two low-severity hardening gaps were identified.

## Repository Overview

`dzup-ui` is an open-source Vue 3 component-library monorepo (`core`, `tokens`, `contracts`, `compat`, `codemods`, `nuxt`) rather than an application backend or API service ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3), [README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:53)).  
The repository summary in `out/` also classifies it as a UI repo/package set, with Storybook and sandbox demo surfaces ([ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:1)).  
This matters for security interpretation: most real authn/authz and tenant isolation controls are expected in consuming apps/services, not in this library itself.

## Trust Boundaries

- Users: end users interact with downstream apps that render these components; this repo does not run the app auth boundary directly ([README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3)).
- Tenants: no tenant model or tenant-scoped data model exists in this codebase.
- Admins: no admin policy engine or privileged route guard subsystem exists in this repo.
- External providers: npm publish and GitHub release automation rely on GitHub Actions + npm registry credentials (`NPM_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`) ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:51), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:57)).
- Webhooks: no inbound webhook handlers were found in runtime packages.
- Background jobs/queues: no queue worker/job processor surface was found in runtime packages.
- Storage: browser `localStorage` is used for UI preference persistence (theme/sidebar), not session credentials ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:65)).
- Internal services: no service-to-service calls or backend client layer is present in runtime component code.

## Security Strengths

- Theme persistence is allowlisted to expected values (`light|dark|system`) before use, reducing storage-tampering impact ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:45)).
- Theme bootstrap string generation uses `JSON.stringify` for user-configurable fields, which reduces script-construction injection risk in that helper ([theme-script.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:52)).
- CI uses immutable install mode (`yarn install --immutable`), reducing lockfile drift and accidental dependency mutation during pipeline runs ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:37)).
- Secret-hygiene workflow exists (`scan:credentials`) and includes working-tree + git-history scanning patterns ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:50), [credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:53)).
- Private vulnerability disclosure policy is documented ([SECURITY.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/SECURITY.md:11)).

## Findings

### SR-01 (Medium): Unvalidated navigable URL props are forwarded to anchor navigation sinks

- Impact: if consuming applications bind attacker-controlled URLs to `href`/string `to`, this can enable client-side XSS via `javascript:` URIs or malicious redirect/navigation.
- Evidence: direct sink forwarding appears in [DzButton.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/buttons/DzButton.vue:126), [DzMenuItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenuItem.vue:49), [DzBreadcrumbItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzBreadcrumbItem.vue:49), and [DzSidebarItem.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebarItem.vue:83). Related prop types are unconstrained `string` ([DzButton.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/buttons/DzButton.types.ts:47), [DzMenu.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzMenu.types.ts:56), [DzBreadcrumb.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzBreadcrumb.types.ts:50), [DzSidebar.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/navigation/DzSidebar.types.ts:80)).
- Attack path: attacker controls URL-like content (CMS/profile/link field) -> downstream app maps it into these props -> user clicks rendered element -> browser navigates/executes payload.
- Remediation: introduce a shared URL sanitizer/validator for all navigable props with explicit allowlist (`http:`, `https:`, `mailto:`, `tel:`, safe relative paths), reject active/mixed schemes (`javascript:`, `data:`), and add contract tests with obfuscated payload variants.

### SR-02 (Low): Nuxt integration injects inline script with `innerHTML` by default, pressuring CSP relaxation

- Impact: consumers with strict CSP may weaken policy to keep theme pre-init behavior, increasing exploitability of unrelated DOM injection bugs in downstream apps.
- Evidence: Nuxt module pushes inline head script via `innerHTML` with no security toggle/nonce integration in options ([module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:3), [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:273), [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:275)).
- Attack path: downstream app allows `'unsafe-inline'` to accommodate module behavior -> unrelated script injection elsewhere becomes materially easier to weaponize.
- Remediation: add module options to disable inline injection and/or support nonce/hash-based CSP-safe mode; provide external-script alternative and explicit CSP guidance.

### SR-03 (Low): CI/release workflows use third-party GitHub Actions by mutable major tags instead of immutable SHAs

- Impact: compromised upstream action release or tag hijack increases supply-chain risk in CI/release pipeline.
- Evidence: workflows use `@v4`/`@v1` tag pins for actions (for example `actions/checkout@v4`, `actions/setup-node@v4`, `changesets/action@v1`) ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:22), [release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:20), [release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:53)).
- Attack path: malicious or compromised upstream action update under same major tag -> pipeline executes unreviewed code with repo and publish credentials.
- Remediation: pin critical actions to full commit SHAs and add periodic update workflow for controlled upgrades.

## Sensitive Data And Secrets Review

- No hard-coded high-confidence credentials were identified in repository source by targeted static pattern review (excluding dependencies/build artifacts).
- Publish credentials are referenced through GitHub Secrets and environment injection (`NPM_TOKEN`, `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`), not plaintext commits ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:60), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:61)).
- Runtime preference persistence uses `localStorage` for theme/sidebar state; no session token persistence path was found in core runtime code ([DzThemeProvider.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/DzThemeProvider.vue:44), [useSidebar.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useSidebar/useSidebar.ts:69)).
- No `document.cookie` handling, auth header construction, or session/csrf token management was found in runtime packages.
- Secret scanning exists as a script but is not currently visible as a mandatory CI gate in the reviewed workflows ([credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:53), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:13)).

## Authorization And Isolation Review

- No route guards, login/session middleware, RBAC/ABAC framework, or server-side authorization enforcement is implemented in this repo’s runtime surface.
- No tenant/team isolation logic exists in package runtime code; multi-tenant separation is delegated to consuming applications/services.
- Component states such as `disabled`, `active`, or role labels are presentation semantics, not authorization controls.
- No public sharing endpoints, webhook ingest routes, or privileged admin API surfaces were found in the repository runtime code.
- Conclusion: authn/authz and tenant isolation are out-of-scope by architecture for this repo; security contract should explicitly state these responsibilities for integrators.

## Residual Risks

- Primary residual risk is downstream misuse: consumers may bind untrusted URL/content into component props without validation, creating XSS/open-redirect conditions in host apps.
- CSP and security-header posture are mostly downstream app/server concerns; this repo cannot guarantee safe defaults at deployment perimeter.
- Release pipeline supply-chain exposure remains non-zero while actions are major-tag pinned and credential scanning is optional rather than enforced.
- Demo/storybook surfaces include external image URLs; while not a direct vulnerability here, they create outbound request behavior in preview environments.

## Recommended Remediation Plan

1. Immediate: implement `sanitizeNavigableUrl` in `@dzup-ui/core` and apply it consistently to all `href`/string `to` sinks before DOM binding.
2. Immediate: add security-focused tests for malicious URI schemes and obfuscation variants across `DzButton`, `DzMenuItem`, `DzBreadcrumbItem`, and `DzSidebarItem`.
3. Immediate: document a clear consumer security contract in repo docs: authn/authz/tenant enforcement is app-owned, and navigable props must be treated as untrusted input.
4. Short-term: extend `@dzup-ui/nuxt` options for CSP-safe theme bootstrap (disable inline mode, nonce/hash support, external script strategy).
5. Short-term: make secret scanning and dependency/audit checks first-class CI gates, not just local scripts.
6. Short-term: pin critical GitHub Actions to immutable SHAs and enable controlled update cadence.
7. Structural: add a lightweight security checklist to PR/release workflow covering URL sanitization, CSP compatibility, and supply-chain hardening controls.
```

```md
# 05 Code Quality and Maintainability

## Repository Overview
`dzup-ui` is a Yarn 4 monorepo with package-level separation across core UI runtime, contracts, tokens, compatibility adapters, Nuxt integration, codemods, and internal tooling. The current surface is large and still expanding.

- Core implementation scale: ~160 Vue components in `packages/core/src/components` and ~52 composable TS files in `packages/core/src/composables`.
- Test/docs scale: 223 package-level spec files, 6 e2e specs, and 97 Storybook story files.
- Build/governance posture is mature (typecheck, lint, contracts/a11y/SSR test lanes, boundary/export/d.ts validators): [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:14).
- Secondary static artifact underreports real scale (40 components), which creates planning noise: [ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9).

## Maintainability Overview
Maintainability is above average for a UI library of this size. The repo has strong structure and governance, but risk is increasingly concentrated in cross-package drift and a handful of oversized, multi-responsibility modules. The most serious issues are not style-level; they are contract synchronization, duplicated source-of-truth logic, and implicit runtime invariants that weaken refactor safety.

## Strengths
- Strong type/lint baseline: `strict` and `noUncheckedIndexedAccess` are enabled: [tsconfig.base.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:7).
- No explicit `any` usage found in source files (`: any`, `as any`, `<any>` patterns absent), which keeps API contracts cleaner over time.
- Good package boundary governance with explicit dependency rules and deep-import checks: [import-boundary.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/validators/import-boundary.ts:44).
- Manifest-driven export generation avoids manual barrel churn and supports consistent API publishing: [manifest-generator.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/manifest-generator.ts:90).
- Test footprint is broad and includes behavior-rich composable tests (e.g., DataGrid): [useDataGrid.spec.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGrid.spec.ts:29).

## Maintainability Findings
1. **High: Cross-package API naming drift (core manifest vs Nuxt auto-registration)**
- Nuxt maintains a hardcoded `CORE_COMPONENTS` list: [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:22).
- The canonical core API lives in manifest exports: [public-api.manifest.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:6).
- There are concrete mismatches (`DzSearchBox` vs `DzSearchInput`, `DzTabsList` vs `DzTabList`, `DzToastContainer` vs `DzToastViewport`, etc.): [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:90), [public-api.manifest.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:38), [public-api.manifest.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:93), [public-api.manifest.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:155).
- Nuxt tests are mostly import/smoke checks and do not validate parity with core manifest: [module.spec.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:5).
- **Why this is maintainability risk:** rename/add/remove operations require manual updates in multiple places and regressions can ship silently.

2. **High: Duplicated source-of-truth for theme bootstrap behavior**
- Theme init logic exists in tokens as an inline script constant: [theme-script.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tokens/src/utils/theme-script.ts:23).
- Core has a separate generator with richer options: [theme-script.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:45).
- Nuxt injects another inline variant directly in module setup: [module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:275).
- **Why this is maintainability risk:** behavior can diverge by host environment (storage fallback/system resolution), and fixes must be repeated across three implementations.

3. **Medium: DataGrid type contracts rely on implicit invariants and casts**
- Filter/sort keys use broad `string` types: [DzDataGrid.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts:61), [DzDataGrid.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts:78), [DzDataGrid.types.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts:131).
- Runtime logic compensates with casts (`as keyof T`): [useDataGridSort.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGridSort.ts:123), [useDataGridSort.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGridSort.ts:140).
- Row identity falls back to object reference when `rowKey` is absent: [useDataGrid.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGrid.ts:165).
- **Why this is maintainability risk:** compiler guarantees are weaker than they appear; immutable data refreshes can break selection/filter behavior subtly.

4. **Medium: Config duplication increases environment skew risk**
- Alias definitions are duplicated across TS paths, vitest, sandbox Vite, and Storybook configs: [tsconfig.base.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:24), [vitest.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/vitest.config.ts:8), [sandbox vite.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/vite.config.ts:9), [storybook vite.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/vite.config.ts:9), [storybook main.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:28).
- **Why this is maintainability risk:** path changes require multi-file synchronization and tend to break one toolchain first.

5. **Low-Medium: TODO/stub debt and placeholder duplication**
- Core includes explicit stub components with unresolved contract/test artifacts: [TeamMemberBadge.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/TeamMemberBadge.vue:2), [GovernanceBadge.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/GovernanceBadge.vue:2).
- Sandbox has repeated “coming soon” page structures with copy-pasted styles/comments: [NavigationPage.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/NavigationPage.vue:2), [MediaPage.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/MediaPage.vue:2), [LayoutPage.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/LayoutPage.vue:2).
- **Why this is maintainability risk:** low immediate runtime risk, but it dilutes signal and makes completion boundaries ambiguous.

6. **Low: Documentation/telemetry drift around scale claims**
- Public docs still market “147 components”: [README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3), [Introduction.mdx](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/stories/Introduction.mdx:8).
- External extracted artifact reports 40 components: [ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9).
- **Why this matters:** planning, QA scope, and onboarding are affected by inconsistent repository metadata.

**Stylistic nits (non-risk)**
- Test title typo (`cORE_COMPONENTS`) is cosmetic: [module.spec.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:11).
- Repeated placeholder page styling is mostly polish debt unless placeholders persist long-term.

## Large-Module And Legacy Risk
- `packages/tokens/src/generate.ts` (~445 LOC): multi-output generator (CSS/TS/Tailwind concerns in one place) increases change coupling.
- `packages/tooling/scripts/validate-dts.ts` (~361 LOC) and `validate-exports.ts` (~350 LOC): important gatekeepers, but single-file complexity raises onboarding and modification cost.
- `packages/core/src/components/forms/DzCombobox.vue` (~347 LOC): combines rendering, state, and accessibility interactions in one SFC.
- `packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue` (~299 LOC): repeated item markup across grouped/ungrouped/flat branches creates high edit blast radius: [DzCommandPalette.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:204), [DzCommandPalette.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:233), [DzCommandPalette.vue](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:262).
- `packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.ts` (~291 LOC): many side effects (scroll lock, aria hiding, outside click, focus trap, RAF timing) in a single composable: [useDialog.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.ts:118).
- `apps/sandbox/src/pages/FormsPage.vue` (~560 LOC) and `CardsPage.vue` (~422 LOC): demo harness pages are becoming monolithic and harder to evolve.

Legacy risk indicators:
- Compatibility package intentionally carries deprecated adapters (useful but long-tail maintenance load): [compat index](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/index.ts:1).
- Deprecated re-export remains for `useTheme`: [useTheme.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useTheme/useTheme.ts:2).

## Testability And Refactorability
What helps:
- Extensive automated validation and test lanes for contracts, a11y, SSR, and e2e: [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:21), [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:22), [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:23), [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:43).
- Composable-first architecture in core creates reusable seams and unit-testable behavior blocks (DataGrid, Sidebar, Dialog).
- Tooling validators catch structural contract drift before publishing.

What blocks safe change:
- Nuxt integration lacks robust parity tests for the registration list; refactors there are high-risk/low-feedback.
- Side-effect-dense composables (especially dialog/focus/DOM orchestration) require timing-sensitive tests and are easier to make flaky.
- Manual registries and duplicate configs increase cross-file coordination for what should be single-change operations.
- Type looseness in DataGrid key models reduces compile-time safety during schema evolution.

## Recommended Refactoring Priorities
1. Replace Nuxt `CORE_COMPONENTS` hardcoding with generated data from core manifest, and add a parity test that fails on drift.
2. Consolidate theme bootstrap into one canonical generator and consume it from tokens/Nuxt instead of embedding variants.
3. Tighten DataGrid contracts (`field`/`column` as keyed generics), then remove cast-based access paths.
4. Split `DzCommandPalette` repeated item markup into a reusable render fragment/component to reduce branch duplication.
5. Decompose `useDialog` side effects into focused helpers (`scrollLock`, `ariaSiblings`, `focusTrap`, `outsideClick`) with isolated tests.
6. Centralize workspace alias resolution in one shared helper imported by Vitest/Vite/Storybook configs.
7. Decide explicitly on stub components/pages: complete contract+tests or remove/feature-flag to reduce ambiguous TODO surface.
8. Add lightweight consistency checks for published component-count metadata in README/Storybook/out artifacts.

## Overall Assessment
Code quality is solid and engineering discipline is strong. The primary maintainability threat is cross-surface drift, not local coding style. If single-source generation is enforced for component registration and theme bootstrap, and DataGrid key typing is tightened, refactor safety and long-term change velocity should improve materially.
```

```text
 succeeded in 0ms:
# 05 Code Quality and Maintainability

## Maintainability Overview
`dzup-ui` has a generally strong maintainability baseline for a large UI monorepo: clear package segmentation, strict TypeScript defaults, and unusually strong custom validation tooling. The core package is sizeable (about 160 Vue SFC components in `packages/core/src/components`, plus 52 files in `packages/core/src/composables`) and is supported by substantial tests.

The maintainability risks are mostly not “lack of standards.” They are drift and concentration risks:
- contract drift between packages (especially `core` vs `nuxt` registries),
- a handful of large multi-responsibility files where change impact is broad,
- partial TODO/stub debt in sandbox/core,
- duplicated source-of-truth patterns (component lists, theme bootstrap logic, alias maps).

Secondary artifact quality is also drifting: the static summary underreports current scale (40 components) versus the live tree, which can mislead planning ([out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9), [out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:17](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:17)).

## Strengths
- Strong type and lint posture is enforced repo-wide ([tsconfig.base.json:7](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:7), [tsconfig.base.json:8](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:8), [eslint.config.js:14](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/eslint.config.js:14)).
- Maintainability-focused tooling is mature: boundary validation, export validation, declaration validation, bundle/peer/license checks are first-class scripts ([package.json:29](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:29), [package.json:38](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:38)).
- Manifest-driven API generation reduces manual barrel churn in core ([manifest-generator.ts:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/manifest-generator.ts:2), [manifest-generator.ts:90](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/manifest-generator.ts:90)).
- Package boundary intent is explicit and checked in code ([import-boundary.ts:44](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/src/validators/import-boundary.ts:44)).
- Core interaction logic is often extracted into composables with good test depth (for example `useDataGrid`, `useSelect`, `useDialog` specs at 627/513/339 LOC), which improves refactor safety.

## Maintainability Findings
1. **High: Core/Nuxt naming contract drift with weak guardrails**
- `nuxt` keeps a hardcoded component list with legacy names (`DzSearchBox`, `DzTabsList`, `DzTabsTrigger`, `DzTabsContent`, `DzDrawer`, `DzCollapsible`, `DzToastContainer`) ([module.ts:90](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:90), [module.ts:141](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:141), [module.ts:152](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:152)).
- Core manifest exports canonical names (`DzSearchInput`, `DzTabList`, `DzTabTrigger`, `DzTabContent`, `DzCollapse`, `DzToastViewport`) ([public-api.manifest.json:38](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:38), [public-api.manifest.json:93](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:93), [public-api.manifest.json:155](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json:155)).
- Nuxt tests are smoke-level and do not validate registration parity ([module.spec.ts:1](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:1), [module.spec.ts:11](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:11)).
- Maintainability impact: every rename requires multi-file manual sync and can silently regress consumers.

2. **Medium: DataGrid contracts are type-permissive in key places, forcing casts and implicit invariants**
- Filter/sort keys are `string` instead of `keyof T` in core types ([DzDataGrid.types.ts:61](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts:61), [DzDataGrid.types.ts:78](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts:78)).
- Runtime logic compensates with casts (`filter.column as keyof T`, `sortEntry.field as keyof T`) ([useDataGridSort.ts:123](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGridSort.ts:123), [useDataGridSort.ts:140](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGridSort.ts:140)).
- Row identity falls back to object reference when `rowKey` is omitted ([useDataGrid.ts:165](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGrid.ts:165)), which is fragile for immutable data refresh patterns.
- Maintainability impact: harder refactors and easier hidden breakage when schema/keys evolve.

3. **Medium: Source-of-truth duplication for theme bootstrap logic**
- Theme initialization script exists in tokens util, provider utility, and Nuxt inline head injection with different implementations ([tokens theme-script.ts:23](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tokens/src/utils/theme-script.ts:23), [core theme-script.ts:45](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/theme-script.ts:45), [nuxt module.ts:275](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:275)).
- Maintainability impact: behavior diverges over time and fixes must be repeated in multiple packages.

4. **Medium: Complexity hotspots in component/composable internals**
- Representative high-LOC modules: `DzCombobox.vue` (347), `DzCommandPalette.vue` (299), `useDialog.ts` (291), `useDataGrid.ts` (256), `useDataGridSort.ts` (227), `DzDataGrid.types.ts` (311).
- `DzCommandPalette` duplicates almost identical item rendering paths across grouped/ungrouped/flat branches ([DzCommandPalette.vue:204](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:204), [DzCommandPalette.vue:233](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:233), [DzCommandPalette.vue:262](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue:262)).
- `useDialog` centralizes many side effects (scroll lock, sibling aria hiding, key handlers, click-outside, focus trap) in one function ([useDialog.ts:118](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.ts:118)).
- Maintainability impact: broad edit blast radius and higher regression probability per change.

5. **Low-Medium: TODO/stub debt and duplicated placeholder pages**
- Core has stub components with explicit TODOs and no contract/spec artifacts yet (`TeamMemberBadge`, `GovernanceBadge`) ([TeamMemberBadge.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/TeamMemberBadge.vue:2), [GovernanceBadge.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/components/feedback/GovernanceBadge.vue:2)).
- Sandbox has multiple placeholder pages with repeated structure/styles and TODO comments ([NavigationPage.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/NavigationPage.vue:2), [MediaPage.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/MediaPage.vue:2), [DataPage.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/DataPage.vue:2), [LayoutPage.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/LayoutPage.vue:2), [TypographyPage.vue:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/src/pages/TypographyPage.vue:2)).
- Maintainability impact: repo noise and uncertain completion boundaries.

6. **Low: Alias/config duplication across TS/test/app/storybook surfaces**
- Alias maps are repeated across tsconfig, vitest, sandbox vite, storybook main, and storybook vite ([tsconfig.base.json:24](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/tsconfig.base.json:24), [vitest.config.ts:8](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/vitest.config.ts:8), [apps/sandbox/vite.config.ts:9](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/sandbox/vite.config.ts:9), [apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:28](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/.storybook/main.ts:28), [apps/storybook/vite.config.ts:9](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/vite.config.ts:9)).
- Maintainability impact: high chance of environment skew during package/path changes.

**Stylistic nits (non-blocking)**
- Minor naming typo in test title (`cORE_COMPONENTS`) reduces polish but not behavior ([module.spec.ts:11](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:11)).
- Placeholder sandbox pages duplicate one-line scoped style blocks verbatim; this is mostly cosmetic unless placeholders stay long-term.

## Large-Module And Legacy Risk
Largest representative modules by LOC and risk profile:
- `packages/tokens/src/generate.ts` (445): generation of CSS + TS + Tailwind outputs in one file; high change coupling.
- `packages/tooling/scripts/validate-dts.ts` (361) and `packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts` (350): valuable checks but single-file complexity increases onboarding cost.
- `packages/core/src/components/forms/DzCombobox.vue` (347): heavy behavior + rendering + accessibility integration in one SFC.
- `packages/core/src/components/overlays/DzCommandPalette.vue` (299): branch-heavy template with repeated render blocks.
- `packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.ts` (291): side-effect dense state orchestration.
- `packages/core/src/composables/useDataGrid/useDataGrid.ts` + `useDataGridSort.ts` + `DzDataGrid.types.ts` (256 + 227 + 311): logically one subsystem spread across files with shared casts/invariants.
- `apps/sandbox/src/pages/FormsPage.vue` (560) and `CardsPage.vue` (422): demo harness growth is becoming monolithic.

Legacy/deprecation indicators:
- Deprecated compatibility re-export remains in composables (`useTheme`) ([useTheme.ts:2](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useTheme/useTheme.ts:2)).
- Public docs still cite older component counts (147) despite current larger surface ([README.md:3](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3), [Introduction.mdx:8](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/apps/storybook/stories/Introduction.mdx:8)).

## Testability And Refactorability
What helps safe refactoring:
- Strong baseline test harness and script coverage (unit, a11y, contract, SSR, e2e lanes) ([package.json:18](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:18), [package.json:21](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:21), [package.json:43](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:43)).
- Good composable extraction creates natural seams for unit testing in many areas.
- Boundary/exports/d.ts validation scripts catch structural regressions before publish.

What blocks or complicates refactoring:
- DOM-coupled composables require timing-sensitive tests (`requestAnimationFrame`, `setTimeout`) which are harder to keep deterministic ([useDialog.spec.ts:166](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.spec.ts:166), [useDialog.spec.ts:239](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/composables/useDialog/useDialog.spec.ts:239)).
- Nuxt integration has poor behavioral test seams; current tests mostly assert importability ([module.spec.ts:5](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.spec.ts:5)).
- DataGrid generics are not strict enough, so refactors rely on runtime behavior and casts instead of compiler guarantees.
- Multiple manual registries/lists (Nuxt components, aliases, theme scripts) increase cross-file coordination tax.

## Recommended Refactoring Priorities
1. **Generate Nuxt `CORE_COMPONENTS` from core manifest and add parity tests.**  
   Remove manual list drift by making `nuxt` consume canonical export metadata.
2. **Strengthen DataGrid type contracts.**  
   Replace `string` column keys with generic keyed types, eliminate `as keyof T` casts where possible, and formalize row identity requirements.
3. **Unify theme bootstrap source.**  
   Keep one script generator (core or tokens) and have Nuxt/tokens consume it rather than embedding variants.
4. **Split hotspot modules by responsibility.**  
   For example: extract command item renderer in `DzCommandPalette`, split `useDialog` side-effect handlers into focused helpers, separate `tokens/generate` emitters.
5. **Centralize workspace alias definitions.**  
   Create a shared alias builder used by vitest, sandbox vite, and storybook configs.
6. **Burn down TODO/stub debt intentionally.**  
   Either complete `TeamMemberBadge`/`GovernanceBadge` contract + tests or remove/park behind explicit feature flags.
7. **Automate doc/telemetry consistency checks.**  
   Validate public component counts and naming across README/Storybook intro and `out/` summaries against live manifests.

## Overall Assessment
Code quality is solid and above average for a UI monorepo, with strong discipline in typing, validation, and testing infrastructure. The main maintainability caveat is not local code style; it is cross-surface drift and a few concentrated complexity hotspots. If registry generation/parity checks and DataGrid typing cleanup are prioritized, long-term refactor safety will improve significantly.

     1	/**
     2	 * DzDataGrid — type definitions for the compound DataGrid family.
     3	 *
     4	 * DzDataGrid is the most complex core component. Phase 1 scope:
     5	 * sorting, pagination, and row selection.
     6	 *
     7	 * Context injection via DZ_DATA_GRID_KEY (ADR-08).
     8	 *
     9	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/components/data/DzDataGrid
    10	 */
    11	
    12	import type {
    13	  BaseAccessibilityProps,
    14	  CanonicalSize,
    15	} from '@dzup-ui/contracts'
    16	import type { Component, InjectionKey, Ref } from 'vue'
    17	
    18	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19	// Column definition
    20	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    21	
    22	/** Column definition for the data grid */
    23	export interface ColumnDef<T> {
    24	  /** Field key to access the data from the row */
    25	  field: keyof T & string
    26	  /** Header label text */
    27	  header: string
    28	  /** Column width (number = px, string = CSS value) */
    29	  width?: number | string
    30	  /** Whether this column is sortable */
    31	  sortable?: boolean
    32	  /** Whether this column is filterable */
    33	  filterable?: boolean
    34	  /** Filter type for this column */
    35	  filterType?: FilterType
    36	  /** Filter options for select type filter */
    37	  filterOptions?: string[]
    38	  /** Custom cell renderer component */
    39	  cellRenderer?: Component
    40	  /** Custom header renderer component */
    41	  headerRenderer?: Component
    42	  /** Text alignment */
    43	  align?: 'left' | 'center' | 'right'
    44	  /** Pin column to left or right */
    45	  pinned?: 'left' | 'right'
    46	}
    47	
    48	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    49	// Filter types
    50	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    51	
    52	/** Filter type for column filtering */
    53	export type FilterType = 'text' | 'number' | 'select'
    54	
    55	/** Filter comparison operator */
    56	export type FilterOperator = 'contains' | 'equals' | 'gt' | 'lt' | 'gte' | 'lte'
    57	
    58	/** Filter state for a single column */
    59	export interface DzDataGridFilter {
    60	  /** Column field key being filtered */
    61	  column: string
    62	  /** Filter value */
    63	  value: string | number
    64	  /** Comparison operator */
    65	  operator: FilterOperator
    66	}
    67	
    68	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    69	// Sort model
    70	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    71	
    72	/** Sort direction */
    73	export type SortDirection = 'asc' | 'desc'
    74	
    75	/** Sort model for a single column */
    76	export interface SortModel {
    77	  /** Field key being sorted */
    78	  field: string
    79	  /** Sort direction */
    80	  direction: SortDirection
    81	}
    82	
    83	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    84	// Pagination config
    85	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86	
    87	/** Pagination configuration */
    88	export interface PaginationConfig {
    89	  /** Number of items per page */
    90	  pageSize: number
    91	  /** Available page size options */
    92	  pageSizeOptions?: number[]
    93	}
    94	
    95	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    96	// Grid density
    97	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    98	
    99	/** Grid row density */
   100	export type GridDensity = 'compact' | 'default' | 'comfortable'
   101	
   102	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   103	// Context (ADR-08)
   104	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   105	
   106	/** Context provided to DzDataGrid sub-parts via inject */
   107	export interface DzDataGridContext<T = Record<string, unknown>> {
   108	  /** Column definitions */
   109	  columns: Ref<ColumnDef<T>[]>
   110	  /** Current data rows */
   111	  data: Ref<T[]>
   112	  /** Row density */
   113	  density: Ref<GridDensity>
   114	  /** Size */
   115	  size: Ref<CanonicalSize>
   116	  /** Whether sorting is enabled */
   117	  sortable: Ref<boolean>
   118	  /** Current sort model */
   119	  sortModel: Ref<SortModel[]>
   120	  /** Whether filtering is enabled */
   121	  filterable: Ref<boolean>
   122	  /** Current filter state */
   123	  filters: Ref<DzDataGridFilter[]>
   124	  /** Whether selection is enabled */
   125	  selectable: Ref<boolean | 'single' | 'multiple'>
   126	  /** Currently selected rows */
   127	  selectedRows: Ref<T[]>
   128	  /** Loading state */
   129	  loading: Ref<boolean>
   130	  /** Sort a column */
   131	  sort: (field: string) => void
   132	  /** Set a filter on a column */
   133	  setFilter: (column: string, filter: DzDataGridFilter) => void
   134	  /** Clear a filter on a column */
   135	  clearFilter: (column: string) => void
   136	  /** Clear all filters */
   137	  clearAllFilters: () => void
   138	  /** Toggle row selection */
   139	  toggleRowSelection: (row: T) => void
   140	  /** Toggle all row selection */
   141	  toggleAllSelection: () => void
   142	  /** Check if a row is selected */
   143	  isRowSelected: (row: T) => boolean
   144	  /** Check if all rows are selected */
   145	  isAllSelected: Ref<boolean>
   146	  /** Check if some rows are selected (indeterminate) */
   147	  isSomeSelected: Ref<boolean>
   148	}
   149	
   150	/** Typed injection key for DzDataGrid context (ADR-08, SCREAMING_SNAKE) */
   151	export const DZ_DATA_GRID_KEY: InjectionKey<DzDataGridContext> = Symbol('dz-data-grid')
   152	
   153	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   154	// DzDataGrid Props
   155	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156	
   157	/** Props for the DzDataGrid root component */
   158	export interface DzDataGridProps<T = Record<string, unknown>> extends BaseAccessibilityProps {
   159	  /** Data rows to display */
   160	  data: T[]
   161	  /** Column definitions */
   162	  columns: ColumnDef<T>[]
   163	  /** Loading state */
   164	  loading?: boolean
   165	  /** Whether sorting is enabled */
   166	  sortable?: boolean
   167	  /** Current sort model (controlled) */
   168	  sortModel?: SortModel[]
   169	  /** Whether column filtering is enabled */
   170	  filterable?: boolean
   171	  /** Current filter state (controlled) */
   172	  filters?: DzDataGridFilter[]
   173	  /** Whether pagination is enabled */
   174	  pagination?: boolean | PaginationConfig
   175	  /** Selection mode */
   176	  selectable?: boolean | 'single' | 'multiple'
   177	  /** Currently selected rows */
   178	  selectedRows?: T[]
   179	  /** Row density */
   180	  density?: GridDensity
   181	  /** Component size */
   182	  size?: CanonicalSize
   183	  /** Function to get unique row key */
   184	  rowKey?: keyof T & string
   185	}
   186	
   187	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   188	// DzDataGrid Emits
   189	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   190	
   191	/** Events emitted by DzDataGrid */
   192	export interface DzDataGridEmits<T = Record<string, unknown>> {
   193	  /** Sort model changed */
   194	  'update:sortModel': [value: SortModel[]]
   195	  /** Filter state changed */
   196	  'update:filters': [value: DzDataGridFilter[]]
   197	  /** Selected rows changed */
   198	  'update:selectedRows': [value: T[]]
   199	  /** A row was clicked */

   205	  /** Page changed */

   207	  /** Page size changed */

   209	}
   210	
   211	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   212	// DzDataGrid Slots
   213	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   214	
   215	/** Slot definitions for DzDataGrid */
   216	export interface DzDataGridSlots<T = Record<string, unknown>> {
   217	  /** Custom header content */
   218	  header?: () => unknown
   219	  /** Custom body content */
   220	  body?: () => unknown
   221	  /** Custom cell content */
   222	  cell?: (props: { row: T, column: ColumnDef<T>, value: unknown }) => unknown
   223	  /** Content shown when data is empty */
   224	  empty?: () => unknown
   225	  /** Custom loading indicator */

   227	  /** Custom pagination content */
   228	  pagination?: () => unknown
   229	}

   232	// Sub-part types
   233	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

   240

   257	}
   258	
   259	/** Props for DzDataGridRow */
   260	export interface DzDataGridRowProps<T = Record<string, unknown>> {

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * useDataGridSort — Sorting logic extracted from useDataGrid.
     3	 *
     4	 * Handles multi-column sorting with direction cycling (asc → desc → none).
     5	 *
     6	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/composables/useDataGrid
     7	 */
     8	
     9	import type { ComputedRef, Ref } from 'vue'
    10	import type {
    11	  ColumnDef,
    12	  DzDataGridFilter,
    13	  FilterOperator,
    14	  SortDirection,
    15	  SortModel,
    16	} from '../../components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts'
    17	import { computed, ref } from 'vue'
    18	
    19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20	// Options
    21	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    22	
    23	/** Options for the useDataGridSort composable */
    24	export interface UseDataGridSortOptions<T> {
    25	  /** Raw data rows */
    26	  data: Readonly<Ref<T[]>>
    27	  /** Column definitions */
    28	  columns: Readonly<Ref<ColumnDef<T>[]>>
    29	  /** Whether sorting is enabled */
    30	  sortable: Readonly<Ref<boolean>>
    31	  /** Whether filtering is enabled */
    32	  filterable: Readonly<Ref<boolean>>
    33	  /** Initial sort model */
    34	  initialSortModel?: SortModel[]
    35	  /** Initial filter state */
    36	  initialFilters?: DzDataGridFilter[]
    37	  /** Callback when sort changes */
    38	  onSortChange?: (sortModel: SortModel[]) => void
    39	  /** Callback when filter changes */
    40	  onFilterChange?: (filters: DzDataGridFilter[]) => void
    41	}
    42	
    43	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    44	// Return type
    45	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    46	
    47	/** Return value of the useDataGridSort composable */
    48	export interface UseDataGridSortReturn<T> {
    49	  /** Current sort model */
    50	  sortModel: Ref<SortModel[]>
    51	  /** Current filter state */
    52	  filters: Ref<DzDataGridFilter[]>
    53	  /** Sorted and filtered data (before pagination) */
    54	  sortedData: ComputedRef<T[]>
    55	  /** Sort by a column field */
    56	  sort: (field: string) => void
    57	  /** Set a filter on a column */
    58	  setFilter: (column: string, filter: DzDataGridFilter) => void
    59	  /** Clear a filter on a column */
    60	  clearFilter: (column: string) => void
    61	  /** Clear all filters */
    62	  clearAllFilters: () => void
    63	  /** Get sort direction for a column */
    64	  getSortDirection: (field: string) => SortDirection | undefined
    65	  /** Reset current page (called by filter changes -- set via ref) */
    66	  resetPageRef: Ref<(() => void) | undefined> | undefined
    67	}
    68	
    69	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70	// Filter helpers
    71	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72	
    73	/** Evaluate whether a cell value matches a filter condition */
    74	function applyFilter(cellValue: unknown, filter: DzDataGridFilter): boolean {
    75	  if (filter.value === '' || filter.value === undefined)
    76	    return true
    77	
    78	  const operators: Record<FilterOperator, (cell: unknown, val: string | number) => boolean> = {
    79	    contains: (cell, val) => {
    80	      const strCell = String(cell ?? '').toLowerCase()
    81	      const strFilter = String(val).toLowerCase()
    82	      return strCell.includes(strFilter)
    83	    },
    84	    equals: (cell, val) => {
    85	      if (typeof val === 'number')
    86	        return Number(cell) === val
    87	      return String(cell ?? '').toLowerCase() === String(val).toLowerCase()
    88	    },
    89	    gt: (cell, val) => Number(cell) > Number(val),
    90	    lt: (cell, val) => Number(cell) < Number(val),
    91	    gte: (cell, val) => Number(cell) >= Number(val),
    92	    lte: (cell, val) => Number(cell) <= Number(val),
    93	  }
    94	
    95	  const evaluator = operators[filter.operator]
    96	  return evaluator ? evaluator(cellValue, filter.value) : true
    97	}
    98	
    99	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100	// Composable
   101	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Manages sorting and filtering logic for the data grid.
   105	 *
   106	 * @typeParam T - Row data type
   107	 */
   108	export function useDataGridSort<T>(
   109	  options: UseDataGridSortOptions<T>,
   110	  resetPageRef?: Ref<(() => void) | undefined>,
   111	): UseDataGridSortReturn<T> {
   112	  const sortModel = ref<SortModel[]>(options.initialSortModel ?? []) as Ref<SortModel[]>
   113	  const filters = ref<DzDataGridFilter[]>(options.initialFilters ?? []) as Ref<DzDataGridFilter[]>
   114	
   115	  // ── Filtered data (applied before sorting) ──
   116	  const filteredData = computed<T[]>(() => {
   117	    const raw = options.data.value
   118	    if (!options.filterable.value || filters.value.length === 0)
   119	      return raw
   120	
   121	    return raw.filter((row) => {
   122	      return filters.value.every((filter) => {
   123	        const cellValue = row[filter.column as keyof T]
   124	        return applyFilter(cellValue, filter)
   125	      })
   126	    })
   127	  })
   128	
   129	  // ── Sorted data ──
   130	  const sortedData = computed<T[]>(() => {
   131	    const raw = filteredData.value
   132	    if (!options.sortable.value || sortModel.value.length === 0)
   133	      return raw
   134	
   135	    const sorted = [...raw]
   136	    const sorts = sortModel.value
   137	
   138	    sorted.sort((a, b) => {
   139	      for (const sortEntry of sorts) {
   140	        const field = sortEntry.field as keyof T
   141	        const aVal = a[field]
   142	        const bVal = b[field]
   143	        const dir = sortEntry.direction === 'asc' ? 1 : -1
   144	
   145	        if (aVal < bVal)
   146	          return -1 * dir
   147	        if (aVal > bVal)
   148	          return 1 * dir
   149	      }
   150	      return 0
   151	    })
   152	
   153	    return sorted
   154	  })
   155	
   156	  // ── Sort action ──
   157	  function sort(field: string): void {
   158	    if (!options.sortable.value)
   159	      return
   160	
   161	    const col = options.columns.value.find(c => c.field === field)
   162	    if (col && col.sortable === false)
   163	      return
   164	
   165	    const existing = sortModel.value.find(s => s.field === field)
   166	
   167	    let newModel: SortModel[]
   168	    if (!existing) {
   169	      newModel = [{ field, direction: 'asc' }]
   170	    }
   171	    else if (existing.direction === 'asc') {
   172	      newModel = [{ field, direction: 'desc' }]
   173	    }
   174	    else {
   175	      newModel = []
   176	    }
   177	
   178	    sortModel.value = newModel
   179	    options.onSortChange?.(newModel)
   180	  }
   181	
   182	  // ── Filter actions ──
   183	  function setFilter(column: string, filter: DzDataGridFilter): void {
   184	    if (!options.filterable.value)
   185	      return
   186	
   187	    const existing = filters.value.findIndex(f => f.column === column)
   188	    if (existing >= 0) {
   189	      const updated = [...filters.value]
   190	      updated[existing] = filter
   191	      filters.value = updated
   192	    }
   193	    else {
   194	      filters.value = [...filters.value, filter]
   195	    }
   196	    resetPageRef?.value?.()
   197	    options.onFilterChange?.(filters.value)
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  function clearFilter(column: string): void {
   201	    filters.value = filters.value.filter(f => f.column !== column)
   202	    resetPageRef?.value?.()
   203	    options.onFilterChange?.(filters.value)
   204	  }
   205	
   206	  function clearAllFilters(): void {
   207	    filters.value = []
   208	    resetPageRef?.value?.()
   209	    options.onFilterChange?.(filters.value)
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  function getSortDirection(field: string): SortDirection | undefined {
   213	    return sortModel.value.find(s => s.field === field)?.direction
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  return {
   217	    sortModel,
   218	    filters,
   219	    sortedData,
   220	    sort,
   221	    setFilter,
   222	    clearFilter,
   223	    clearAllFilters,
   224	    getSortDirection,
   225	    resetPageRef,
   226	  }
   227	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * useDataGrid — Composable managing data grid state (sorting, pagination, selection).
     3	 *
     4	 * Orchestrates useDataGridSort and useDataGridPagination, adding row selection.
     5	 * Phase 1 scope: sorting, pagination, row selection.
     6	 *
     7	 * @module @dzup-ui/core/composables/useDataGrid
     8	 */
     9	
    10	import type { MaybeRef, Ref } from 'vue'
    11	import type {
    12	  ColumnDef,
    13	  DzDataGridFilter,
    14	  PaginationConfig,
    15	  SortDirection,
    16	  SortModel,
    17	} from '../../components/data/DzDataGrid.types.ts'
    18	import { computed, ref, toRef, toValue } from 'vue'
    19	import { useDataGridPagination } from './useDataGridPagination.ts'
    20	import { useDataGridSort } from './useDataGridSort.ts'
    21	
    22	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    23	// Options
    24	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    25	
    26	/** Options for the useDataGrid composable */
    27	export interface UseDataGridOptions<T> {
    28	  /** Data rows */
    29	  data: MaybeRef<T[]>
    30	  /** Column definitions */
    31	  columns: MaybeRef<ColumnDef<T>[]>
    32	  /** Whether sorting is enabled */
    33	  sortable?: MaybeRef<boolean>
    34	  /** Initial sort model */
    35	  sortModel?: MaybeRef<SortModel[]>
    36	  /** Pagination configuration */
    37	  pagination?: MaybeRef<boolean | PaginationConfig>
    38	  /** Whether filtering is enabled */
    39	  filterable?: MaybeRef<boolean>
    40	  /** Initial filter state */
    41	  filters?: MaybeRef<DzDataGridFilter[]>
    42	  /** Selection mode */
    43	  selectable?: MaybeRef<boolean | 'single' | 'multiple'>
    44	  /** Initially selected rows */
    45	  selectedRows?: MaybeRef<T[]>
    46	  /** Key field for row identity */
    47	  rowKey?: keyof T & string
    48	  /** Callback when sort changes */
    49	  onSortChange?: (sortModel: SortModel[]) => void
    50	  /** Callback when filter changes */
    51	  onFilterChange?: (filters: DzDataGridFilter[]) => void
    52	  /** Callback when selection changes */
    53	  onSelectionChange?: (rows: T[]) => void
    54	  /** Callback when page changes */
    55	  onPageChange?: (page: number) => void
    56	  /** Callback when page size changes */
    57	  onPageSizeChange?: (pageSize: number) => void
    58	}
    59	
    60	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    61	// Return type
    62	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    63	
    64	/** Return value of the useDataGrid composable */
    65	export interface UseDataGridReturn<T> {
    66	  /** Sorted and paginated data for current view */
    67	  displayData: Ref<T[]>
    68	  /** Total number of rows (before pagination) */
    69	  totalRows: Ref<number>
    70	  /** Current sort model */
    71	  sortModel: Ref<SortModel[]>
    72	  /** Current filter state */
    73	  filters: Ref<DzDataGridFilter[]>
    74	  /** Current page (1-based) */
    75	  currentPage: Ref<number>
    76	  /** Current page size */
    77	  pageSize: Ref<number>
    78	  /** Total number of pages */
    79	  totalPages: Ref<number>
    80	  /** Currently selected rows */
    81	  selectedRows: Ref<T[]>
    82	  /** Whether all current rows are selected */
    83	  isAllSelected: Ref<boolean>
    84	  /** Whether some (but not all) current rows are selected */
    85	  isSomeSelected: Ref<boolean>
    86	  /** Sort by a column field */
    87	  sort: (field: string) => void
    88	  /** Set a filter on a column */
    89	  setFilter: (column: string, filter: DzDataGridFilter) => void
    90	  /** Clear a filter on a column */
    91	  clearFilter: (column: string) => void
    92	  /** Clear all filters */
    93	  clearAllFilters: () => void
    94	  /** Toggle selection on a row */
    95	  toggleRowSelection: (row: T) => void
    96	  /** Toggle all rows selection */
    97	  toggleAllSelection: () => void
    98	  /** Check if a specific row is selected */
    99	  isRowSelected: (row: T) => boolean
   100	  /** Go to a specific page */
   101	  goToPage: (page: number) => void
   102	  /** Change page size */
   103	  changePageSize: (size: number) => void
   104	  /** Get sort direction for a column */
   105	  getSortDirection: (field: string) => SortDirection | undefined
   106	}
   107	
   108	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109	// Composable
   110	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   111	
   112	/**
   113	 * Manages data grid state including sorting, pagination, and row selection.
   114	 *
   115	 * @typeParam T - Row data type
   116	 */
   117	export function useDataGrid<T>(options: UseDataGridOptions<T>): UseDataGridReturn<T> {
   118	  const data = toRef(() => toValue(options.data))
   119	  const columns = toRef(() => toValue(options.columns))
   120	  const sortable = toRef(() => toValue(options.sortable) ?? false)
   121	  const filterableMode = toRef(() => toValue(options.filterable) ?? false)
   122	  const selectableMode = toRef(() => toValue(options.selectable) ?? false)
   123	  const paginationConfig = toRef(() => toValue(options.pagination) ?? false)
   124	  const rowKey = options.rowKey
   125	
   126	  // ── Pagination (created first so resetPage is available) ──
   127	  const initialPageSize = typeof toValue(options.pagination) === 'object'
   128	    ? (toValue(options.pagination) as PaginationConfig).pageSize
   129	    : undefined
   130	
   131	  // Shared ref for resetPage callback (set after pagination is created)
   132	  const resetPageRef = ref<(() => void) | undefined>(undefined)
   133	
   134	  // ── Sort + Filter ──
   135	  const sortResult = useDataGridSort<T>(
   136	    {
   137	      data,
   138	      columns,
   139	      sortable,
   140	      filterable: filterableMode,
   141	      initialSortModel: toValue(options.sortModel) ?? undefined,
   142	      initialFilters: toValue(options.filters) ?? undefined,
   143	      onSortChange: options.onSortChange,
   144	      onFilterChange: options.onFilterChange,
   145	    },
   146	    resetPageRef,
   147	  )
   148	
   149	  // ── Pagination ──
   150	  const paginationResult = useDataGridPagination<T>({
   151	    sortedData: sortResult.sortedData,
   152	    paginationConfig,
   153	    initialPageSize,
   154	    onPageChange: options.onPageChange,
   155	    onPageSizeChange: options.onPageSizeChange,
   156	  })
   157	
   158	  // Wire resetPage into sort/filter operations
   159	  resetPageRef.value = paginationResult.resetPage
   160	
   161	  // ── Selection state ──
   162	  const selectedRows = ref<T[]>(toValue(options.selectedRows) ?? []) as Ref<T[]>
   163	
   164	  // ── Row identity ──
   165	  function getRowId(row: T): unknown {
   166	    if (rowKey)
   167	      return row[rowKey]
   168	    return row
   169	  }
   170	
   171	  function rowsEqual(a: T, b: T): boolean {
   172	    return getRowId(a) === getRowId(b)
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  // ── Selection computed ──
   176	  const isAllSelected = computed(() => {
   177	    if (!selectableMode.value || paginationResult.displayData.value.length === 0)
   178	      return false
   179	    return paginationResult.displayData.value.every(row =>
   180	      selectedRows.value.some(sel => rowsEqual(sel, row)),
   181	    )
   182	  })
   183	
   184	  const isSomeSelected = computed(() => {
   185	    if (!selectableMode.value || paginationResult.displayData.value.length === 0)
   186	      return false
   187	    const someSelected = paginationResult.displayData.value.some(row =>
   188	      selectedRows.value.some(sel => rowsEqual(sel, row)),
   189	    )
   190	    return someSelected && !isAllSelected.value
   191	  })
   192	
   193	  // ── Selection actions ──
   194	  function isRowSelected(row: T): boolean {
   195	    return selectedRows.value.some(sel => rowsEqual(sel, row))
   196	  }
   197	
   198	  function toggleRowSelection(row: T): void {
   199	    if (!selectableMode.value)
   200	      return
   201	
   202	    const mode = selectableMode.value
   203	    const alreadySelected = isRowSelected(row)
   204	
   205	    if (mode === 'single' || mode === true) {
   206	      selectedRows.value = alreadySelected ? [] : [row]
   207	    }
   208	    else if (mode === 'multiple') {
   209	      if (alreadySelected) {
   210	        selectedRows.value = selectedRows.value.filter(sel => !rowsEqual(sel, row))
   211	      }
   212	      else {
   213	        selectedRows.value = [...selectedRows.value, row]
   214	      }
   215	    }
   216	
   217	    options.onSelectionChange?.(selectedRows.value)
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  function toggleAllSelection(): void {
   221	    if (!selectableMode.value || selectableMode.value === 'single')
   222	      return
   223	
   224	    if (isAllSelected.value) {
   225	      selectedRows.value = []
   226	    }
   227	    else {
   228	      selectedRows.value = [...paginationResult.displayData.value]
   229	    }
   230	
   231	    options.onSelectionChange?.(selectedRows.value)
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  return {
   235	    displayData: paginationResult.displayData,
   236	    totalRows: paginationResult.totalRows,
   237	    sortModel: sortResult.sortModel,
   238	    filters: sortResult.filters,
   239	    currentPage: paginationResult.currentPage,
   240	    pageSize: paginationResult.pageSize,
   241	    totalPages: paginationResult.totalPages,
   242	    selectedRows,
   243	    isAllSelected,
   244	    isSomeSelected,
   245	    sort: sortResult.sort,
   246	    setFilter: sortResult.setFilter,
   247	    clearFilter: sortResult.clearFilter,
   248	    clearAllFilters: sortResult.clearAllFilters,
   249	    toggleRowSelection,
   250	    toggleAllSelection,
   251	    isRowSelected,
   252	    goToPage: paginationResult.goToPage,
   253	    changePageSize: paginationResult.changePageSize,
   254	    getSortDirection: sortResult.getSortDirection,
   255	  }
   256	}
```
