 succeeded in 0ms:
        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
      - name: Run E2E tests (Chromium)
        run: yarn test:e2e --project=chromium
      - name: Upload Playwright report
        if: failure()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: playwright-report
          path: playwright-report/
          retention-days: 7

  coverage:
    name: Test Coverage
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [test]
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: 20
      - name: Enable Corepack
        run: corepack enable
      - name: Get yarn cache directory
        id: yarn-cache-dir
        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: yarn install --immutable
      - name: Run coverage with threshold enforcement
        run: |
          yarn test:coverage -- --coverage.thresholds.lines=80 --coverage.thresholds.functions=80 --coverage.thresholds.branches=80 --coverage.thresholds.statements=80
      - name: Upload coverage
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: coverage
          path: coverage/
          retention-days: 7

 succeeded in 0ms:
# Security Policy

## Supported Versions

| Version | Supported |
|---------|-----------|
| 0.1.x (alpha) | Yes |

## Reporting a Vulnerability

**Do not open a public GitHub issue for security vulnerabilities.**

Please report security issues by emailing the maintainers directly (see the contact in the GitHub repository's security advisories tab), or by using [GitHub's private vulnerability reporting](https://docs.github.com/en/code-security/security-advisories/guidance-on-reporting-and-writing/privately-reporting-a-security-vulnerability).

Include in your report:
- A description of the vulnerability
- Steps to reproduce
- Potential impact
- Any suggested fix (optional)

You will receive a response within 5 business days. We aim to release a fix within 30 days of confirmation.

## Scope

This policy applies to the `@dzup-ui/core`, `@dzup-ui/tokens`, `@dzup-ui/contracts`, and `@dzup-ui/nuxt` packages published under the `dzup-ui` repository.

## Type of change

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New component / variant
- [ ] Refactor / internal improvement
- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] Tooling / CI

## Checklist

- [ ] Tests added / updated (`yarn test`)
- [ ] Types check (`yarn typecheck`)
- [ ] Lint passes (`yarn lint`)
- [ ] Contract spec updated if props/events changed
- [ ] Changeset added (`yarn changeset`) — unless this is a docs/CI-only change
- [ ] Storybook story added / updated for visual changes
- [ ] Accessibility: keyboard nav, ARIA roles, focus management verified

# 07 Operability and Release Readiness

## Operational Surface
This repository is primarily a publishable UI library monorepo, not a long-running backend service.

- Runtime/service model:
  - No API server, worker process, queue processor, scheduler, or migration runner is defined in repo-level scripts; operational execution is build/test/publish oriented ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:13), [README.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/README.md:3)).
  - Publishable units are package artifacts (`@dzup-ui/core`, `tokens`, `contracts`, `compat`, `codemods`, `nuxt`) rather than deployable services ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:24), [packages/core/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/package.json:10), [packages/nuxt/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/package.json:7)).
- Environment handling:
  - Env access is minimal and mostly CI/dev mode toggles (`process.env.CI`, `import.meta.env?.DEV`) ([playwright.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:6), [packages/compat/src/utils/deprecation.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/utils/deprecation.ts:25), [packages/core/src/providers/useTheme.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/providers/useTheme.ts:41)).
  - Release credentials are injected from GitHub Secrets (`NPM_TOKEN`, `NODE_AUTH_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN`) in release workflows ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:59), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:60)).
  - `.env*` patterns are ignored in git, with no committed env template in repo root ([.gitignore](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.gitignore:17)).
- Migrations and compatibility:
  - “Migration” is consumer-code migration, not schema migration: compat adapters + codemods (`--dry-run` supported) ([packages/compat/src/index.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/index.ts:6), [packages/codemods/bin/dzup-codemod.js](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/bin/dzup-codemod.js:16), [packages/codemods/src/runner.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/src/runner.ts:77)).
- Health endpoints:
  - No HTTP health/readiness endpoints exist in this repo’s own runtime model; closest “health” concept is Storybook smoke/e2e checks ([e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/e2e/smoke/storybook.spec.ts:6)).
- Deployment assumptions:
  - Primary deployment target is npm publication via Changesets workflows, not container/service deployment ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:51), [package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:50)).

## Observability Review
Current observability is strong for CI build health, weak for runtime telemetry (which is partly expected for a library).

- Strong operator feedback loops:
  - CI publishes artifacts for dist/storybook/coverage and failure diagnostics for Playwright ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:200), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:231), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:263), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:296)).
  - Playwright enables retry tracing (`trace: on-first-retry`) to improve failure triage ([playwright.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:12)).
  - Codemod execution emits summary stats and per-file errors for migration runs ([packages/codemods/src/utils/logger.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/src/utils/logger.ts:55)).
- Observability gaps:
  - No structured logging framework, metrics pipeline, tracing instrumentation, or alerting hooks are present in repo runtime/tooling code.
  - No explicit operational SLO/SLI definitions exist for release flow (publish latency, failure rate, rollback MTTR).
  - Coverage enforcement is conditioned to PR events, reducing continuous visibility on direct `main` pushes ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:275)).

## Release And Migration Risks
- Schema/data migration risk:
  - Not applicable in the classic DB sense; there is no schema migration subsystem.
- Compatibility and rollout controls:
  - Backward-compatibility strategy exists through compat wrappers and deprecation warnings ([packages/compat/src/utils/deprecation.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/src/utils/deprecation.ts:20)).
  - Automated source migration exists through codemods with dry-run mode ([packages/codemods/bin/dzup-codemod.js](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/bin/dzup-codemod.js:16)).
- Key release risks:
  - Release workflows are less strict than CI and can publish based on narrower checks (`build` + `yarn test`) ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:45), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:51)).
  - Nuxt auto-import registry is manually maintained and drift-prone versus generated core exports ([packages/nuxt/src/module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:22), [packages/core/src/index.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/index.ts:2)).
  - Changesets config ignores `@dzup-ui/compat` and `@dzup-ui/codemods` despite those packages being publish-configured as public, creating versioning/release ambiguity ([.changeset/config.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.changeset/config.json:12), [packages/compat/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/package.json:20), [packages/codemods/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/package.json:21)).
- Rollback concerns:
  - npm package publishes are effectively immutable for many rollback scenarios; repo has no documented “release rollback playbook” (deprecate/revert/version-forward sequence) in release workflow definitions.

## CI And Automation Review
- Strong automation in CI:
  - Broad PR/main checks: typecheck, lint, tests (Node 20/22), validation suite, build, storybook build, Playwright e2e, coverage ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:18), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:64), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:96), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:238)).
  - Packaging and artifact quality checks are mature: ESM-only, dist existence, d.ts checks, bundle budgets, pack smoke tests ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:160), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:177), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:179), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:185)).
- Missing/partial automation:
  - Release/publish workflows do not reuse full CI gate set before publish ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:45), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:51)).
  - E2E gate in CI only runs Chromium despite multi-browser project definitions ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:261), [playwright.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:14)).
  - Manual hardening scripts (`scan:credentials`, `rehearse:release`) are not wired into CI/release workflows ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:51)).
  - `validate:interaction-contract` exists but is not present in CI validate job ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:30), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:116)).

## Findings
1. **High: Publish workflows are weaker than merge-gate CI and can ship with unchecked regressions.**  
Evidence: release/prerelease workflows run only install/build/test before publish actions; they omit lint, full validate suite, SSR/a11y/contracts, pack checks, and e2e ([release.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/release.yml:45), [publish-prerelease.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/publish-prerelease.yml:51), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:96)).

2. **High: Nuxt auto-import registry drift risk is already material.**  
Evidence: core exports are generated from manifest, but Nuxt list is manually curated; local diff of current tree shows significant mismatch (44 core exports missing from Nuxt auto-import list, 18 names present in Nuxt list but not in core export set) ([packages/core/src/index.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/core/src/index.ts:2), [packages/nuxt/src/module.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/nuxt/src/module.ts:22), [packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts:80)).

3. **Medium-High: Changeset ignore policy conflicts with publishable migration packages.**  
Evidence: `@dzup-ui/compat` and `@dzup-ui/codemods` are in `ignore`, but both are configured for public publish, creating unclear release/version governance for migration tooling ([.changeset/config.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.changeset/config.json:12), [packages/compat/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/compat/package.json:20), [packages/codemods/package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/package.json:21)).

4. **Medium: Critical preflight scripts are optional/manual, not enforced.**  
Evidence: credential scan and release rehearsal are exposed as scripts but not called by CI or publish workflows ([package.json](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/package.json:51), [scripts/credential-scan.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/credential-scan.sh:54), [scripts/release-rehearsal.sh](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/release-rehearsal.sh:49)).

5. **Medium: Observability is CI-centric; runtime/operator telemetry is sparse.**  
Evidence: useful CI artifacts and traces exist, but no structured logs/metrics/tracing framework appears in repo runtime/tooling paths ([ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:263), [playwright.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:12), [packages/codemods/src/utils/logger.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/packages/codemods/src/utils/logger.ts:55)).

6. **Low-Medium: Cross-browser smoke confidence is lower than config suggests.**  
Evidence: Playwright config defines Chromium/Firefox/WebKit projects, but CI executes only Chromium e2e ([playwright.config.ts](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/playwright.config.ts:14), [ci.yml](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/.github/workflows/ci.yml:261)).

7. **Low: Secondary `out/` telemetry is stale/inconsistent for operations planning.**  
Evidence: generated summaries report contradictory/underfit inventory signals (for example, “40 components” and “Features: 0”), which are unsuitable as authoritative release-readiness telemetry ([out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-current/extracted/ui-dzup-ui.md:9), [out/code-features-live-sample-3/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md](/media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/out/code-features-live-sample-3/summaries/repos/ui-dzup-ui.md:5)).

## Recommended Hardening Plan
1. **Unify publish gating with CI gates.**  
Create a single `verify:release` script that runs typecheck, lint, tests (`unit/contracts/a11y/ssr`), `validate:*`, build, pack smoke, and e2e smoke; require it in `release.yml` and `publish-prerelease.yml` before any publish step.

2. **Eliminate Nuxt registry drift by generation.**  
Generate `CORE_COMPONENTS` from the same core manifest/export source used for `packages/core/src/index.ts`, and add a CI assertion that Nuxt registry equals generated export set.

3. **Resolve Changesets governance mismatch.**  
Either remove `compat`/`codemods` from `ignore` or explicitly mark them non-publishable; document intended policy and enforce it with CI validation.

4. **Automate preflight security/release checks.**  
Run `scan:credentials` and `rehearse:release` (or equivalent machine-friendly checks) in CI for release branches/tags; fail fast on hits.

5. **Promote cross-browser release smoke.**  
Run Playwright smoke on at least Chromium + one secondary engine (Firefox or WebKit) in merge gate and release gate.

6. **Add rollback runbook for npm release incidents.**  
Document exact operator playbook: identify bad package/version, deprecate npm version, patch forward, publish corrected version, and communicate migration guidance.

7. **Strengthen operator telemetry for release pipeline.**  
Persist and trend release-health metrics (publish duration, failed publish attempts, flaky job counts, e2e failure categories) and add alerting thresholds.

8. **Treat `out/` artifacts as advisory only unless refreshed in CI.**  
If these artifacts are used in release decisions, regenerate them in a gated job and stamp commit SHA + generation timestamp.

## Overall Assessment
`dzup-ui` is **moderately strong for release readiness as a library monorepo**, with mature CI quality gates and packaging checks. It is **not yet fully hardened for scale-grade operations** because publish workflows are looser than CI, Nuxt export registration is drift-prone, and release/rollback governance is under-specified. With unified publish gating and generated Nuxt registry controls, it would move to a clearly safer operating posture.
codex
I reviewed the existing report file and it’s largely complete, but it lacks the `Repository Overview` section that failed the prior quality gate. I’m now validating a few high-risk claims (Nuxt export drift, publish policy mismatch, and release gating differences) so the rewritten report is both complete and evidence-accurate.

   242

   245

   248	    if (options.includePro) {

   250	    }
   251

   254	      addComponent({
   255	        name: options.prefix ? `${options.prefix}${name.slice(2)}` : name,
   256	        export: name,
   257	        filePath: '@dzup-ui/core',
   258	      })
   259	    }

     1	/**
     2	 * Export Validation Script (ADR-01)
     3	 *
     4	 * For manifest-driven packages (core): reads public-api.manifest.json and validates:
     5	 *   1. Every referenced source file exists on disk
     6	 *   2. Every declared export symbol is actually exported from its source file
     7	 *   3. No orphaned manifest entries (file exists but exports nothing declared)
     8	 *
     9	 * For flat-export packages (compat, nuxt): validates that:
    10	 *   1. The package entry file exists
    11	 *   2. The file exports at least one symbol (non-empty barrel)
    12	 *   3. The package.json `exports` map points to resolvable paths
    13	 *
    14	 * Usage:
    15	 *   tsx packages/tooling/scripts/validate-exports.ts
    16	 *
    17	 * Exit code 1 if any errors found.
    18	 */
    19	
    20	import { existsSync, readFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    21	import { dirname, relative, resolve } from 'node:path'
    22	import process from 'node:process'
    23	import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url'
    24	
    25	// --- Types matching manifest JSON shape (from manifest-generator.ts) ---
    26	
    27	interface ManifestEntry {
    28	  path: string
    29	  exports: string[]
    30	}
    31	
    32	interface ManifestExports {
    33	  components: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    34	  composables: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    35	  utilities: Record<string, ManifestEntry>
    36	  injectionKeys?: string[]
    37	  variants?: string[]
    38	  runtimeExports?: string[]
    39	  types?: string[]
    40	}
    41	
    42	interface Manifest {
    43	  $schema?: string
    44	  package: string
    45	  version: string
    46	  description?: string
    47	  exports: ManifestExports
    48	}
    49	
    50	interface ValidationError {
    51	  package: string
    52	  category: string
    53	  entry: string
    54	  filePath: string
    55	  message: string
    56	}
    57	
    58	interface PackageJson {
    59	  exports?: Record<string, unknown>
    60	  main?: string
    61	  module?: string
    62	}
    63	
    64	// --- Constants ---
    65	
    66	const ROOT = resolve(dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '../../../')
    67	
    68	const MANIFEST_PATHS: Array<{ name: string, path: string, packageDir: string }> = [
    69	  {
    70	    name: '@dzup-ui/core',
    71	    path: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core/manifests/public-api.manifest.json'),
    72	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/core'),
    73	  },
    74	]
    75	
    76	/**
    77	 * Flat-export packages: no manifest — validate the entry file and export map.
    78	 * entryFile is relative to packageDir (src/ path, the source of truth pre-build).
    79	 */
    80	const FLAT_EXPORT_PACKAGES: Array<{ name: string, packageDir: string, entryFile: string }> = [
    81	  {
    82	    name: '@dzup-ui/compat',
    83	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/compat'),
    84	    entryFile: 'src/index.ts',
    85	  },
    86	  {
    87	    name: '@dzup-ui/nuxt',
    88	    packageDir: resolve(ROOT, 'packages/nuxt'),
    89	    entryFile: 'src/module.ts',
    90	  },
    91	]
    92	
    93	// --- Helpers ---
    94	
    95	/**
    96	 * Resolves a manifest-relative path (e.g. "./src/components/buttons/index.ts")
    97	 * to an absolute path from the package directory.
    98	 */
    99	function resolveManifestPath(packageDir: string, manifestPath: string): string {
   100	  return resolve(packageDir, manifestPath)
   101	}
   102	
   103	/**
   104	 * Reads a file and returns the set of exported symbol names.
   105	 * For `export *` re-exports, returns a special marker.
   106	 */
   107	function getExportedSymbols(filePath: string): { symbols: Set<string>, hasStarExport: boolean } {
   108	  const content = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   109	  const symbols = new Set<string>()
   110	  let hasStarExport = false
   111	
   112	  // Check for `export *` re-exports
   113	  if (/export\s*\*\s*from/.test(content)) {
   114	    hasStarExport = true
   115	  }
   116	
   117	  // Named exports: export const/let/var/function/class/interface/type/enum
   118	  const namedRe = /export\s+(?:default\s+)?(?:const|let|var|function|class|interface|type|enum|abstract\s+class)\s+(\w+)/g
   119	  let match: RegExpExecArray | null = namedRe.exec(content)
   120	  while (match !== null) {
   121	    if (match[1] !== undefined) {
   122	      symbols.add(match[1])
   123	    }
   124	    match = namedRe.exec(content)
   125	  }
   126	
   127	  // Destructured exports: export { Foo, Bar, Baz as Qux }
   128	  const bracketRe = /export\s*\{([^}]+)\}/g
   129	  match = bracketRe.exec(content)
   130	  while (match !== null) {
   131	    if (match[1] !== undefined) {
   132	      const names = match[1].split(',').map(s => s.trim())
   133	      for (const name of names) {
   134	        // Handle "Foo as Bar" — the exported name is "Bar"
   135	        const asMatch = /\w+\s+as\s+(\w+)/.exec(name)
   136	        if (asMatch !== null && asMatch[1] !== undefined) {
   137	          symbols.add(asMatch[1])
   138	        }
   139	        else {
   140	          // Simple export, handle "type Foo" prefix
   141	          const cleanName = name.replace(/^type\s+/, '').trim()
   142	          if (cleanName.length > 0) {
   143	            symbols.add(cleanName)
   144	          }
   145	        }
   146	      }
   147	    }
   148	    match = bracketRe.exec(content)
   149	  }
   150	
   151	  return { symbols, hasStarExport }
   152	}
   153	
   154	/**
   155	 * Validates all entries in a single category (components, composables, utilities).
   156	 */
   157	function validateCategory(
   158	  packageName: string,
   159	  packageDir: string,
   160	  categoryName: string,
   161	  entries: Record<string, ManifestEntry>,
   162	): ValidationError[] {
   163	  const errors: ValidationError[] = []
   164	
   165	  for (const [entryName, entry] of Object.entries(entries)) {
   166	    const filePath = resolveManifestPath(packageDir, entry.path)
   167	    const relPath = relative(ROOT, filePath)
   168	
   169	    // Check 1: Source file exists
   170	    if (!existsSync(filePath)) {
   171	      errors.push({
   172	        package: packageName,
   173	        category: categoryName,
   174	        entry: entryName,
   175	        filePath: relPath,
   176	        message: `Source file does not exist: ${relPath}`,
   177	      })
   178	      continue // Cannot check exports if file is missing
   179	    }
   180	
   181	    // Check 2: Declared exports exist in the source file
   182	    const { symbols, hasStarExport } = getExportedSymbols(filePath)
   183	
   184	    for (const exportName of entry.exports) {
   185	      if (!symbols.has(exportName) && !hasStarExport) {
   186	        errors.push({
   187	          package: packageName,
   188	          category: categoryName,
   189	          entry: entryName,
   190	          filePath: relPath,
   191	          message: `Export "${exportName}" declared in manifest but not found in ${relPath}`,
   192	        })
   193	      }
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    // Check 3: Orphan detection — file has no matching exports and no star re-export
   197	    if (!hasStarExport && entry.exports.length > 0) {
   198	      const foundCount = entry.exports.filter(e => symbols.has(e)).length
   199	      if (foundCount === 0 && symbols.size === 0) {
   200	        errors.push({
   201	          package: packageName,
   202	          category: categoryName,
   203	          entry: entryName,
   204	          filePath: relPath,
   205	          message: `File ${relPath} exports nothing — all ${entry.exports.length} manifest symbols are orphaned`,
   206	        })
   207	      }
   208	    }
   209	  }
   210	
   211	  return errors
   212	}
   213	
   214	// --- Flat-export validation (compat, nuxt) ---
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * Validates a flat-export package (no manifest):
   218	 *  1. Entry source file exists
   219	 *  2. Entry file exports at least one symbol

   221	 *     is skipped pre-build — we validate the source barrel only)

   235	      entry: pkg.entryFile,

   237	      message: `Entry file does not exist: ${relEntry}`,

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Logger and stats utilities for codemod output.
     3	 *
     4	 * Keeps a running tally of processed / modified / skipped / errored files
     5	 * and prints a human-readable summary at the end of a run.
     6	 */
     7	
     8	import process from 'node:process'
     9	
    10	/** Aggregated statistics for a codemod run. */
    11	export interface TransformStats {
    12	  filesProcessed: number
    13	  filesModified: number
    14	  filesSkipped: number
    15	  filesErrored: number
    16	  errors: ReadonlyArray<{ file: string, error: string }>
    17	}
    18	
    19	export class CodemodLogger {
    20	  private stats: {
    21	    filesProcessed: number
    22	    filesModified: number
    23	    filesSkipped: number
    24	    filesErrored: number
    25	    errors: Array<{ file: string, error: string }>
    26	  } = {
    27	    filesProcessed: 0,
    28	    filesModified: 0,
    29	    filesSkipped: 0,
    30	    filesErrored: 0,
    31	    errors: [],
    32	  }
    33	
    34	  incrementProcessed(): void {
    35	    this.stats.filesProcessed++
    36	  }
    37	
    38	  incrementModified(): void {
    39	    this.stats.filesModified++
    40	  }
    41	
    42	  incrementSkipped(): void {
    43	    this.stats.filesSkipped++
    44	  }
    45	
    46	  logError(file: string, error: string): void {
    47	    this.stats.errors.push({ file, error })
    48	    this.stats.filesErrored++
    49	  }
    50	
    51	  getStats(): TransformStats {
    52	    return { ...this.stats, errors: [...this.stats.errors] }
    53	  }
    54	
    55	  printSummary(): void {
    56	    const { filesProcessed, filesModified, filesSkipped, filesErrored, errors } = this.stats
    57	
    58	    process.stdout.write('\n=== Codemod Summary ===\n')
    59	    process.stdout.write(`Files processed: ${String(filesProcessed)}\n`)
    60	    process.stdout.write(`Files modified:  ${String(filesModified)}\n`)
    61	    process.stdout.write(`Files skipped:   ${String(filesSkipped)}\n`)
    62	
    63	    if (filesErrored > 0) {
    64	      process.stdout.write(`Files errored:   ${String(filesErrored)}\n`)
    65	    }
    66	
    67	    if (errors.length > 0) {
    68	      process.stdout.write('\nErrors:\n')
    69	      for (const { file, error } of errors) {
    70	        process.stdout.write(`  ${file}: ${error}\n`)
    71	      }
    72	    }
    73	
    74	    process.stdout.write('\n')
    75	  }
    76	}

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	import { expect, test } from '@playwright/test'
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * Storybook infrastructure smoke tests.
     5	 *
     6	 * These tests verify that the Storybook dev server is healthy,
     7	 * the UI shell renders correctly, and at least one story from each
     8	 * component family can be loaded and rendered successfully.
     9	 *
    10	 * They are intentionally broad — they catch broken builds,
    11	 * missing chunk errors, and misconfigured story loaders without
    12	 * testing every component in detail.
    13	 */
    14	
    15	test.describe('Storybook root', () => {
    16	  test('root page loads without JavaScript errors', async ({ page }) => {
    17	    const jsErrors: string[] = []
    18	    page.on('pageerror', err => jsErrors.push(err.message))
    19	
    20	    await page.goto('/')
    21	
    22	    // Allow time for the app shell to bootstrap
    23	    await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')
    24	
    25	    // No uncaught JS exceptions during load
    26	    expect(jsErrors).toHaveLength(0)
    27	  })
    28	
    29	  test('navigation sidebar is visible', async ({ page }) => {
    30	    await page.goto('/')
    31	
    32	    // Storybook 7/8 renders a sidebar nav with role="navigation" or data attributes.
    33	    // The sidebar contains the component tree — we look for the nav landmark.
    34	    const sidebar = page.locator('[data-layout="sidebar"], nav[aria-label*="story"], .sidebar-container').first()
    35	
    36	    // Fallback: Storybook wraps sidebar in a nav or aside element
    37	    const storybookSidebar = page.locator('#storybook-sidebar, .css-sidebar, [class*="sidebar"]').first()
    38	
    39	    // At minimum, the page should contain at least one navigation structure
    40	    await expect(page.locator('nav').first()).toBeVisible({ timeout: 15_000 })
    41	  })
    42	
    43	  test('sidebar contains component family entries', async ({ page }) => {
    44	    await page.goto('/')
    45	
    46	    await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle')
    47	
    48	    // These labels correspond to the story hierarchy root groups
    49	    // Storybook renders them as expandable tree items in the sidebar
    50	    const sidebar = page.locator('body')
    51	    await expect(sidebar).toContainText('Buttons', { timeout: 15_000 })
    52	    await expect(sidebar).toContainText('Inputs')
    53	    await expect(sidebar).toContainText('Overlays')
    54	    await expect(sidebar).toContainText('Navigation')
    55	  })
    56	})
    57	
    58	test.describe('Story rendering', () => {
    59	  /**
    60	   * Helper that navigates to a story URL and waits for the iframe canvas
    61	   * to finish rendering. Returns the frame locator.
    62	   */
    63	  async function loadStory(page: import('@playwright/test').Page, storyId: string) {
    64	    await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${storyId}`)
    65	    const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
    66	    // Wait for the iframe body to exist — proof the story rendered
    67	    await frame.locator('body').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 20_000 })
    68	    return frame
    69	  }
    70	
    71	  // ---- Buttons family ----
    72	
    73	  test('DzButton default story renders a visible button', async ({ page }) => {
    74	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-buttons-dzbutton--default')
    75	    await expect(frame.getByRole('button', { name: 'Button' })).toBeVisible()
    76	  })
    77	
    78	  test('DzIconButton default story renders without errors', async ({ page }) => {
    79	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-buttons-dziconbutton--default')
    80	    await expect(frame.locator('button').first()).toBeVisible()
    81	  })
    82	
    83	  // ---- Inputs family ----
    84	
    85	  test('DzInput default story renders a visible text input', async ({ page }) => {
    86	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-inputs-dzinput--default')
    87	    await expect(frame.getByRole('textbox')).toBeVisible()
    88	  })
    89	
    90	  test('DzPasswordInput default story renders a visible password input', async ({ page }) => {
    91	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-inputs-dzpasswordinput--default')
    92	    await expect(frame.locator('input[type="password"]')).toBeVisible()
    93	  })
    94	
    95	  test('DzTextarea default story renders a visible textarea', async ({ page }) => {
    96	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-inputs-dztextarea--default')
    97	    await expect(frame.getByRole('textbox')).toBeVisible()
    98	  })
    99	
   100	  // ---- Overlays family ----
   101	
   102	  test('DzDialog default story renders trigger button', async ({ page }) => {
   103	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-overlays-dzdialog--default')
   104	    await expect(frame.getByRole('button', { name: /open dialog/i })).toBeVisible()
   105	  })
   106	
   107	  test('DzTooltip default story renders trigger element', async ({ page }) => {
   108	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-overlays-dztooltip--default')
   109	    await expect(frame.locator('body')).not.toBeEmpty()
   110	  })
   111	
   112	  // ---- Navigation family ----
   113	
   114	  test('DzTabs default story renders tab list', async ({ page }) => {
   115	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-navigation-dztabs--default')
   116	    await expect(frame.getByRole('tablist')).toBeVisible()
   117	  })
   118	
   119	  test('DzBreadcrumb default story renders navigation', async ({ page }) => {
   120	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-navigation-dzbreadcrumb--default')
   121	    await expect(frame.getByRole('navigation')).toBeVisible()
   122	  })
   123	
   124	  // ---- Feedback family ----
   125	
   126	  test('DzAlert default story renders alert element', async ({ page }) => {
   127	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-feedback-dzalert--default')
   128	    await expect(frame.locator('body')).not.toBeEmpty()
   129	  })
   130	
   131	  test('DzToast tone gallery renders toast items', async ({ page }) => {
   132	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-feedback-dztoast--all-tones')
   133	    await expect(frame.getByText('Success')).toBeVisible()
   134	  })
   135	
   136	  // ---- Forms family ----
   137	
   138	  test('DzCheckbox default story renders a checkbox', async ({ page }) => {
   139	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-forms-dzcheckbox--default')
   140	    await expect(frame.getByRole('checkbox')).toBeVisible()
   141	  })
   142	
   143	  test('DzSelect default story renders a listbox trigger', async ({ page }) => {
   144	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-forms-dzselect--default')
   145	    await expect(frame.locator('body')).not.toBeEmpty()
   146	  })
   147	
   148	  // ---- Cards family ----
   149	
   150	  test('DzCard default story renders a card element', async ({ page }) => {
   151	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-cards-dzcard--default')
   152	    await expect(frame.locator('body')).not.toBeEmpty()
   153	  })
   154	
   155	  // ---- Typography family ----
   156	
   157	  test('DzHeading default story renders a heading', async ({ page }) => {
   158	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-typography-dzheading--default')
   159	    await expect(frame.getByRole('heading').first()).toBeVisible()
   160	  })
   161	
   162	  test('DzText default story renders text content', async ({ page }) => {
   163	    const frame = await loadStory(page, 'core-typography-dztext--default')
   164	    await expect(frame.locator('body')).not.toBeEmpty()
   165	  })
   166	})
   167	
   168	test.describe('No console errors on key stories', () => {
   169	  /**
   170	   * Verifies that no console.error calls are emitted when rendering
   171	   * the most-used stories. Vue's runtime emits errors there for
   172	   * prop type violations and failed watchers.
   173	   */
   174	
   175	  const criticalStories = [
   176	    'core-buttons-dzbutton--default',
   177	    'core-inputs-dzinput--default',
   178	    'core-inputs-dzpasswordinput--default',
   179	    'core-overlays-dzdialog--default',
   180	    'core-navigation-dztabs--default',
   181	    'core-feedback-dztoast--all-tones',
   182	    'core-forms-dzcheckbox--default',
   183	  ]
   184	
   185	  for (const storyId of criticalStories) {
   186	    test(`no console errors on ${storyId}`, async ({ page }) => {
   187	      const consoleErrors: string[] = []
   188	
   189	      page.on('console', msg => {
   190	        if (msg.type() === 'error') {
   191	          consoleErrors.push(msg.text())
   192	        }
   193	      })
   194	
   195	      await page.goto(`/?path=/story/${storyId}`)
   196	      const frame = page.frameLocator('#storybook-preview-iframe')
   197	      await frame.locator('body').waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 20_000 })
   198	
   199	      // Filter out known non-critical browser warnings that are not Vue/component errors
   200	      const actionableErrors = consoleErrors.filter(
   201	        msg =>
   202	          !msg.includes('favicon') &&
   203	          !msg.includes('net::ERR') &&
   204	          !msg.includes('ResizeObserver') &&
   205	          // Storybook HMR websocket disconnects in CI are not component errors
   206	          !msg.includes('WebSocket') &&
   207	          !msg.includes('[HMR]'),
   208	      )
   209	
   210	      expect(actionableErrors).toHaveLength(0)
   211	    })
   212	  }
   213	})

     1	/**
     2	 * @dzup-ui/compat
     3	 * Migration adapters from old dzup-ui API to vNext.
     4	 *
     5	 * IMPORTANT: Stable core/pro must NOT import from compat (ADR-06).
     6	 * This package provides backward-compatible wrappers for migration.
     7	 *
     8	 * @example
     9	 * ``\`ts
    10	 * // Before (old dzup-ui)
    11	 * import { DzButton } from 'dzup-ui'
    12	 * // <DzButton type="primary" size="small">Click</DzButton>
    13	 *
    14	 * // Migration step (use compat adapters)
    15	 * import { DzButtonCompat as DzButton } from '@dzup-ui/compat'
    16	 * // <DzButton type="primary" size="small">Click</DzButton>  -- still works
    17	 *
    18	 * // Final (migrate to vNext API)
    19	 * import { DzButton } from '@dzup-ui/core'
    20	 * // <DzButton variant="solid" tone="primary" size="sm">Click</DzButton>
    21	 * ``\`
    22	 */
    23	
    24	// ── Compat Adapters ──
    25	export { default as DzAccordionCompat } from './adapters/DzAccordionCompat.vue'
    26	export { default as DzAlertCompat } from './adapters/DzAlertCompat.vue'
    27	export { default as DzButtonCompat } from './adapters/DzButtonCompat.vue'
    28	export { default as DzCheckboxCompat } from './adapters/DzCheckboxCompat.vue'
    29	export { default as DzDialogCompat } from './adapters/DzDialogCompat.vue'
    30	export { default as DzInputCompat } from './adapters/DzInputCompat.vue'
    31	export { default as DzRadioCompat } from './adapters/DzRadioCompat.vue'
    32	export { default as DzSelectCompat } from './adapters/DzSelectCompat.vue'
    33	export { default as DzSwitchCompat } from './adapters/DzSwitchCompat.vue'
    34	export { default as DzTabsCompat } from './adapters/DzTabsCompat.vue'
    35	export { default as DzTooltipCompat } from './adapters/DzTooltipCompat.vue'
    36	
    37	// ── Utilities ──
    38	export { resetDeprecationWarnings, warnDeprecated } from './utils/deprecation.ts'

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env node
     2	
     3	/**
     4	 * dzup-codemod CLI entry point.
     5	 *
     6	 * Usage:
     7	 *   dzup-codemod <transform> <target-directory> [options]
     8	 *
     9	 * Transforms:
    10	 *   rename-imports   Rewrite old import paths to @dzup-ui/core and @dzup-ui/pro
    11	 *   rename-props     Rename deprecated props to vNext equivalents
    12	 *   rename-events    Rename deprecated events to vNext equivalents
    13	 *   all              Run all transforms in sequence
    14	 *
    15	 * Options:
    16	 *   --dry-run        Preview changes without writing files
    17	 *   --verbose        Print per-file status
    18	 *   --extensions     Comma-separated file extensions (default: js,jsx,ts,tsx,vue)
    19	 *   --parser         jscodeshift parser: babel | ts | tsx (default: tsx)
    20	 *   --help           Show this help message
    21	 *
    22	 * Examples:
    23	 *   dzup-codemod rename-imports ./src
    24	 *   dzup-codemod all ./src --dry-run --verbose
    25	 */
    26	
    27	import { existsSync } from 'node:fs'
    28	import { resolve } from 'node:path'
    29	import process from 'node:process'
    30	
    31	const TRANSFORMS = ['rename-imports', 'rename-props', 'rename-events', 'all']
    32	
    33	function printHelp() {
    34	  process.stdout.write(`
    35	dzup-codemod - Migrate from old dzup-ui to vNext
    36	
    37	Usage:
    38	  dzup-codemod <transform> <target-directory> [options]
    39	
    40	Transforms:
    41	  rename-imports   Rewrite old import paths to @dzup-ui/core and @dzup-ui/pro
    42	  rename-props     Rename deprecated props to vNext equivalents
    43	  rename-events    Rename deprecated events to vNext equivalents
    44	  all              Run all transforms in sequence
    45	
    46	Options:
    47	  --dry-run        Preview changes without writing files
    48	  --verbose        Print per-file status
    49	  --extensions     Comma-separated file extensions (default: js,jsx,ts,tsx,vue)
    50	  --parser         jscodeshift parser: babel | ts | tsx (default: tsx)
    51	  --help           Show this help message
    52	
    53	Examples:
    54	  dzup-codemod rename-imports ./src
    55	  dzup-codemod all ./src --dry-run --verbose
    56	`)
    57	}
    58	
    59	function parseArgs(argv) {
    60	  const args = argv.slice(2)
    61	  const opts = {
    62	    transform: undefined,
    63	    target: undefined,
    64	    dryRun: false,
    65	    verbose: false,
    66	    extensions: undefined,
    67	    parser: undefined,
    68	    help: false,
    69	  }
    70	
    71	  const positional = []
    72	
    73	  for (let i = 0; i < args.length; i++) {
    74	    const arg = args[i]
    75	
    76	    if (arg === '--help' || arg === '-h') {
    77	      opts.help = true
    78	    }
    79	    else if (arg === '--dry-run') {
    80	      opts.dryRun = true
    81	    }
    82	    else if (arg === '--verbose' || arg === '-v') {
    83	      opts.verbose = true
    84	    }
    85	    else if (arg === '--extensions' && i + 1 < args.length) {
    86	      opts.extensions = args[++i].split(',')
    87	    }
    88	    else if (arg === '--parser' && i + 1 < args.length) {
    89	      opts.parser = args[++i]
    90	    }
    91	    else if (!arg.startsWith('--')) {
    92	      positional.push(arg)
    93	    }
    94	  }
    95	
    96	  opts.transform = positional[0]
    97	  opts.target = positional[1]
    98	
    99	  return opts
   100	}
   101	
   102	async function loadTransform(name) {
   103	  const modulePath = new URL(`../dist/transforms/${name}.js`, import.meta.url)
   104	  const mod = await import(modulePath.href)
   105	  return mod.default ?? mod.transformer
   106	}
   107	
   108	async function main() {
   109	  const opts = parseArgs(process.argv)
   110	
   111	  if (opts.help || !opts.transform || !opts.target) {
   112	    printHelp()
   113	    process.exit(opts.help ? 0 : 1)
   114	  }
   115	
   116	  if (!TRANSFORMS.includes(opts.transform)) {
   117	    process.stderr.write(
   118	      `Unknown transform: ${opts.transform}\nAvailable: ${TRANSFORMS.join(', ')}\n`,
   119	    )
   120	    process.exit(1)
   121	  }
   122	
   123	  const targetPath = resolve(process.cwd(), opts.target)
   124	  if (!existsSync(targetPath)) {
   125	    process.stderr.write(`Target directory does not exist: ${targetPath}\n`)
   126	    process.exit(1)
   127	  }
   128	
   129	  // Dynamically import the runner
   130	  const { CodemodRunner } = await import('../dist/runner.js')
   131	
   132	  const runnerOpts = {
   133	    dryRun: opts.dryRun,
   134	    verbose: opts.verbose,
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  if (opts.extensions)
   138	    runnerOpts.extensions = opts.extensions
   139	  if (opts.parser)
   140	    runnerOpts.parser = opts.parser
   141	
   142	  const runner = new CodemodRunner(runnerOpts)
   143	
   144	  const transformNames
   145	    = opts.transform === 'all'
   146	      ? ['rename-imports', 'rename-props', 'rename-events']
   147	      : [opts.transform]
   148	
   149	  let allSuccess = true
   150	
   151	  for (const name of transformNames) {
   152	    process.stdout.write(`\nRunning transform: ${name}\n`)
   153	    process.stdout.write(`${'─'.repeat(40)}\n`)
   154	
   155	    const transform = await loadTransform(name)
   156	    const result = await runner.run(transform, targetPath)
   157	
   158	    if (!result.success) {
   159	      allSuccess = false
   160	    }
   161	  }
   162	
   163	  process.exit(allSuccess ? 0 : 1)
   164	}
   165	
   166	main().catch((err) => {
   167	  process.stderr.write(`Fatal error: ${err.message}\n`)
   168	  process.exit(1)
   169	})

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	/**
     2	 * Codemod runner.
     3	 *
     4	 * Discovers files, applies a jscodeshift-compatible transform, handles
     5	 * Vue SFC extraction, and reports results.
     6	 *
     7	 * @module
     8	 */
     9	
    10	import type { TransformStats } from './utils/logger.js'
    11	import { readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    12	import process from 'node:process'
    13	import { globSync } from 'glob'
    14	import { CodemodLogger } from './utils/logger.js'
    15	import { extractScriptFromVue, isVueFile, replaceScriptInVue } from './utils/vue-sfc.js'
    16	
    17	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    18	// Types
    19	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    20	
    21	/** A jscodeshift-compatible transform function. */
    22	export type TransformFn = (
    23	  file: { path: string, source: string },
    24	  api: { jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI, stats: () => void },
    25	  options: Record<string, unknown>,
    26	) => string | null
    27	
    28	/**
    29	 * Minimal jscodeshift API surface used by the runner.
    30	 * We import jscodeshift lazily so the module can be loaded without it
    31	 * installed (e.g. for type-checking only).
    32	 */
    33	interface JscodeshiftAPI {
    34	  (source: string): unknown
    35	  withParser: (parser: string) => JscodeshiftAPI
    36	  [key: string]: unknown
    37	}
    38	
    39	/** Options accepted by {@link CodemodRunner}. */
    40	export interface RunnerOptions {
    41	  /** When true, do not write files — only report what would change. */
    42	  dryRun?: boolean
    43	  /** File extensions to process (without leading dot). */
    44	  extensions?: string[]
    45	  /** Glob patterns to ignore. */
    46	  ignore?: string[]
    47	  /** jscodeshift parser to use. */
    48	  parser?: 'babel' | 'ts' | 'tsx'
    49	  /** Print per-file status messages. */
    50	  verbose?: boolean
    51	}
    52	
    53	interface ResolvedOptions {
    54	  dryRun: boolean
    55	  extensions: string[]
    56	  ignore: string[]
    57	  parser: 'babel' | 'ts' | 'tsx'
    58	  verbose: boolean
    59	}
    60	
    61	/** Result returned by {@link CodemodRunner.run}. */
    62	export interface RunResult {
    63	  success: boolean
    64	  stats: TransformStats
    65	}
    66	
    67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    68	// Runner
    69	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    70	
    71	export class CodemodRunner {
    72	  private readonly logger = new CodemodLogger()
    73	  private readonly options: ResolvedOptions
    74	
    75	  constructor(options: RunnerOptions = {}) {
    76	    this.options = {
    77	      dryRun: options.dryRun ?? false,
    78	      extensions: options.extensions ?? ['js', 'jsx', 'ts', 'tsx', 'vue'],
    79	      ignore: options.ignore ?? ['**/node_modules/**', '**/dist/**', '**/.git/**'],
    80	      parser: options.parser ?? 'tsx',
    81	      verbose: options.verbose ?? false,
    82	    }
    83	  }
    84	
    85	  /**
    86	   * Run a transform against all matching files under `targetPath`.
    87	   *
    88	   * @param transform - The transform function (default export of a codemod file).
    89	   * @param targetPath - Directory to scan for files.
    90	   * @returns A {@link RunResult} with success status and statistics.
    91	   */
    92	  async run(transform: TransformFn, targetPath: string): Promise<RunResult> {
    93	    const files = this.findFiles(targetPath)
    94	
    95	    if (this.options.verbose) {
    96	      process.stdout.write(`Found ${String(files.length)} file(s) in ${targetPath}\n`)
    97	    }
    98	
    99	    if (files.length === 0) {
   100	      process.stdout.write('No files to transform.\n')
   101	      return { success: true, stats: this.logger.getStats() }
   102	    }
   103	
   104	    // Lazy-import jscodeshift so the module can load without it.
   105	    const jscodeshift = await this.loadJscodeshift()
   106	
   107	    for (const file of files) {
   108	      this.processFile(file, transform, jscodeshift)
   109	    }
   110	
   111	    this.logger.printSummary()
   112	
   113	    return {
   114	      success: this.logger.getStats().filesErrored === 0,
   115	      stats: this.logger.getStats(),
   116	    }
   117	  }
   118	
   119	  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   120	  // Private helpers
   121	  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------
   122	
   123	  private findFiles(targetPath: string): string[] {
   124	    const exts = this.options.extensions.join(',')
   125	    const pattern = `${targetPath}/**/*.{${exts}}`
   126	    return globSync(pattern, {
   127	      ignore: this.options.ignore,
   128	      absolute: true,
   129	    })
   130	  }
   131	
   132	  private processFile(
   133	    filePath: string,
   134	    transform: TransformFn,
   135	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   136	  ): void {
   137	    this.logger.incrementProcessed()
   138	
   139	    try {
   140	      const source = readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')
   141	      let result: string | null = null
   142	
   143	      if (isVueFile(filePath)) {
   144	        result = this.transformVueFile(filePath, source, transform, jscodeshift)
   145	      }
   146	      else {
   147	        result = this.transformJsFile(filePath, source, transform, jscodeshift)
   148	      }
   149	
   150	      if (result !== null && result !== source) {
   151	        if (!this.options.dryRun) {
   152	          writeFileSync(filePath, result, 'utf-8')
   153	        }
   154	        this.logger.incrementModified()
   155	        if (this.options.verbose) {
   156	          process.stdout.write(`  modified: ${filePath}\n`)
   157	        }
   158	      }
   159	      else {
   160	        this.logger.incrementSkipped()
   161	        if (this.options.verbose) {
   162	          process.stdout.write(`  skipped:  ${filePath}\n`)
   163	        }
   164	      }
   165	    }
   166	    catch (error: unknown) {
   167	      const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)
   168	      this.logger.logError(filePath, message)
   169	      if (this.options.verbose) {
   170	        process.stderr.write(`  error:    ${filePath}: ${message}\n`)
   171	      }
   172	    }
   173	  }
   174	
   175	  private transformJsFile(
   176	    filePath: string,
   177	    source: string,
   178	    transform: TransformFn,
   179	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   180	  ): string | null {
   181	    const fileInfo = { path: filePath, source }
   182	    const api = {
   183	      jscodeshift: jscodeshift.withParser(this.options.parser),
   184	      stats: () => {},
   185	    }
   186	    return transform(fileInfo, api, { dryRun: this.options.dryRun })
   187	  }
   188	
   189	  private transformVueFile(
   190	    filePath: string,
   191	    source: string,
   192	    transform: TransformFn,
   193	    jscodeshift: JscodeshiftAPI,
   194	  ): string | null {
   195	    // Many transforms handle Vue files themselves (template + script).
   196	    // Try the transform on the whole file first — if the transform is
   197	    // Vue-aware it will handle extraction internally.
   198	    const wholeFileResult = transform(
   199	      { path: filePath, source },
   200	      { jscodeshift: jscodeshift.withParser(this.options.parser), stats: () => {} },
   201	      { dryRun: this.options.dryRun },
   202	    )
   203	
   204	    if (wholeFileResult !== null) {
   205	      return wholeFileResult
   206	    }
   207	
   208	    // Fallback: extract script and transform it in isolation.
   209	    const extraction = extractScriptFromVue(source)
   210	    if (!extraction)
   211	      return null
   212	
   213	    const scriptResult = this.transformJsFile(
   214	      filePath,
   215	      extraction.script,
   216	      transform,
   217	      jscodeshift,
   218	    )
   219	
   220	    if (scriptResult !== null && scriptResult !== extraction.script) {
   221	      return replaceScriptInVue(source, scriptResult, extraction)
   222	    }
   223	
   224	    return null
   225	  }
   226	
   227	  private async loadJscodeshift(): Promise<JscodeshiftAPI> {
   228	    try {
   229	      const mod = await import('jscodeshift')
   230	      return (mod.default ?? mod) as JscodeshiftAPI
   231	    }
   232	    catch {
   233	      throw new Error(
   234	        'jscodeshift is required but not installed. Run: yarn add jscodeshift',
   235	      )
   236	    }
   237	  }
   238	}

     1	name: CI

     3	on:
     4	  push:
     5	    branches: [main]
     6	  pull_request:
     7	    branches: [main]
     8	
     9	concurrency:
    10	  group: ci-${{ github.ref }}
    11	  cancel-in-progress: true
    12	
    13	jobs:
    14	  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	  # G0: PR Gates (blocking)
    16	  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    17	
    18	  typecheck:
    19	    name: Typecheck
    20	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    21	    steps:
    22	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    23	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    24	        with:
    25	          node-version: 20
    26	      - name: Enable Corepack
    27	        run: corepack enable
    28	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    29	        id: yarn-cache-dir

    33	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    34	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    35	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    36	      - name: Install dependencies
    37	        run: yarn install --immutable
    38	      - name: Typecheck (all packages)
    39	        run: yarn typecheck:all
    40	
    41	  lint:
    42	    name: Lint
    43	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    44	    steps:
    45	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    46	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    47	        with:
    48	          node-version: 20
    49	      - name: Enable Corepack
    50	        run: corepack enable
    51	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    52	        id: yarn-cache-dir
    53	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    54	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    55	        with:
    56	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    57	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    58	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    59	      - name: Install dependencies
    60	        run: yarn install --immutable
    61	      - name: ESLint
    62	        run: yarn lint
    63	
    64	  test:
    65	    name: Unit Tests (Node ${{ matrix.node-version }})
    66	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    67	    strategy:
    68	      matrix:
    69	        node-version: [20, 22]
    70	    steps:
    71	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    72	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
    73	        with:
    74	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
    75	      - name: Enable Corepack
    76	        run: corepack enable
    77	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    78	        id: yarn-cache-dir
    79	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    80	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    81	        with:
    82	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    83	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    84	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    85	      - name: Install dependencies
    86	        run: yarn install --immutable
    87	      - name: Run tests
    88	        run: yarn test
    89	      - name: Run contract tests
    90	        run: yarn test:contracts
    91	      - name: Run a11y tests
    92	        run: yarn test:a11y
    93	      - name: Run SSR smoke tests
    94	        run: yarn test:ssr
    95	
    96	  validate:
    97	    name: Validate (boundaries + tokens)
    98	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    99	    steps:
   100	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   101	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   102	        with:
   103	          node-version: 20
   104	      - name: Enable Corepack
   105	        run: corepack enable
   106	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
   107	        id: yarn-cache-dir
   108	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   109	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
   110	        with:
   111	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
   112	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
   113	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
   114	      - name: Install dependencies
   115	        run: yarn install --immutable
   116	      - name: Import boundary validation
   117	        run: yarn validate:boundaries
   118	      - name: Token compliance check
   119	        run: yarn validate:tokens
   120	      - name: Export map validation
   121	        run: yarn validate:exports
   122	      - name: Token build artifacts unchanged
   123	        run: |
   124	          yarn tokens:generate
   125	          git diff --exit-code packages/tokens/dist/
   126	      - name: Changelog validation
   127	        run: yarn validate:changelog
   128	      - name: Peer dependency validation
   129	        run: yarn validate:peers
   130	      - name: License audit
   131	        run: yarn validate:licenses
   132	
   133	  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   134	  # G1: Merge Gates (main branch)
   135	  # ──────────────────────────────────────────────
   136	
   137	  build:
   138	    name: Build (library mode)
   139	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   140	    needs: [typecheck, lint, test, validate]
   141	    steps:
   142	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   143	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   144	        with:
   145	          node-version: 20
   146	      - name: Enable Corepack
   147	        run: corepack enable
   148	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
   149	        id: yarn-cache-dir
   150	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   151	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
   152	        with:
   153	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
   154	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
   155	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
   156	      - name: Install dependencies
   157	        run: yarn install --immutable
   158	      - name: Build all packages
   159	        run: yarn build
   160	      - name: Verify ESM-only output (no CJS)
   161	        run: |
   162	          # ADR-11: ESM-only distribution
   163	          if find packages/core/dist -name "*.cjs" -o -name "*.cjs.js" | grep -q .; then
   164	            echo "ERROR: CJS output detected — ADR-11 violation"
   165	            exit 1
   166	          fi
   167	          echo "ESM-only output verified"
   168	      - name: Verify dist artifacts exist
   169	        run: |
   170	          test -f packages/contracts/dist/index.js || (echo "contracts/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
   171	          test -f packages/tokens/dist/index.js || (echo "tokens/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
   172	          test -f packages/core/dist/index.js || (echo "core/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
   173	          test -f packages/compat/dist/index.js || (echo "compat/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
   174	          test -f packages/codemods/dist/index.js || (echo "codemods/dist/index.js missing" && exit 1)
   175	          test -f packages/nuxt/dist/module.js || (echo "nuxt/dist/module.js missing" && exit 1)
   176	          echo "Build artifacts verified"
   177	      - name: Validate type declarations
   178	        run: yarn validate:dts
   179	      - name: Bundle budget check
   180	        run: yarn validate:bundle
   181	      - name: Bundle budget check (CI report)
   182	        run: npx tsx packages/tooling/src/bundle-budget-check.ts --ci
   183	      - name: Per-component size report
   184	        run: npx tsx packages/tooling/src/component-size-report.ts --top=30
   185	      - name: Pack smoke test (all publishable packages)
   186	        run: |
   187	          for pkg in contracts tokens core compat codemods nuxt; do
   188	            echo "--- packing packages/$pkg ---"
   189	            (cd packages/$pkg && npm pack --dry-run 2>&1) | tee /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt
   190	            if grep -q "link:" /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt; then
   191	              echo "ERROR: link: dependency in packages/$pkg tarball"
   192	              exit 1
   193	            fi
   194	            if grep -q "workspace:" /tmp/pack-$pkg.txt; then
   195	              echo "ERROR: workspace: dependency in packages/$pkg tarball"
   196	              exit 1
   197	            fi
   198	          done
   199	          echo "Pack smoke test passed for all packages"
   200	      - name: Upload build artifacts
   201	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
   202	        with:
   203	          name: dist
   204	          path: |
   205	            packages/core/dist/
   206	          retention-days: 7
   207	
   208	  storybook:
   209	    name: Storybook Build
   210	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   211	    needs: [typecheck]
   212	    steps:
   213	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   214	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   215	        with:
   216	          node-version: 20
   217	      - name: Enable Corepack
   218	        run: corepack enable
   219	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
   220	        id: yarn-cache-dir
   221	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   222	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
   223	        with:
   224	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
   225	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
   226	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
   227	      - name: Install dependencies
   228	        run: yarn install --immutable
   229	      - name: Build Storybook
   230	        run: yarn storybook:build
   231	      - name: Upload Storybook
   232	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
   233	        with:
   234	          name: storybook
   235	          path: apps/storybook/storybook-static/
   236	          retention-days: 7
   237	
   238	  e2e:
   239	    name: E2E Tests (Playwright)
   240	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   241	    needs: [typecheck]
   242	    steps:
   243	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   244	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   245	        with:
   246	          node-version: 20
   247	      - name: Enable Corepack
   248	        run: corepack enable
   249	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
   250	        id: yarn-cache-dir
   251	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   252	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
   253	        with:
   254	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
   255	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
   256	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
   257	      - name: Install dependencies
   258	        run: yarn install --immutable
   259	      - name: Install Playwright browsers
   260	        run: npx playwright install --with-deps chromium
   261	      - name: Run E2E tests (Chromium)
   262	        run: yarn test:e2e --project=chromium
   263	      - name: Upload Playwright report
   264	        if: failure()
   265	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
   266	        with:
   267	          name: playwright-report
   268	          path: playwright-report/
   269	          retention-days: 7
   270	
   271	  coverage:
   272	    name: Test Coverage
   273	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
   274	    needs: [test]
   275	    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
   276	    steps:
   277	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
   278	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
   279	        with:
   280	          node-version: 20
   281	      - name: Enable Corepack
   282	        run: corepack enable
   283	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
   284	        id: yarn-cache-dir
   285	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
   286	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
   287	        with:
   288	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
   289	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
   290	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
   291	      - name: Install dependencies
   292	        run: yarn install --immutable
   293	      - name: Run coverage with threshold enforcement
   294	        run: |
   295	          yarn test:coverage -- --coverage.thresholds.lines=80 --coverage.thresholds.functions=80 --coverage.thresholds.branches=80 --coverage.thresholds.statements=80
   296	      - name: Upload coverage
   297	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
   298	        with:
   299	          name: coverage
   300	          path: coverage/
   301	          retention-days: 7

     9	  cancel-in-progress: false # Don't cancel release in progress
    10	
    11	jobs:
    12	  release:
    13	    name: Release
    14	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15	    permissions:
    16	      contents: write # For creating tags and GitHub releases
    17	      pull-requests: write # For creating version PRs
    18	      id-token: write # For npm provenance
    19	    steps:
    20	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    21	        with:
    22	          fetch-depth: 0 # Full history for changeset version

    24	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4

    27	          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'

    29	      - name: Enable Corepack
    30	        run: corepack enable
    31	
    32	      - name: Get yarn cache directory

    34	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    35	
    36	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    37	        with:
    38	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    39	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    40	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    41	
    42	      - name: Install dependencies
    43	        run: yarn install --immutable
    44	
    45	      - name: Build all packages
    46	        run: yarn build
    47	
    48	      - name: Run tests
    49	        run: yarn test
    50

    54	        with:

    56	          publish: yarn release

    58	          commit: 'chore: version packages'
    59	        env:
    60	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    61	          NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    62	          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

    33	          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
    34	
    35	      - name: Enable Corepack
    36	        run: corepack enable
    37	
    38	      - name: Get yarn cache directory
    39	        id: yarn-cache-dir
    40	        run: echo "dir=$(yarn config get cacheFolder)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
    41	
    42	      - uses: actions/cache@v4
    43	        with:
    44	          path: ${{ steps.yarn-cache-dir.outputs.dir }}
    45	          key: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/yarn.lock') }}
    46	          restore-keys: yarn-${{ runner.os }}-
    47	
    48	      - name: Install dependencies
    49	        run: yarn install --immutable
    50	
    51	      - name: Build all packages
    52	        run: yarn build
    53	
    54	      - name: Run tests
    55	        run: yarn test
    56	
    57	      - name: Publish prerelease
    58	        run: |
    59	          yarn changeset publish --tag ${{ inputs.tag }}
    60	        env:
    61	          NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    62	          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}

/bin/bash -lc "nl -ba /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev/ui/dzup-ui/scripts/release-rehearsal.sh | sed -n '1,260p'" in /media/ninel/Second/code/datazup/ai-internal-dev
 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
     2	# release-rehearsal.sh — full pre-publish smoke test for dzup-ui
     3	# Simulates what CI does on a clean checkout, but runs locally.
     4	#
     5	# Usage:
     6	#   bash scripts/release-rehearsal.sh
     7	#   bash scripts/release-rehearsal.sh --skip-install   # reuse existing node_modules
     8	#
     9	# Requirements: node >=20, yarn (corepack), git
    10	
    11	set -euo pipefail
    12	
    13	REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
    14	cd "$REPO_ROOT"
    15	
    16	RED='\033[0;31m'
    17	GREEN='\033[0;32m'
    18	YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
    19	BOLD='\033[1m'
    20	NC='\033[0m'
    21	
    22	SKIP_INSTALL=false
    23	for arg in "$@"; do
    24	  [[ "$arg" == "--skip-install" ]] && SKIP_INSTALL=true
    25	done
    26	
    27	step() { echo -e "\n${BOLD}==> $*${NC}"; }
    28	ok()   { echo -e "${GREEN}✓ $*${NC}"; }
    29	fail() { echo -e "${RED}✗ FAILED: $*${NC}"; exit 1; }
    30	warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}⚠ $*${NC}"; }
    31	
    32	echo ""
    33	echo -e "${BOLD}dzup-ui release rehearsal${NC}"
    34	echo "Repo: $REPO_ROOT"
    35	echo "Date: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
    36	echo "Node: $(node --version)"
    37	echo "Yarn: $(yarn --version 2>/dev/null || echo 'not found')"
    38	echo ""
    39	
    40	# ─── 1. Clean install ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    41	if [[ "$SKIP_INSTALL" == "false" ]]; then
    42	  step "1. yarn install --immutable"
    43	  yarn install --immutable || fail "yarn install --immutable"
    44	  ok "Dependencies installed"
    45	else
    46	  warn "Skipping install (--skip-install)"
    47	fi
    48	
    49	# ─── 2. Typecheck ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50	step "2. yarn typecheck:all"
    51	yarn typecheck:all || fail "typecheck:all"
    52	ok "No type errors"
    53	
    54	# ─── 3. Lint ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    55	step "3. yarn lint"
    56	yarn lint || fail "lint"
    57	ok "No lint errors"
    58	
    59	# ─── 4. Tests ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60	step "4. yarn test (unit + contracts + a11y)"
    61	yarn test           || fail "yarn test"
    62	yarn test:contracts || fail "yarn test:contracts"
    63	yarn test:a11y      || fail "yarn test:a11y"
    64	ok "All tests pass"
    65	
    66	# ─── 5. Build ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    67	step "5. yarn build"
    68	yarn build || fail "yarn build"
    69	ok "Build succeeded"
    70	
    71	# ─── 6. Verify dist artifacts ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    72	step "6. Verify dist artifacts"
    73	MISSING=()
    74	declare -a EXPECTED=(
    75	  "packages/contracts/dist/index.js"
    76	  "packages/contracts/dist/index.d.ts"
    77	  "packages/tokens/dist/index.js"
    78	  "packages/tokens/dist/index.d.ts"
    79	  "packages/core/dist/index.js"
    80	  "packages/core/dist/index.d.ts"
    81	  "packages/compat/dist/index.js"
    82	  "packages/codemods/dist/index.js"
    83	  "packages/nuxt/dist/module.js"
    84	)
    85	for f in "${EXPECTED[@]}"; do
    86	  [[ -f "$f" ]] || MISSING+=("$f")
    87	done
    88	if [[ ${#MISSING[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
    89	  echo "Missing dist artifacts:"
    90	  printf '  %s\n' "${MISSING[@]}"
    91	  fail "dist artifacts incomplete"
    92	fi
    93	ok "All expected dist artifacts present"
    94	
    95	# ─── 7. ESM-only check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    96	step "7. ESM-only policy check (no CJS output)"
    97	if find packages/core/dist packages/tokens/dist packages/contracts/dist \
    98	     -name "*.cjs" -o -name "*.cjs.js" 2>/dev/null | grep -q .; then
    99	  fail "CJS output detected — ESM-only policy violation"
   100	fi
   101	ok "ESM-only output verified"
   102	
   103	# ─── 8. Pack smoke tests for all publishable packages ─────────────────────────
   104	step "8. npm pack --dry-run smoke tests"
   105	declare -a PUBLISHABLE=(
   106	  "packages/contracts"
   107	  "packages/tokens"
   108	  "packages/core"
   109	  "packages/compat"
   110	  "packages/codemods"
   111	  "packages/nuxt"
   112	)
   113	for pkg in "${PUBLISHABLE[@]}"; do
   114	  if [[ ! -f "$pkg/package.json" ]]; then
   115	    warn "Skipping $pkg (no package.json)"
   116	    continue
   117	  fi
   118	  pack_out=$(cd "$pkg" && npm pack --dry-run 2>&1)
   119	  if echo "$pack_out" | grep -q "link:"; then
   120	    echo "$pack_out"
   121	    fail "$pkg: link: dependency in tarball"
   122	  fi
   123	  if echo "$pack_out" | grep -q "workspace:"; then
   124	    echo "$pack_out"
   125	    fail "$pkg: workspace: dependency in tarball (not resolved — use real semver)"
   126	  fi
   127	  ok "$pkg: pack clean"
   128	done
   129	
   130	# ─── Summary ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   131	echo ""
   132	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}=========================================${NC}"
   133	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}  Release rehearsal PASSED${NC}"
   134	echo -e "${GREEN}${BOLD}=========================================${NC}"
   135	echo ""
   136	echo "All gates passed. The repo is ready to publish."
   137	echo "Next: coordinate H3 history purge, then run the actual changeset publish."
